advice_to_management: Humble yourselves. Remember when you were coming up in your career. Respect your associates and appreciate all that they do to try and keep up with your ever changing orders. Stop treating people like numbers.
review_pros: Salary was good for the position, but only because they never promoted anyone.
review_cons: Relentless pressure from the top. Constantly changing direction. Senior management is easily sold on every shiny new toy, rather than strengthening existing infrastructure.

advice_to_management: Bring this great Company at par with similar great companies in India, e.g., Infosys, TATA, etc., in terms of work culture and environment.
review_pros: Great brand name. Top Management are consistent on bank's goals and strategies. Constantly growing company.
review_cons: Work hours do not adhere to internationally accepted standards. Supervisory culture still embedded in 20th century, with juniors addressing Supervisors as 'Sir', and Supervisor wielding his authority with abandon.

advice_to_management: Good
review_pros: Good and best and bestest best
review_cons: Not bad and not bad and not bad

summary: Vodafone is an Egotistical Company that only cares about its net promoter score
advice_to_management: You have some amazing staff members who are totally disengaged. Your TNPS system is flawed. Your computer system Seibel, regardless of what you say, is not fit for purpose. People's jobs should not be on the line based on a survey score.
review_pros: Unfortunately there were no pros during my time working for this company.
review_cons: Retail stores are severely understaffed. The new system, Seibel, is not properly understood by staff. Bonuses are based on Net Promoter Score, specifically the first question: 'Would you recommend Vodafone to a friend or family?' Staff are penalized if a customer gives less than 9/10, potentially leading to job loss. Vodafone does not realize that people rank based on their whole experience, not just the last one. Most departments that can resolve customer issues are only available via email.

summary: Great company, poor processes, high expectations
advice_to_management: Handle customer escalations so BAMs can focus on sales. Be realistic about sales goals, especially given the recent environment. Fix broken processes, or figure out the chain of command so someone is accountable to get the problems fixed.
review_pros: Working with business owners or employees to solve telecommunication and technology. Great salary, if sales goals are met.
review_cons: Sales expectations are unrealistic. Due to Covid, the job shifted from outside sales to inside sales, becoming a glorified telemarketer role. Leadership micromanages and constantly changes directives. It's impossible to consistently perform without feeling overwhelmed and stressed, often working 50-60 hours per week. Operations and customer service teams are unhelpful, with broken and inconsistent processes, causing more problems for customers that Business Account Managers are expected to fix, even if they didn't create the issue.

review_pros: Very inclusive and helpful environment which made succeeding in the role very achievable.
Decent work-life balance. Opportunity for learning.
review_cons: Growth within the company is based on favoritism and proximity to the boss. No 13th month pay, performance bonus, or employee stock options. The CEO promised job retention in 2020, but staff were quietly let go, leading to disappointment and resignations among those who stayed.

summary: Regrettable Experience - A Workplace To Be Wary Of
review_pros: Eye-opening: Teaches you exactly how a company should not be run. Compensation is tolerable. Diverse clientele: Exposure to a broad range of clients if you can tolerate the cons.
review_cons: Communication breakdown: An endemic lack of communication exacerbates every problem. Soul-crushing work-life balance: Expectation to dedicate your life to the job; personal time and commitments are disregarded. Dead-end job: Promises of advancement are smoke and mirrors; no growth or meaningful recognition. Toxic culture: Cut-throat environment fosters hostility and stress; cooperation and collegiality are non-existent.

advice_to_management: Get it together.
review_pros: Most employees are great. Management will ask you to do things that are against policy, potentially leading to legal issues.
review_cons: HR and upper management do not support employees who report ethical violations. When employees exceed goals (e.g., 200%), management questions why they are above goal instead of recognizing excellent sales ability. Payouts are the lowest seen at a financial institution. Clawbacks occur if customers close or charge off accounts within 90 days, negating sales credit and requiring double work to catch up. They don't run Chex Systems, leading to frequent clawbacks. Technology is poor. Hold times for Bank Support, Fraud Management, or Collection Department average an hour, meaning only about 5 customers can be helped per day for service instead of making sales. Most branches are open 7 days a week and late hours, but not all necessary departments are available during those times.

review_pros: Although they are sweet initially at the time of interview, making everyone feel it is a great place to work, only the salary package is okay.
review_cons: No job security. Management shouts at employees and makes them feel ashamed and embarrassed even for small mistakes. No good work culture. A lot of politics, especially senior people blame juniors for every mistake. Hiring process is too slow. Senior HRs are irresponsible, making teamwork difficult as they try to dominate and shift blame.

summary: Don't expect to advance at your home store
review_pros: Okay to work for.
review_cons: Don't expect to be treated fairly by District and General Managers.

review_pros: Provides good salary package on time.
review_cons: Products are too costly. Frequent staff changes (every 6 months). Poor team management. Too much pressure from the senior team. No cooperation from admins. Constant threat of losing job. Too much workload. Heavy targets but poor products. Delivers too late and products are damaged. Credit facility is a way of exploitation where employees are caught between dealer and company. If anything goes well, ZSM takes credit; if anyone makes a single mistake, ZSM blames them entirely.

summary: Digital Sales Irving, TX is a mixed bag.
advice_to_management: Provide better new hire training; hiring professionals doesn't automatically mean they know Microsoft. Train them on day-to-day tools and internal processes. Enforce more consistent expectations across all first-line managers and sales teams, as consistency is currently lacking. Improve and streamline internal tools, centralizing them as much as possible. Provide AEs with better access to support for job concerns and task completion. Prevent first-line managers from hiring teams composed exclusively of reps from a single company; ensure diversity in backgrounds. Maintain more continuity in business policies and expectations, insulating reps from misinformation and constantly shifting business priorities.
review_pros: Microsoft offers excellent benefits, including generous 401k matching, great dental insurance, personal wellness programs, and ESPP. Due to name recognition and company footprint, it's easy to secure appointments with customers who are heavily invested in Microsoft technology. Microsoft continuously innovates its technology, which is helpful to sellers.
review_cons: Microsoft's inside sales organization in Irving, TX, called Digital Sales, has a poor onboarding experience. Despite expecting world-class training, new hires receive a quick 2-hour HR meeting and are then left to set up their own equipment. There is no formalized training for external hires beyond standard product-focused videos. Sales tools are archaic and systems don't integrate; for example, spending weeks on Help Desk tickets for system access. Dynamics CRM is antiquated and cumbersome. Management is highly political, imposing constantly shifting priorities that benefit leadership's metrics, forcing reps to sell specific products through discounting rather than solution selling. There's a shift in how specialist teams interact with Digital Account Executives, with mandates for AEs to upskill, raising concerns about the future of specialist roles and piling more responsibilities onto AEs. Many digital sales managers are duplicitous and political, creating a hostile work environment. Policies are implemented haphazardly without proper training or context. There's a lot of internal noise from contractors seeking introductions to customers, many of whom are inconsistent and don't add value. Many long-term employees in this org seem to be 'riding the wave' due to high pay but lack basic skills and Microsoft knowledge. The Digital Sales AE and Specialist roles in Las Colinas are not recommended unless you have extensive prior experience in the Microsoft ecosystem, as enablement for navigating complexities and shifting priorities is lacking for those from other software companies.

review_pros: The front desk receptionist is extremely helpful. The company's location across from Taco Bell and KFC makes meal breaks convenient.
review_cons: AMP has horrible upper management and a toxic, outdated work culture. The CEO's response during the pandemic was, 'Working from home is an earned privilege,' while many accounting firms initiated work-from-home policies until November 2021. Technology and IT infrastructure are obsolete and outdated, with frequently crashing software. While demanding hours are expected during tax season (January through April), working 50-55 hours is expected year-round, making work-life balance non-existent.

summary: Sorry Microsoft - crummy place to work.
advice_to_management: To attract Millennials, overhaul outdated HR tactics. Millennials seek bold, fresh approaches for career growth. There's an intolerance for the 40 and 50+ age crowd, who are hardworking and bring valuable experience. Old processes and mindsets persist. Enable truly diverse teams by age, gender, and race. Remove HR process burdens from employees exploring new growth areas. If headcount reduction is necessary, use ethical formal RIFs instead of playing games with individuals.
review_pros: Azure and Office 365 offer cool tech with a growing customer base.
review_cons: Nothing has changed since leaving Microsoft. Forced ranking persists, with zero bonuses even when goals are exceeded. No tolerance for employees taking career risks or trying new growth areas; a poor fit results in a 'no internal moves' label, ending one's time at MS. There is a strong tendency for Indians to hire Indians, leading to a lack of diversity in teams.

advice_to_management: Communicate with contractors. The manager was seen only 10 times before contract termination, none in formal meetings, which was regrettable.
review_pros: Work in the heart of a Washington legend with a decent salary.
review_cons: Employees are disposable and treated as such. Ideas were overshadowed despite having the most experience in the technology, and larger projects were pushed off for deadlines. Turnover was high, even within the team, with constant rotation and varied input leading to a jumbled mess in development.

advice_to_management: Simplify the admin system.
review_pros: Colleagues are helpful and nice.
review_cons: Too much admin and paperwork.

summary: The worst place you'll ever work.
review_pros: You get paid, though not a lot.
review_cons: Employees can expect mental abuse. Owners will curse at you, constantly threaten dismissal, and overload you with pointless objectives. There are no sick days, maternity leave, or 401k. Relationships with other employees are frowned upon, preventing after-work socialization. Expect long nights and rough days, with expectations to over-deliver constantly for little reward. Management has mental breakdowns, marital disputes, and colleagues cry after department meetings. High turnover, with people quitting or being fired as quickly as they are hired. No one gets a good reference, and moving to another position is seen as betrayal. Employment here is not worth the stress, effort, and pay. Past employee reviews confirm this; anyone saying otherwise is likely the owners themselves. This place cannot be recommended.

review_pros: High income. Above-average benefits package.
review_cons: High income isn't worth the issues. The CEO is a clueless puppet who bankrupted two companies before Champion. Customer service is secondary to profit. Quality of craftsmanship is low.

summary: No help, long hours, miserable environment.
review_pros: The job is fully remote.
review_cons: There is no proper training period; training consists of doing tasks for other consultants for less than two weeks before getting clients. Once assigned clients, you are on your own and referred to a training manual for questions. Employees are given extremely difficult clients with unrealistic expectations and are fired without warning if these expectations aren't met. Expect to work insane hours to catch up on overdue tasks and be yelled at for not finishing work on time. For example, a client requiring 80 hours of work per week is expected to be completed by week's end, regardless of working all night. Many other SEO jobs offer better culture, support, and benefits.

review_pros: Good people (teammates). Growth potential. Good technology.
review_cons: Senior leadership's vision is disconnected from reality. Technical leadership has too much control when trying to monetize software. Mid-level and frontline management lack ideas and simply bark usual directives at reps. Too many tools to manage. Sales cycles are very long, so decent commission checks are not expected for at least 12 months. A large amount of equity is given, which seems good, but not when the stock has only gone down since going public.

review_pros: You work with and learn from the best in the industry; CBI employees take pride in their work and team success. Professional development is encouraged, leading to career success, though a competitive culture means those seeking minimal effort won't thrive. Your voice is heard; CBI embraces the Lencioni Pyramid, allowing for constructive conflict. Opinions and recommendations to management are well-received, provided they come with proposed solutions, not just complaints. As a small company, CBI offers ample opportunities for driven, smart individuals to make significant accomplishments and be recognized for their impact.
review_cons: Being a smaller company means less competitive benefits and limitations to career growth.

summary: Great company ruined by horrible management.
review_pros: A lot was learned when joining Getir. Teammates and boss were amazing, quickly developing business acumen. Deep and meaningful relationships were established with teammates, making work enjoyable.
review_cons: Things went downhill once former consultants were brought into C-level positions. While founders and new hires may be smart, professionalism and company history are needed to avoid issues, such as placing underqualified family members in key roles. The pandemic boost helped, but the company swelled with underqualified people in redundant positions, overburdening those doing actual work. Management was absent.

summary: Great place to learn, bad place to work.
advice_to_management: Listen to employee opinions. Work on proper communication structure. Identify who's working hard and who's stealing credit.
review_pros: Huge brand name. Great learning experience. Good canteen facility.
review_cons: Very low salary compared to the market. No growth opportunities. Politics, favoritism, bad working environment.

advice_to_management: There should be a direct support/advice service to HR, as C-Suite leaders are unaware of the poor culture due to SLT manipulating feedback. Take escalations of professional misconduct seriously and conduct fair investigations. Consider people/leadership training for some managers.
review_pros: Great pay.
review_cons: The company culture is very toxic, and I would not recommend returning or working here. Senior leaders gossip and form cliques, openly discussing staff and other leaders. Some leaders bully and intimidate, which is known, but complaints are ignored, and there's no safeguarding for non-management staff, leading to retaliation. Incidents include staff crying on calls, working while sick, requests not to take holidays, delivery metric manipulation to punish team members, requests for cameras to be on despite valid reasons, and team member burnout. Most people work evenings and weekends, and employees are often fired before replacements are onboarded, leading to zero handover and increased team stress. It's an unhappy workplace, despite colleague relationships. The workload is unrealistic and stressful. There are many other well-paying roles with realistic workloads, competent managers, and less office politics.

review_pros: Good cab facility, good food, excellent work environment.
review_cons: Mental pressure, work partiality, stress.

summary: Your job is in the hands of drunk, stoned, or angry passengers. Can good judgment be expected from them?
advice_to_management: Scrap the rating system. Implement a live support system instead of responding to emails 3 or 4 days later.
review_pros: Paid on time. Plenty of passengers. Fairly anonymous, reducing victimization by hostile cab drivers. Airport trips are generally incident-free and pay the most. One nice rider bought dinner.
review_cons: The driver rating system is fatally flawed. Failure to maintain a 4.7 rating (or 94%) results in termination. Passengers are often drunk, intoxicated, or angry, affecting ratings. The training video's advice ('Hold the door for passengers, offer them water, and you'll get 5-star ratings every time') is misleading. Management does nothing to address driver problems, offering 'Drive more hours' as a solution. The app only shows the correct address about half the time. Incidents include passengers throwing up in the back seat and getting into fistfights.

review_pros: Learned underwriting skills.
review_cons: Terrible management, terrible benefits, no incentive plan, no updated policies, favoritism.

advice_to_management: Invest in new systems and stop equating 'hard-working people' with those who work 24/7. The company is losing many employees daily, and management questions why people are leaving. Overworking people without paying overtime indicates a mental issue with management.
review_pros: Nice office, modern building, free coffee.
review_cons: Be prepared for lack of sleep and food, as only work matters; lunch breaks are non-existent. Systems are outdated, inefficient, and a waste of time; modern systems would improve efficiency and effectiveness. Questionable use of Excel.

advice_to_management: More downtime between calls would help morale.
review_pros: Very welcoming. They understand and treat employees as human beings.
review_cons: The phones are very busy.

advice_to_management: Trust your people.
review_pros: Health plan, benefits, office, snacks.
review_cons: Micromanagement. Low salary. Constant performance reviews.

advice_to_management: Parse evaluations for managers who report to a larger group in Vital Signs. When management's evaluations are cumulative for a group, incompetent managers can thrive and mismanage employees without accountability.
review_pros: Starting pay is $15 an hour for most people.
review_cons: At the highest levels, they are unprofessional, egotistical, and shallow. Medical benefits don't activate until thousands in debt, and management is incompetent.

review_pros: Frontline employees are dedicated, loyal, and incredibly hardworking. They have endured ridiculous deadlines and poor planning, yet achieved miracles with minimal complaints. Current and former StackPath employees are excellent candidates for individual contributor roles.
review_cons: The company's leadership consists of finance professionals and incompetent individuals who have managed to rise. None have real experience managing a technology firm, and they spend most of their time covering this up instead of listening to experienced and knowledgeable people. Anyone who doesn't manage up to them is managed out, and the resulting mass exodus of talent is blamed on former employees. Too many fragile egos and insecurities at the management and executive level prevent ground-level employees from getting things done. The company has been stagnant for two years while competitors posted record profits.

advice_to_management: Get off your high horse. You were all sales reps once.
review_pros: If you work hard and have ambition, you will be very successful and earn great commission. You decide your success; Verizon will not hinder you.
review_cons: There are a lot of politics. A manager was a horrific bully who continually got away with picking on people, forming cliques with some and destroying others.

advice_to_management: Perhaps too many managers are out of touch or big-headed. Overall, if you do your work, you are mostly left alone.
review_pros: Well looked after. Good working environment. Sick scheme. Top pension. Approachable employer. Holiday bonus. Attendance bonus. Progression. Car scheme purchase. Ambassador program. Full canteen. Gym. Shower rooms. EDAP training funding. Weekly pay. Steady pay rises. On-site union. Cash machine. Regular breaks. Good shift pattern. Best hourly pay rate. Generous shift allowance. Cycle to work scheme. 8-hour shifts. Tuck shop (newspapers).
review_cons: None really. Everything was more than generous, though perhaps some employees took it for granted.

advice_to_management: No
review_pros: McDonald's has a good environment.
review_cons: The store's location was bad.

review_pros: Great culture. Remote team, though most are in Colorado, with gatherings twice a year in Denver. Real respect for work-life balance, unlimited PTO, and ability to work from anywhere. Dev and PM teams are competent and efficient. Management truly cares about the team as people, not just resources for financial goals.
review_cons: No major cons. Benefits are expensive, which is common for smaller companies, not a specific con of Fruition but a reality of working for an organization with less than 50 people.

review_pros: Flexibility and culture made tax prep season manageable.
review_cons: The hours associated with tax prep season were long, as is common in the industry.

advice_to_management: good
review_pros: Supportive environment and a very growth-focused environment.
review_cons: Not very well-paid job for freshers.

review_pros: Free credit repair for employee and one other. Good salary (at the time of employment). Yearly employee appreciation activities like a paid movie day.
review_cons: Extreme micromanagement. Unachievable metrics that constantly increase. Employees were forced to mislead and manipulate clients into staying and paying monthly fees for credit repair, which is done by a remote team using a fill-in-the-blank form. When clients want to cancel or cannot pay due to financial issues (e.g., job loss, homelessness), management instructs to 'Just put them on x service level and don’t let them cancel.' The only reason it's not a full-fledged scam is that some people do see great results and credit score improvements.

advice_to_management: Fire people who sexually harass employees.
review_pros: Flexible and fun job with great coworkers.
review_cons: They don't take sexual harassment seriously.

advice_to_management: Get rid of many mid-tier managers (E & F grades) who are stuck in the past. Address the culture gap between ex C&W teams and mobile; act as one company. Review the reward package to attract talent that can make a difference.
review_pros: Very few pros, as cost optimization activities continually sap employee morale. The new CEO is the right person for the job.
review_cons: Rewards are significantly behind market rates. Management is slow to react to market changes. Overly bureaucratic; cuts are not made at the center but always in sales, service, or network. Tools are not fit for purpose. Needs better focus on turnaround programs, currently too internally focused, forgetting customer value.

advice_to_management: Non-stop harassment by the manager. Despite complaining with proof, no action was taken by upper management or the investigating officer. Many team members left because of this manager.
review_pros: Good team members, great teamwork, and knowledge sharing.
review_cons: Non-stop harassment by the manager, trash-talking, humiliating, and nagging.

advice_to_management: Service leadership is a highly successful approach.
review_pros: Loved the people I worked with. Great benefits package.
review_cons: Very strong corporate culture, which has both good and bad aspects. Decisions from New York sometimes didn't make sense for the local market but had to be implemented. Office politics are rampant. As a giant corporation, it can be hard to feel seen, though my sector tried to mitigate that.

Company leadership lies and misleads employees. Account Managers are arrogant without reason. The company does not aim for a positive work environment; its goal is to maximize stress. The real CEO thinks he's above everyone and rarely shows his face.

summary: You will work hard and not get paid much.
advice_to_management: Higher compensation for those doing the work. Make transitioning to different roles easier.
review_pros: You have a job to go to every day.
review_cons: They won't reward hard work, and it just keeps coming. Transitioning to a better role is difficult.

review_pros: Good salary experience, good attendance management.
review_cons: Only English is spoken; if you speak French, you don't work.

summary: Not the worst place, but stale, outdated, and inefficient.
advice_to_management: Do better.
review_pros: The vast majority of junior people seem okay. The salary is higher than the industry average, even for entry-level jobs.
review_cons: Insane hours. Back-to-back meetings during the day, requiring actual work to be done during evenings, weekends, and non-existent spare time, leading to no work-life balance. The vaping division announced a 52% rise in profits, followed by the biggest wave of company cuts in years, indicating how little the company values its staff. Senior management prioritizes profit increases by laying off people and cutting resources. Extremely disorganized processes; no one on the team understands the fundamental step-by-step processes for project execution, which is alarming. No proper structure, at least in the vaping division. Pointless bureaucracy with meaningless forms and excessive alignment/pre-alignment meetings. The top leadership is clearly an 'old boy's club.' Excessive sucking up to senior management in meetings is unpleasant to witness. A proper, old-fashioned corporate machine.

advice_to_management: Completely revamp the entire business management approach.
review_pros: Safe, good working hours, nice internal staff (except sales).
review_cons: Boring. Lack of progression. Self-service hell. Extremely bureaucratic. Non-competitive products. Bad customer service. Impossible to get pay raises.

advice_to_management: Empower employees.
review_pros: Size and reach provide great scope.
review_cons: Size and spread of function limit innovation.

review_pros: Holidays off and personal time.
review_cons: Management is terrible. The school is a dangerous environment because the administration has no control. They have no backbone or experience for their roles. The school asks staff to physically manage students but does not support staff when parents have complaints. You can lose the credentials you worked hard for because staff are often hotlined with no support from the school; their solution is to fire you.

review_pros: Great environment, great people to work with.
review_cons: Do not share management issues with employees; always report to HR.

advice_to_management: Change uniforms. Change back commission.
review_pros: Used to be great. Good benefits.
review_cons: No opportunities for growth anymore. Socialist commission structure.

advice_to_management: Help local managers more, with training.
review_pros: Good people, good staff members.
review_cons: Poor management, underpaid and stressed.

advice_to_management: Changing policies and rules every other month is really confusing. Not allowing branch managers to hire new staff is very inconvenient to both employees and customers. Most of the training is irrelevant to the job position. Too many responsibilities for the amount of pay that is offered. Other banks seem to be much more organized in this aspect.
review_pros: Really good benefits including healthcare, vacation time, sick time, etc.
review_cons: Majority of the time you are dealing with more than 3 things at once, especially when understaffed. The training never ends (new training almost every month). 30-minute breaks. Customers can be really rude at times. Too many responsibilities for the pay.

advice_to_management: Hire better managers.
review_pros: Good people around and it’s a nice office.
review_cons: Overworked and underpaid, and performance review isn’t accurate.

review_pros: Compensation, flexibility, and a great team.
review_cons: Workload and schedule.

review_pros: Looking back at it, it was particularly hard work, and perks like drinks were nice.
review_cons: Management was failing; after I left, my whole organization fell apart. My manager constantly demanded things that were unproducible. Delivered on large projects and got no validation.

summary: Great company to work but very little growth
advice_to_management: Increase compensation.
review_pros: Great work. New technologies. Freedom to take any user stories and no compulsion.
review_cons: Slow growth. Very low compensation.

advice_to_management: Control on senior level employees.
review_pros: Good relationship with customers.
A good place to work.
review_cons: Residents create problems during work.

advice_to_management: Local managers, stand up for your employees.
review_pros: Steady work, great benefits. Casual attire.
review_cons: Very stressful call center; we were holding up to 100 calls every day upon arrival, with up to 60 calls waiting at quitting time. Extremely strict rules for all metrics to be met to hold your job.

summary: Pathetic .25 cent raise after working during COVID-19
advice_to_management: Stop being so greedy and pass some of that profit down to the hard workers on your front line. Do what you say. Stop lying.
review_pros: Work on your own mostly, pension plan.
review_cons: Treated like pond scum. Merchandisers are at the very bottom. You and your life hold no importance. You are replaceable. They will work you to death and use you up. Not appreciated or valued at all. They only hire young adults 22 to 30 for management positions, rarely anyone over 30. All they care about is their profit, but next to nothing trickles down to you. They can donate 2 billion to BLM, but only give merchandisers a 25 cent raise. Pathetic.

advice_to_management: Eat healthy food
review_pros: If you want it to look good on your resume, it might be worth it. Benefits are acceptable, but only when compared to employee contributions.
review_cons: Over 80% of your energy is wasted on office politics, as you never know if the person sitting beside you is related to management. Working smart is not the reason for promotion; knowing the 'game' is. For example, if someone is doing a job incorrectly in a meeting, it's best to stay silent and act ignorant, otherwise you will be 'let go'. When issues arise, the first thing to do is cover your own back. To stay long-term, you either work extremely hard without respect, or you play the political game by any means necessary. System failures are common due to low-quality system design, analysis, and management, and no one truly cares about their work or their peers.

review_pros: Pay is good for sales position.
review_cons: Operations team is unorganized, racist, and terrible.

summary: Great Company Who Cares About Employees
advice_to_management: I had the absolute best branch manager ever. If they are all like him, I would say keep doing what you're doing. A great manager can dramatically impact an employee's career and makes the biggest difference in the workplace.
review_pros: Very positive company culture and support from all levels.
review_cons: Employment was based on passing the Series 6 in a short amount of time. I was unaware at the time of hire that my employment was contingent upon passing the test the first attempt and that I would be terminated immediately. I happened to be among the first round of employees they were terminating upon failure of Series 6 on the first attempt. Prior to me, if an employee failed, they were given another chance. My branch manager was a huge support and fought to see if anything could be done.

summary: Great place to work pending your manager
advice_to_management: Needs improvement.
review_pros: Plenty of opportunities throughout the firm.
review_cons: Constant organizational changes to the firm.

summary: This is a good job if you’re trying to move up to a better position with another job. I left because of burnout.
advice_to_management: Improve your communication or you will continue to be a revolving door. Be more aware of employee burnout and let employees have some time off of work and stop caring only for yourselves.
review_pros: Okay pay, work from home.
review_cons: Slow moving up at this company, hardly any time off, no paid holidays. They don’t really care about you and they don’t know how to communicate well.

review_pros: None that I saw while being there for over 6 years.
review_cons: Too many to mention in this small space, a nasty toxic environment to work in.

review_pros: Flexibility with schedule and work from home, great benefits.
review_cons: No growth in yearly pay.

summary: If you want to make money
review_pros: If you want to grow, you just have to ask and you’ll probably start getting trained to level up.
review_cons: There’s a lot. First, wages. It’s heartbreaking to see how much the cost of living has increased in Idaho—where they’re headquartered—and they have made almost no increases to wages. Most hourly jobs base pays are 15-25% lower than the market average, which you can look at from their HR page. Somehow they keep bringing on new clients and can’t figure out how to budget for more people at higher wages. When COVID hit and fast food restaurants started posting their new pay at $14+, they still haven’t found a way to accommodate and adjust the pay for their employees. The benefits are not great, but that’s intentional. They market how they can save companies money on health insurance by making very high deductibles and out-of-pocket maximums; it’s silly to think they wouldn’t use that on their own company. They made it seem like getting bought by Anthem would be so beneficial for everyone, but years later, still no changes in pay or benefits. They can’t keep up with their workload so they’ve started outsourcing to “backfill” for some teams, but my guess is that those teams will end up becoming fully outsourced. Anthem is going to steamroll Ameriben, and my guess is within the next 5 years (if that) Ameriben will cease to exist.

summary: Do not lay off pregnant women and take harassment and bullying seriously
review_pros: Great spirit within the local teams. Interesting challenges to solve and huge impact possible. Good work-life balance.
review_cons: Often unhealthy relationships across locations worldwide caused by upper management. Most/all decisions are made in Germany. Upper management and HR accept/protect company-wide known toxic managers who bully/harass on a daily basis. There is a lot of talk about Bosch values, but upper management doesn't live by them.

review_pros: The floor staff on subacute.
review_cons: Everything else. Administration doesn't listen to any of their employees.

advice_to_management: HR needs progressive leadership.
review_pros: On the surface, the HR team seems like an encouraging bunch, but when you dig deeper, they are just trying to keep their heads above water. Averaging over 100 plus open jobs with a time to fill at about six months, it's like digging a hole on a beach with a cup. The budget is shoestring, but they have high hopes. It's a no-win if you don't have the right people on your side.
review_cons: Pay, work-life balance.

summary: Here people work for overtime and money only
advice_to_management: HR is a puppet of Management; think for employees too.
review_pros: Exceptional perks and awesome bonus. Company provides medical benefits and family support. Join company only if you want money and nothing else.
review_cons: Bad Korean culture, master-slave relationship between headquarters and subsidiaries, technically no growth. Company follows military culture. HR is a puppet of management, not an employee-friendly company. Foreigners are not treated well.

review_pros: You get paid on time.
review_cons: All you do is have meaningless meetings and hear others talking about your work because, for reasons unknown, one or two 'stars' in the team get to present everything. Some of your coworkers who just graduated from an average school a few years ago, for reasons unclear to me, take themselves to be the 'expert' in the field and definitely acted like they are better than the rest of the team and kept telling you what to do. I can go on with how toxic it is to work there, but you can save yourself some trouble and avoid this place. You better do whatever the leadership team tells you to do, including coming to the office to meet in person, otherwise you'll be fired.

advice_to_management: Give better pay.
review_pros: Good environment, helpful staff.
review_cons: Low salary and poor work-life balance.

summary: Does Not Honor their Mission statement and Values
review_pros: There is not one 'Pro' at this company.
review_cons: No communication, so you can't do your job efficiently and correctly. No training. Gossip and extremely unprofessional Senior leaders who lack much-needed knowledge of managing an organization. Discrimination, and you feel like you're walking on eggshells all the time. Very heartless management with one-sided views who aren't open to others' thoughts or suggestions. No empowerment; no room for growth; low team morale across the board, whether it's in the clinics or their corporate headquarters. Worst place I've worked in my whole life. Horrible work environment and thick air 24/7. If you want to feel undervalued and not appreciated for your dedication and hard work, work here.

summary: Great People but Terrible Leadership/Business Outlook
review_pros: The people at this company are where it shines. It's a very close-knit group of people who genuinely care about you. There's a lot of room for internal movement if you're okay with putting in maximum effort without the proper tools. It's a great place to learn, especially if you're right out of school. Small company, so you get direct time with leadership.
review_cons: Startup company mindset without being a startup. For some reason, leadership can't get out of the old, toxic patterns many startups have like grind culture and 'we're all a family here' attitudes. This company demands everything quickly and doesn't care about sustainability. The company culture is poor. When I started, it was healthier. Now it's large workloads with little recognition. Very much an attitude of 'keep grinding and don't stop.' I wish they celebrated wins. I grew to very much dislike the upper leadership, mainly COO and CEO. We were told that we were the experts on our projects, but then they'd tell you exactly how to do everything and give you unachievable goals. Very quick to speak and slow to listen. Leadership wants to have every department under their thumb and under complete control that they aren't willing to admit they don't have all of the answers. They should rely on the experience of those actually doing the day-to-day work, not just their own opinions. Their business outlook is terrible. Everything is on a month-to-month basis. Leadership could tell you to focus all your time on one project for a month then scrap it the next. If they don't see success within a few weeks, they consider the initiative a failure. Definitely not how business projects work. Leadership is so focused on succeeding quickly that they don't care to give you the time or the resources to properly do your job. From my experience/outlook, leadership is sexist when it comes to promotions. During my time there, at least two male colleagues received promotions while female workers (who had been there longer/were doing just as well) never advanced even though there was room for their advancement. I don't think leadership feels like they have a preference towards men, but that's exactly what a blind bias is. They need to reconsider how they value their employees. You will be expected to work without the proper tools. Anything outside of the meager platforms they already have is out of the question. They aren't willing to invest in the proper tools for you to do your job. Very much a 'use what you have' attitude, but their expectations and goals far outshine what can be done with what they gave us.

review_pros: Work-life balance. Good people. Fun atmosphere.
review_cons: Uncertainty of job security during a pandemic.

advice_to_management: This company has more VPs and CEOs than necessary. All the money they are paying their higher-ups to sit around making videos to send to staff that nobody has time to watch since we are monitored every single second on our PCs. There is no recognition given out, only criticism, and I’ve been here for years. I do love my teammates though and the lower-level Supervisors.
review_pros: Flexibility for taking time off.
review_cons: The insurance is so incredibly expensive! Raises at 2-3% mean you need to change positions after 2 years to actually make money.

review_pros: The residents and co-workers.
review_cons: Everything else: management, pay, short-staffed.

review_pros: Casual dress. General sense of friendliness at the office.
review_cons: The longer you work at Kipsu, the more you realize that the company is spinning its wheels. Yes, Kipsu was impacted by COVID-19 more than other software companies (being entrenched in the hospitality industry), but even despite that, before the pandemic and through the recovery, you can see that Kipsu is just sputtering along. The product barely ever changes. Sales goals are often not met. Bugs are rarely resolved. Customers request things that rarely ever get followed through on. As a whole, the company is way too frugal and tries to DIY everything, which makes the employees feel strapped and overworked.

review_pros: RH has been around a long time and is trying to move into its next phase. Nice office space and nice management team.
review_cons: The company monitors your calls and watches everything you do. They are also very intent on adhering to a scripted call and meeting quotas within a specific time frame, which during a pandemic was almost impossible to meet.

review_pros: There are some pretty talented people there, but the only ones that last are the ones who are either 'yes' people, or the ones who somehow happen to agree with management.
review_cons: Very toxic culture, crazy high turnover. Big whale customers clearly steer the ship. Very unlikely it will ever manage to turn a profit due to insane overhead. You can give your all to the company, generally over-extending yourself, but the minute you disagree and push back, you're gone with zero warning. Promises are made to investors without factoring in the reality of how that will harm the employees, and then employees are expected to make up for the inadequacies of management. The business chases whales instead of thoughtful business practices. Their business is too heavily reliant on service providers that make the concept of them ever actually growing from a startup to a mature company completely implausible. They've been around for 10 years and still consider themselves a startup and use that to make excuses for lack of vision and guidance. Buyer beware.

review_pros: Some perks included free breakfasts and free cinema tickets.
review_cons: This is for DMS UK located in London Bridge, United Kingdom. A highly unprofessional company that is predominantly based on the labor of graduates or young people with little to no experience. The company does not value experience and does not want to pay experienced staff what they deserve. Employees are overburdened with work and constantly put under very tight, almost unreasonable, deadlines. There is no appreciation at all, no equal treatment, no internal training: just a constant flow of requests. The company expects employees to work overtime more often than not, to skip lunch breaks and, in general, to finish late. There is a high level of turnover, partly due to the fact that employees do not pass their probation period and the company does not offer valid reasons for their dismissal. One of the worst, if not the worst company I ever worked for.

summary: Good company, not so good to work for
review_pros: Knowledgeable employees, great 401k match, great benefits.
review_cons: Non-competitive salary and compensation program for salaried/corporate employees, not good working hours, hidden expectations, decent management at corporate level.

summary: Not a bad place to sharpen your skills
review_pros: Lots of training opportunities, and because it's under the Class III FDA bubble, there's a ton of attention to detail skills you gain. State-of-the-art facilities. Really awesome co-workers. A lot of little benefits like gift card giveaways, team lunches, etc. Great working culture.
review_cons: No sick pay, which became very apparent during COVID-19. Medical benefits are very pricey. Because of the lack of sick time, you are forced to manage your 112 off hours as if you would get sick for a week or two a year, leaving you with maybe a week or so of vacation. Like most companies, you have to negotiate really hard for higher pay.

review_pros: YVFWC is a good place to work overall. They really have a mission-oriented approach, and the heart of Carlos and senior leadership is in the right place. YVFWC is the kind of company that is dedicated to underserved populations and often provides services that are not reimbursable because it's the right thing to do. While it varies from clinic to clinic, it often feels like a 'big family' type of environment with good people rallied around the same mission. Carlos (YVFWC's CEO) is passionate about the community and is willing to take calculated risks to be the best in healthcare. The company is committed to actually providing good care and not just checking the boxes.
review_cons: Because the company is so mission-focused and federal funding can fluctuate, YVFWC relies on high productivity from their medical providers. Sometimes this causes burnout and frustration and causes a divide between clinical and administrative employees. That said, the providers are given adequate resources such as dietitians, behavioral health providers, and community health workers so they are not isolated in caring for their patients. These resources help shoulder the burden for the providers even though the volume expectations may be high.

advice_to_management: No point - if management can approve of such leadership.
review_pros: Pipeline, culture, opportunities, and remuneration.
review_cons: Poor leadership in Singapore country division. Too much 'fats' at region level.

summary: I enjoyed working at Merck until my department was OUTSOURCED to India. Seems to be the trend.
advice_to_management: When you outsource your employees, guess what? You get cut too!
review_pros: Work-life balance. Good salary.
review_cons: Too much management, not enough workers.

advice_to_management: Better management.
review_pros: Good working balance with great benefits.
review_cons: Management rude and very tired.

advice_to_management: Work with more empathy towards your staff to empower them.
review_pros: Corporate learning. Good management school.
review_cons: No work-life balance.

advice_to_management: Change the culture. A lot more can be achieved with the right mindset and values.
review_pros: Just like a government office.
review_cons: Old people with aging hustle culture. They don't allow you to take risks.

summary: Okay job, ruined by poor leadership
advice_to_management: Stop communicating via third parties (it is not effective); if you need to talk to someone, talk to them. Treat your people like people. You don't own people; it is illegal. People have choices. Stop expecting part-time retail employees to care so much. Sort your attitude out; if you have a rubbish attitude, so will your staff. Respect is a luxury, not a right. The time for managers sitting in their offices and barking orders is over; people need and want good leaders. Advice to regional management and up: If your stores have high turnover, it's because of the manager. People leave when they don't feel respected and appreciated.
review_pros: Discount is okay. The night job is pretty fun when you get along with your colleagues. No management to deal with on nights.
review_cons: Poor management - they take advantage of the fact most colleagues cannot lose their jobs with no notice as a tactic for bullying and threatening behavior. They basically like to think they own you. No appreciation - you can have a really busy night and still get everything done and more, with not even a thank you. Help out at other branches again with no thank you, from your manager or the manager at the other branch. Lack of understanding regarding how it is more difficult to sleep during the day due to life noise of the world, light, and heat. Poor attitude and behavior from management - especially when they are coming in at the end of a long night. Lack of training - Training/continuous training is nonexistent for night staff. My induction was a 9-5 shift during the day in which I spent most of it on a till serving customers on my own. And the only training I got was regarding day shift jobs/procedures. I got 2 night shifts training with 2 Customer Assistants in which they are not paid extra to do, and after that I was pretty much on my own. Extra tasks that get pushed onto night shift are not communicated effectively, leading to us being scolded because the task wasn't done/done incorrectly, for tasks which we have never been trained to do. Management just expects us to figure it out. Poor ineffective communication - finding out after going to work that you were supposed to be at another branch, therefore no communication regarding working at other stores, telling other store assistants and hoping the message gets through to the right people. It feels like the morning management settings are downright hostility or apathy, neither of which make you feel good at the end of a shift.

advice_to_management: Need to do something to foster a sense of ownership and community for employees.
review_pros: Lot of client experience and learning challenges.
review_cons: Defragmented employee base due to client support.

advice_to_management: Study leadership. Itaú promotes many good analysts to managers without preparing them for management roles. They are more concerned with their individual careers than with developing people.
review_pros: Great school for career starters. Many growth opportunities in digital, IT, or product areas. Very good benefits. Good salary if negotiated well. Huge networking opportunities due to the number of employees and contact with various areas. If you play the game well, do good work, and are political, you will succeed.
review_cons: Over time, the culture makes you want to leave or prevents you from being as good a professional as they demand. For example, they want you to be questioning, but not too much. Frequent displays of authority from higher-ups, besides the politicking, make it rare to be yourself and grow. Not many opportunities for introverted people; you either play the game or you won't grow as desired.

advice_to_management: Be more careful in evaluating promotions and opportunities for dedicated employees.
review_pros: Defined career plan. Good for career start.
review_cons: Low salary. Difficult to get promoted; the higher the position, the more political it becomes to advance. Depending on the project, there is an excessive accumulation of overtime.

advice_to_management: Treat your CSRs better; almost more than half my training class quit.
review_pros: Great benefits, and that’s really it.
review_cons: They treat contact center employees well in training, but after you hit the floor it’s a different story. You get told not to call out, but you have a lot of sick time to use before the year is up and can only carry over 40 hours. Customers are always rude, and bad surveys affect your metrics negatively.

advice_to_management: Salary correction.
review_pros: Excellent colleagues and manager. Good learning opportunities. Good exposure.
review_cons: Below par salary as per industry standards. Not good hike.

summary: Disgusting lack of leadership across nearly all band levels.
advice_to_management: Grow up.
review_pros: Band 1 and Band 2 employees have very good work ethic and drive.
review_cons: Band 3+ employees do not know what they are doing, and everyone screws each other over for promotions, treating newer employees like slaves without proper compensation. Band 4+ employees are lazy and chase trophies based on their employees' work without rewarding those who actually do the work.

advice_to_management: Ensure workers have enough time off.
review_pros: Good pay, benefits, and overtime compensation.
review_cons: Long hours and weekends can be draining.

advice_to_management: Keep up the great work!
review_pros: Great management and exceptional training.
review_cons: Few cons were found while working there.

advice_to_management: Try changing the culture.
review_pros: Many opportunities to learn and develop.
review_cons: Very bad colleagues and no motivation from managers or peers.

summary: Not good for freshers, but good for experienced individuals due to handsome salary.
review_pros: Work environment is very good, seniors are helpful and knowledgeable (many from Aricent and Cdot). No work pressure. Good salary for experienced people joining VNL.
review_cons: Not much developing work. Still not working in LTE. No training for freshers, which may hinder their career prospects.

summary: The company rejected me after giving an offer letter as a fresher, two months later. Never trust this company again.
review_cons: They can fire you anytime; not trustworthy.

review_pros: Great clinical variety and caseload. Loved the immediate team.
review_cons: The company prioritizes profit over the well-being of clients and clinicians. Pay was terrible, and the overburdening and undervaluing of staff were unacceptable. Hard-working clinicians were run into the ground due to unattainable billable hours, administrative tasks, reporting, and marketing (staff were told to do their own marketing). This, combined with a taxing clinical workload, makes it difficult for new graduates. Unrealistic KPIs and billable hour expectations do not allow for planning, collaboration, or evidence-based practice. The bonus scheme is impossible to benefit from. There is poor communication from management and constant micromanaging of schedules. The company is not inclusive, with no flexibility or willingness to accommodate clinicians with disabilities.

review_pros: Coworkers are very knowledgeable; senior employees provide guidance while juggling their own responsibilities. Company "Peloton" awards (company-wide acknowledgment, gift card) can be granted for recognizing great work. It is a smaller company with a tight-knit "family" feel, fostering friendships through regular happy hours, ping pong/foosball, "President's Club" trips, and recreational sports. As an Oracle/Google partner, there is a high probability of working with large, exciting organizations regularly. Consulting allows for working on multiple projects simultaneously and switching to new projects regularly, accelerating learning and introducing a wide variety of technologies.
review_cons: As a small company supporting many products, employees need to wear many hats and fill in on projects based on sales pipeline demand. It is difficult to become a true expert or keep up with the latest features in a few products when 20-40 hours/week are spent on unrelated projects. This leads to being a reactive resource rather than a proactive authority in sales calls or new projects. Work-from-home options are limited and restrictive. A significant portion of pay comes from an "Incentive Bonus" based solely on billing hours to client projects, which is detrimental to other initiatives like supporting the sales team, internal infrastructure/demo environment maintenance, product development, training and certifications, documenting standard practices, and management/mentorship responsibilities.

review_pros: Work experience depends on the assigned team; it could be amazing with a great leader or the opposite.
review_cons: Low salary and a ridiculous 3% bonus performance. No flexibility for work-from-home. Micromanagement. No career opportunities; employees are stuck in the same role.

summary: Stay away from this company. As previously stated, they lie and manipulate.
review_pros: Employees become immune to extreme politics and unprofessionalism. One can learn how to deal with a fake company and management, and how to digest significant lies, as many, especially HR and seniors, are dishonest.
review_cons: Salaries are never credited on time; sometimes they are paid only once every 1.5 to 2 months. Performance-linked amounts are never credited, even after fulfilling criteria (e.g., 5 years of service). Information on their website and other sites is false; there are fewer than 20 employees and no offices in Singapore or Delhi as claimed. Previous experience shown by employees is fake, using top company brand names. They are not management consultants and have no ongoing projects or clients, misleading new joiners with promises of training when they themselves lack business knowledge. Almost everyone has left, and current employees plan to leave once their delayed salaries are paid. They post fake job openings for social media appearance. Knowledgeable people are hired for short periods (1-3 months) to complete tasks, from whom the company learns, but then they fail to pay salaries.

summary: Expect a problematic 'grad culture' in Mumbai Technology.
advice_to_management: None. People who would listen are no longer there. Many good people in upper management have recently left, and good employees are not looking to stay.
review_pros: Some really good teams exist. Good benefits include around 16 public holidays, unlimited sick leave, and transport. Some may appreciate the culture.
review_cons: The 'grad culture' allows people to move quickly up the ladder without much experience, leading them to unknowingly become pompous. Food is not subsidized. Managers may take credit for travel opportunities employees worked hard for.

advice_to_management: Recruit more young graduates and give more importance to apprentices, interns, and doctoral students, as they represent the future of engineering and production in tomorrow's industry.
review_pros: Very rich environment. Many professions discovered, especially from the design to the production of aircraft.
review_cons: The company's kinetics are affected by too many personnel and niche employee functions.

advice_to_management: Treat people as individuals, not machines.
review_cons: Management treats employees as disposable assets daily. You will regret joining this company.

review_pros: Not held accountable for missing delivery dates. Slow pace of work. Easy to hide.
review_cons: Too many changes in leadership. Leaders are afraid to make decisions, leading to much second-guessing. Not a good place unless you have excellent political skills.

advice_to_management: The CEO is poor; base pay and annual incentive pay are very low.
review_pros: Used to be a good company.
review_cons: The CEO changed the entire culture from customer-first and willing to fix fees to prioritizing fees over everything. Managers are unwilling to promote people or support career advancement.

advice_to_management: Ensure people leaders have a vested interest in their team's professional growth and tie their goals to it.
review_pros: Flexible working arrangements.
review_cons: Leaders could use more training.

advice_to_management: Implement more clearly defined lines of communication between businesses and departments to reduce redundancies. Teams should work together and encourage mutual growth, as competition often stunts progress at Citi.
review_pros: Great benefits and pay in line with industry salaries. Many opportunities for promotion and to switch business areas.
review_cons: Too much bureaucracy. Lack of communication between business units and working groups, leading to the same project being done by multiple groups unknowingly.

review_pros: Great company with great benefits.
review_cons: Depends on the leadership at the time.

advice_to_management: They need to pay more.
review_pros: People were fun to work with.
review_cons: Not worth the pay.

advice_to_management: Invest in marketing and allow sales people to do their jobs. Understand that U.S. regulations are different from Canada's, so operations will not run the same. Let managers do their jobs and stop trying to run every department from ivory towers, and stop bringing in consultants to change things annually. The numbers clearly showed a capable team. Also, expect to work 50+ hours per week.
review_pros: Good benefits, but that's where the advantages end for sales roles.
review_cons: RBC Bank appears to hate its sales staff, constantly reducing staff yearly while increasing goals. Sales staff are reduced, but more audit, risk, and other cost-incurring departments are hired. The sales department, which brings in revenue, is constantly under pressure, and no matter how well you perform, the CEO still treats you poorly.

summary: Liars, scammers, or frauds—it's hard to tell which one.
review_pros: You can work from home, but often for free.
review_cons: Do not believe fake reviews claiming timely payment; it almost never happens. Employees often wait over a month to get paid, and the pay is extremely low. Admins and the CEO of Cosmic Strands speak poor English, making it difficult to imagine how they can judge quality academic work. The company still owes money to employees.

review_pros: Felt supported, loved coworkers, loved the brand concept. Normal hours, united team, good benefits offered.
review_cons: Would like to see faster company growth, quicker career progression, and salary raises commensurate with growing inflation.

review_pros: Great mission. Good technical stack and generally good skills within the engineering community.
review_cons: Seriously lacks leadership and direction from the top.

summary: Poor management and women are not treated well.
review_pros: Depending on the role, there's a good work-life balance.
review_cons: No organization or strategy. Employees are not empowered or enabled to do their jobs. There are chauvinistic men who talk down to female staff.

review_pros: Pay is decent for the work.
review_cons: Employees have to pay for medical. Not vested in the retirement plan for almost 4 years. Everyone is replaceable, regardless of tenure.

summary: Terrible experience overall; do not recommend.
review_pros: Flexibility and remote work.
review_cons: Underpaid (acknowledged by leadership but not changed). Overworked. No recognition. Toxic environment full of favoritism. Target of bullying and harassment. No career planning, growth, or personal development opportunities (internal programs lacked relevant courses). Poor communication and no transparency. Loss of many amazing people in leadership completely changed the culture and department function. Had to fight for an entitled referral bonus, which was never received.

advice_to_management: Pay more money.
review_pros: Home working. Outbound calling. Nice role with no strict targets. Working with nice customers who want to help.
review_cons: Minimum wage. Scripted calls are very robotic.

advice_to_management: Management is professional.
review_pros: Good and professional BPO, and a leading company for growth.
review_cons: There are rules that need to be followed.

advice_to_management: Try to provide lunch for the night batch.
review_pros: Nice place to work, good cafeteria.
review_cons: New process when I joined.

review_pros: Good work-life balance and working hours.
review_cons: No growth perspectives. Management is unreachable, and HR is an absolute shame.
Employees experience free humiliation because management has hired all relatives.

review_pros: Friendly officers to work with.
review_cons: Terrible management and pay. Expectations often involve doing work that should be done by maintenance companies. No COVID safety measures, and cars are not cleaned after positive test results.

review_pros: Easy to move up with minimal effort. Friendly teams. Flexibility for different roles.
review_cons: Long hours. Understaffed, often working with skeleton crews. Constant demands and unorganized direction from upper management.

summary: Terrible upper management made work frustrating.
review_pros: No early hours. Ability to "check out" games for product knowledge. Employee discount.
review_cons: Toxic upper management. Confusing floor plans. Customers expect employees to know everything about every game/system. Non-negotiable trade-in prices frustrate customers, who assume employees determine the value of worn-out controllers.

advice_to_management: Very nice staff.
review_pros: Very good place to work.
review_cons: Very fun people to be

advice_to_management: More meetings and actually take suggestions.
review_pros: Frequent raises, opportunities for promotions, flexible schedule.
review_cons: Favoritism among employees, catty workers, many high schoolers who bring drama, sometimes lazy management, frequent management changes, and lazy employees who don't contribute equally.

summary: Another service-based company calling itself a product-based company.
review_pros: The new office in Phase 3. A lot to learn as a Software Developer (if in the right place).
review_cons: Operates like a typical service-based company despite claiming to be product-based. Products are developed purely based on client requirements, meaning projects start from scratch even for similar ideas. Business Leaders (VPs or AVPs) hold excessive power, able to hire, fire, speak rudely, or impose sanctions without cause or review, often based on tenure (15+ years) or nepotism. Hikes are not performance-based but tied to current salary; freshers get higher hikes (20-30%) than experienced employees (5-8%), or no hike at all. Despite quarter-on-quarter profits during COVID-19, the company imposed salary cuts and layoffs. Leaves and salary are deducted for forgetting to fill time-logs, with no benefit of the doubt. HR has no authority; VPs make final decisions. Everything requires three levels of approval, even unblocking a website.

advice_to_management: Leave.
review_pros: Some people were pleasant to work with.

advice_to_management: Understand the aspirations of consultants and allow them to decide their career paths.
review_pros: UBS has a great work culture and genuinely cares for its employees. Supportive and knowledgeable peer group. Good work-life balance.
review_cons: Operates as a body shopping practice where consultants are placed based on management requirements rather than their interests and competencies, leading to dissatisfaction. Slow growth compared to Big4 firms globally.

summary: One of the worst MNCs to work for; a mistake in my life.
advice_to_management: If you are a product-based company, behave like one.
review_pros: Hardly any pros. The India Unit of shared services GCC may look good from outside, but has a pathetic work environment internally.
review_cons: The company initially acts result-oriented, but if you exceed expectations, they will find fault in your methods (even if long-standing) and try to prove your work is worthless. Management is immature; taking independent decisions for the company's benefit without involving your immediate line manager can jeopardize your project and career, potentially leading to termination. GCC does not value loyalty from consistent performers; even after 10 years, management will find a way to dismiss you, resulting in zero job security at ABInBev. It is advisable not to consider any offer from GCC, even if unemployed. Management and HR are the worst experienced to date; HR was better when with Accenture. Office politics are worse than Indian politics. Aside from formal work attire and GCC office infrastructure, there is nothing attractive about the company.

summary: A good place to work despite a few difficult individuals.
advice_to_management: Implement better structures for reward, recognition, and talent development to avoid favoritism or unfair compensation schemes based solely on HR input. The company is losing a lot of good talent.
review_pros: Good benefits, annual leave, pension, and car allowance. Global leadership has a good philosophy that needs to trickle down to the rest of the organization.
review_cons: Some managers play favorites, affecting growth prospects. HR was not supportive of associates.

summary: You have to work to get the rewards.
advice_to_management: Management is likely frustrated about losing good people to external opportunities. However, paying people to match the competition, even with an out-of-step pay increase requiring higher approval, is worthwhile considering the cost of hiring someone new. More external learning opportunities (e.g., conferences) for mid-level operations staff would improve performance and reduce feelings of isolation within 'the machine'.
review_pros: Lots of opportunity if willing to work hard and be humble. A company this big always has a place for the right fit. Endless opportunities to learn how things work in the financial services industry, if flexible and willing to move laterally or geographically.
review_cons: Generally pays middle-of-the-market and, due to economic downturn, does not bonus to meet or beat the market as in the past. There are many difficult personalities and politics, some of whom advance quickly, requiring tolerance. Finding the right management fit can be difficult. Employees can earn more and advance higher by leaving and returning. A level of tolerance for market uncertainty is required.

advice_to_management: Management should definitely try to improve the company's work culture; otherwise, the high attrition rate problem cannot be solved.
review_pros: Salary is good. Township is good.
review_cons: Bad HR policies. Pathetic food quality. No safety measures followed. Bad middle management. Bad work culture. No work-life balance.

advice_to_management: None, everything is very good.
review_pros: Many and very good; it's worth it.
review_cons: Few; everything is very viable and good.

advice_to_management: Use consultants and temporary workforces instead of H1 visas from Asia.
review_cons: Incompetent management from top down. Many cheap H1B visa laborers run the show with limited understanding of the U.S. financial system and markets. John Waters is incompetent. Full-time employees are treated like contractors. Easy to get a job offer, but can be fired without notice or severance. Age, gender, and race discrimination are rampant in the MRM team from top down.

summary: Great CEO, but other executives are not as good.
review_pros: Liberal environment and socially vibrant culture.
review_cons: Some bullying behavior by senior leaders is very difficult to report or professionally resolve.

summary: Fast-growing company with many opportunities for growth.
review_pros: CommCorp has made strides in DEI efforts, creating an inclusive and supportive workplace culture. They actively encourage applications from all backgrounds and provide ongoing training for everyone to feel valued. There are plenty of opportunities for growth and advancement, allowing employees to take on new responsibilities and learn new skills. Highly recommended for those seeking a great place to work that values DEI and offers room for growth.
review_cons: There have been many recent shifts and growth; if strategies are not understood, it can feel like a lot of change, especially in a remote environment. Work-life balance can be hard to manage due to everyone's passion.

summary: Manage merch team and supervisor of sales
review_pros: Vacation time.
review_cons: No work-life balance.

advice_to_management: Treat employees better.
review_pros: Discounted products. Good salary. Job security.
review_cons: Stress. Overwork. Bad retirement plan.

review_pros: The job is work from home.
review_cons: Management is lazy; everyone makes up rules as they go, leading to inconsistency, then holds employees responsible for their mistakes. Communication is only via Teams, so nothing is recorded, and managers give conflicting instructions. The job lacks professionalism until management wants to criticize something, despite managers having crying kids, barking dogs, and side conversations with spouses during team meetings. Management bullied the employee into working on their son's birthday, then used a computer malfunction as a reason for termination. They also allowed the employee to work from the hospital during their son's surgery using hotspots to avoid being short-handed, then used this as a reason for termination. They fired the employee without incident on their first day back from vacation because they couldn't hold managers accountable for not approving time off correctly.

review_pros: Nice office, snacks, everyone is suffering together.
review_cons: Moved offices 3 times in 2 years, leadership shifted constantly and was secretive, harassment reports, legal concerns.

review_pros: Being able to clock out and walk away for a bathroom break.
review_cons: Training sucked, hours sucked, doing everybody's work on top of your own, and being treated as a person is nonexistent. You're just a number.

summary: Could be good but can never be great.
advice_to_management: Scrap 'LEI'. LEI should be used for FAs, FPs, PBAs. LCEA/CEA roles are not long enough experiences to impress a client. Customers with prior bad experiences elsewhere sometimes give negative survey feedback for unrelated transactions at my branch, unfairly impacting my performance.
review_pros: Depending on the branch, there could be the best co-workers, good managers, and a good environment. Many benefits. You get to feel proud working in a bank.
review_cons: Performance is judged solely on customer survey opinions, which have many flaws. Customers with bad experiences with other departments or branches unfairly give negative survey feedback for transactions at my branch, making me the 'fall guy'. Management acknowledges survey flaws for frontline associates but offers no real solutions. Customers also give negative survey feedback for employees simply doing their job, such as asking for ID for transactions, even when following compliance rules. Management's advice on handling these situations is inconsistent and unhelpful, and HR seems unresponsive.

advice_to_management: Call centers are absurd but necessary. Be more cognizant of employees' mental health and daily responsibilities. Pay is egregiously low; denying monthly bonuses for minor infractions like returning late from a restroom break is unfair. Employees do not enjoy hokey incentives. Recording employees dealing with upset customers while playing 'Happy' is terrible. Coupons for soup after generating millions are insulting. Pay employees better, allow them to participate in appreciation days, be more flexible with PTO, and admit flaws in customer policies.
review_pros: The headquarters building in Richmond is very nice.
review_cons: Strict adherence to phone availability from log-on to log-off, with limited breaks and extended days for longer lunches. Supervisors with lower education critique calls based on absurd metrics, leading to forfeiture of monthly bonuses for minor errors. Monthly bonuses are easily forfeited for minor call failures, despite significant team contributions. Incentives like rubber bracelets and soup coupons are insulting. Employee appreciation days are not extended to call center staff, who are required to remain on phones. Lack of career development and vertical transition opportunities for claims, customer service, and sales departments. Mandatory alternating weekend and holiday work, with a lottery system for major holidays based on accrued points, often leading to working major holidays. Low hourly pay, with slightly higher rates only for undesirable shifts. Strict PTO accrual and usage rules, with limited availability due to staffing requirements. Dealing with disgruntled customers, often delivering bad news about policy coverage (e.g., to cancer patients), leading to emotionally draining and abusive calls. Management is aware of these issues but does not care, relying on a constant pool of high school graduates or underemployed bachelor's degree holders.

review_pros: Fast-paced dynamic company, great working environment, continued staff support during the Covid pandemic, great colleagues and leadership.
Nice office, 'can-do' attitude among staff, city-adjacent location, fulfilling work on a life-saving device.
review_cons: Limited utility and small customer base for the device, with no new version commercialized in a decade. Slow-moving organization, little attention to systemic problems. Upper management politics stall productive change. Bureaucratic and hierarchical environment stifles innovation and advancement for talented engineers and developers. Uncompetitive salaries compared to other tech companies in Pittsburgh.

summary: Fired after 2 weeks, signed on to a 'year long contract'.
review_pros: The team, interaction with cute kids.
review_cons: Management does not care about employees and is rude, showing favoritism. Was required to complete a police clearance, invest $200 in uniform jeans, and buy a watch, only to be fired without cause.

review_pros: Good pay and benefits.
review_cons: A large clique exists due to many employees having worked together previously. Little opportunity to introduce new perspectives. Some senior management are entrenched bullies, which is only discovered after joining.

summary: Started off great and then went downhill.
review_pros: Flexible schedule allowing multiple clock-ins/outs as long as hours are completed. Opportunity to style and get to know amazing customers. Fairly easy and fun job, enjoyable for those who like shopping.
review_cons: Zero stability; company is like a roller coaster. Constant inventory shortages due to poor merchandise management, with no changes despite recurring problems.
