Hours are not guaranteed and are cut during slow periods, including holidays. Lack of transparency despite claims. Feedback is welcomed but never implemented, negatively impacting customer experience. Inventory issues blamed on Covid, but other boutiques managed fine. No raises for two years until many stylists quit. Performance-based bonuses are negated by cut hours, making them feel insignificant. Qualified higher-ups are leaving, which is concerning. Public image and diversity/inclusion are prioritized, but employees for public campaigns are chosen based on skin color rather than merit, and D&I efforts do not include all ethnicities, body types, genders, religions, or experiences.

review_pros: Beautiful locations, fun and diverse coworkers.
review_cons: Upper management treats staff as disposable tools. Micromanagement makes excelling at one's job extremely difficult.

review_pros: Great product, especially in pharma, making a real difference in drug development. Decent commission plan.
review_cons: Awful, toxic management. Employees are put on performance plans and threatened with termination if they don't close deals within 2 months, despite a 4-12 month sales cycle. Management forces last-minute changes to payment terms or contract years. No company culture or collaborative environment. Lack of essential sales tools like Salesforce, ZoomInfo, or LinkedIn Sales Navigator, expecting reps to find contacts magically. Overall, a detrimental environment for mental health.

review_pros: Great pay and benefits, great coworkers, opportunity to serve military members, great work facilities, great culture.
review_cons: Long hours, including weekends and holidays. No backups, making it difficult to take time off. Promotion is contingent on finding a replacement. Lack of recognition. High management turnover. Decreasing early retirement benefits. Inability to retire even after 24 years of service.

review_pros: Steady paycheck with decent management.
review_cons: No career growth or significant upside.

review_pros: Capable international team in the UK and USA.
review_cons: Poor organization of local projects.

review_pros: The product has significant unreached potential. The people (excluding co-founders) are a positive aspect.
review_cons: Tech-bro energy without substance, no work-life balance, bare minimum benefits, salary, social connection, and inclusivity. Unnecessary emotional labor, distressed and confused coworkers, negative psychological safety, gaslighting, and retaliation.

summary: Most degrading company I ever worked for.
review_cons: Awful pay, no benefits. Owners and upper management are vile and create a toxic environment. Upper management is condescending and disrespectful. The company violates many laws and basic practices. Owners act like a Fortune 500 company despite being a strip mall tattoo shop.

review_pros: Great work-life balance, good benefits, and incredible co-workers.
review_cons: Lack of overall direction from C-suite and department leaders. Company-wide direction changes regularly. Unclear career path opportunities, with management avoiding discussions. Lack of diversity in leadership roles. Management uses "collaboration" as a guise to avoid accountability for decision-making.
The time spent here was not worthwhile, causing health issues due to a stressful job and high-pressure management.

review_pros: Challenging and demanding, pushing employees to their limits.
review_cons: A recent Bloomberg article about the CTO reflects a highly biased technology culture, unchecked by HR. Lack of transparency in remuneration and career opportunities.

summary: Great Work-Life Balance with lots of development opportunities.
advice_to_management: Review the evaluation system. Start asset teams. Rotate conference attendance among employees.
review_pros: Great work-life balance, great development opportunities, good environment, lots of data to work with.
review_cons: Low salaries, flawed evaluation system, limited conference attendance, and insufficient asset team work.

review_pros: Opportunity to work with different clients, enhancing knowledge.
review_cons: The company lacks growth, which hinders employee development. I was stuck in the same position for a long time.
Disorganized management. Promised an exit interview but never contacted me, and seems to be interfering with my unemployment application.

summary: Join only if you have no other offers.
review_pros: Some helpful colleagues.
review_cons: Untimely payment, no scope for learning, unavailability of hardware (sometimes requiring personal devices), 3-month notice period, and zero benefits (medical, learning, no canteen).

review_pros: Great products, strong ethics, fun gig.
review_cons: Cult-like upper management, making upward mobility difficult without being part of the 'in-group'.

review_pros: Best patients, fulfilling work.
review_cons: Awful management, the worst I have ever seen.

review_pros: Good pay and benefits. Helpful coworkers.
review_cons: No organized plan for success, only aspirational slides. Business units struggle for survival and are forced into opposing directions. Projects are often disrupted after significant work. Widespread denial of complexity. Disjointed, chaotic, and outdated tech stack. Crippling attrition of senior technical talent. Multiple engineering cultures are forced together, none of which are modern.

summary: Some good apples, but the roots are rotten.
review_pros: Some lovely employees try to maintain a positive attitude despite anxiety and management's blatant disregard for their health and well-being. Everyone is terrified.
review_cons: Outdated strategies for Alzheimer's caretakers, catering only to wealthy patients and caregivers. The company seems unaware of current health crises and safety concerns, managing as if it were 1999.

summary: Looks aren't always as they appear.
review_pros: Unlimited time off, generous salary.
review_cons: Training and onboarding process is inadequate. Policies are disorganized and mixed with other documents. No sense of 'family' culture.

review_pros: Always busy, so no sitting around. Opportunity to learn a lot. Amazing organizational mission. Good salary and benefits, with free to low health insurance.
review_cons: Very hard to achieve work-life balance; management and team contact employees during vacation or after hours, with everything needed 'ASAP'. Department, including leadership, engages in racist and sexist comments and gossip, with no action taken to stop it. Favoritism in all departments. Micromanagement. Ineffective management that takes credit for other employees' work. HR and leadership do not listen to complaints or reports, and nothing gets done.

review_pros: Good compensation, benefits, office location, and independence. Culture is changing under new ownership, but the executive team is out of touch and struggling to stay relevant.
review_cons: Disorganized management, unclear objectives, and attempts to fix multiple issues simultaneously. Management is more concerned with the perception of performance than delivering quality results.

summary: People managing the departments are ineffective.

Their personalized and often inefficient methods are detrimental, costing the company money. High employee turnover, with constant job postings, indicates a significant problem that management tolerates.
Everything is a con. Promises of advancement are lies.

company_name: Tax Law Office of David W Klasing
review_pros: Cannot think of anything good. While there is a nice office view, it doesn't compensate for hating the work environment.
review_cons: Worst managers, toxic environment.

review_cons: Management is constantly shifted, leading to no long-term planning. Schedules and project management are non-existent. Endless work pace makes work-life balance difficult. Too many politics in what should be a creative process. Projects are rushed to completion regardless of quality.

review_pros: Worked with fun people, enjoyed team building activities.
review_cons: Discretionary bonuses. Management does not reward loyalty. Problems are left to fester instead of being dealt with immediately. Non-existent structured training for new employees.

advice_to_management: Promotions should be based on qualifications, not other factors.

summary: Nice if you want to move, but there is a mental price tag.

review_pros: Assistance with documents for relocation, and potentially a nice view.

Employees are left to figure things out independently, with no support. The company only cares about performance numbers, treating employees as mere statistics, leading to judgment for underperformance. The company is disorganized in documentation, finance, and communication, with inconsistent treatment of employees. The job requires emotional detachment and a willingness to lie. Non-agents are assigned the lowest tasks. HR is unresponsive and unhelpful. Instead of hiring professionals, they promote agents and use higher management for micromanagement, especially in underperforming operational departments.

advice_to_management: Improve payroll.

summary: It's not as fancy as portrayed.

review_pros: Nice work environment and good senior colleagues.
Company pays its employees very well.

review_pros: Good for collecting a paycheck and slow career progression.

review_pros: Overall a flexible company with a lot of resources.

review_pros: Very nice place to work.

review_cons: Several individuals are extremely toxic, diminishing and ridiculing others. There is a lot of in-fighting and finger-pointing. Employees are blamed for problems created by upper management. There is little room for growth, and skills learned are not very transferable due to many home-brewed applications. The company does not value those who speak up or try to change things, nor does it value quality or hard work. Success seems to depend on being part of a social clique. The hiring process is deceptive, with over-promising in interviews. The place is riddled with problems.

review_pros: Better clothing, and nice customers.
review_cons: Betrayal of employees, always looking to find mistakes.

review_pros: Stable job, growth for some time, and overall better pay compared to other financial institutions.
review_cons: Very slow salary growth and fewer promotions. Office politics hinder promotion unless you are a management favorite or belong to the same race/nationality as the manager. Racial bias among managers prevents employee advancement.

advice_to_management: Focus on replacing impolite people with polite ones.
Being more flexible would be better.
review_pros: Always on time for everything.
review_cons: Demands way too much in multiple ways.

advice_to_management: Be more proactive and less reactive.
review_pros: Schedule was good, with morning or evening options.
review_cons: Constant changes to try and stop fraud.

advice_to_management: Go back to business basics and reduce internal politics. Pay more salary and less commission to reduce stress and sales manipulation practices.
review_pros: JPMorgan Chase offers excellent benefits and a great work/family life balance. The company provides a career path for employees and great commission opportunities.
review_cons: Hourly pay is low. Corporate red tape is pervasive, making it unsuitable for those who prefer freedom and flexibility. The company focuses more on political agendas than business agendas.

review_pros: Salary is paid on time.

advice_to_management: Keep up the good work.

advice_to_management: Keep up the great work.

summary: Do not work for them unless you hate yourself.

review_cons: Employees are asked to do more than they are paid for, simply because of their bosses' authority. Corporate does not care about employees, contrary to training videos. The job is mentally and physically draining.

advice_to_management: Need to reward innovation and candor, especially when not aligned with desired outcomes.
review_pros: Great company, varied roles are possible, and large assets.
review_cons: Upper management is not fully engaged in day-to-day operations.

summary: Avoid at all costs unless you like ripping people off.

Well-funded office with a good introduction to the job.
review_cons: No work-life balance. Employees are trained to lie to customers. Constant threats to job security. No store repair support for constant issues.

review_pros: Overnight auditing is great for those who can easily keep busy and prefer solitude. Mostly low stress.
review_cons: There was little to no leadership for new people.

review_pros: Employees learn every single thing about the domain-specific industry.
review_cons: If something goes wrong, you are usually the only person available to fix the problem. If management is not clued in on how to best handle situations, the stress falls to the employee.

summary: Absolutely horrible experience! Don't waste your time!
review_cons: The startup culture can be detrimental to personal life, with a very poor work-life balance. The organization operates more like a proprietary business than a company. One individual, Karan Chechi, has 100% authority to change rules and company policies at his convenience. Many cons were experienced over two years. Prospective employees should gather insights from former employees.

summary: Drink the Kool-Aid or leave; free thinkers need not apply.
review_pros: They pay on time.
review_cons: Toxic company culture and very disorganized. The CEO takes ownership for accounts won and closed deals he had no involvement in. The CEO does not care for employees' well-being or career progression, nor for religious holidays or personal lives, even attempting to make an employee work on Christmas.

review_pros: Offers good benefits and is a teaching hospital.
review_cons: The CEO is rarely heard from, except for weekly emails with little substance. Employees are encouraged to listen to the CEO's podcasts or appearances, creating a cult-like atmosphere. There is constant testing on everything. Management claims to care but daily actions show otherwise; employees are treated as KPI measurements.

summary: Probably the least enjoyable bank to work for.
review_pros: Recently increased holidays from 25 to 30 days for all staff.
review_cons: Toxic environment where staff are reluctant to train new nurses due to high turnover of new graduates, who leave due to inadequate training. Nurses do not specialize in the OR, leading to varied assignments. Management fails to hold surgeons accountable for abusive behavior. Management abuses staff, with frequent last-minute mandates and non-emergent cases booked as emergencies, leading to staff being called in often. Benefits are decent but not competitive, and pay is not competitive. The manager is hands-off and displays inappropriate conduct, especially when nurses resign, making comments like "staff don't leave their jobs, they leave their bosses." He made inappropriate personal comments to a nurse in an interracial relationship. He lies to staff about resignation requirements, claiming PTO won't be paid with less than three weeks' notice. He also gives negative performance reviews to prevent OR staff from transferring, limiting growth. Work-life balance is nonexistent; employees work five days a week with two days off, one of which involves 24-hour on-call duty requiring them to stay near the hospital (which is infested with large flying roaches). Call duty is required at least twice a month, and there are no consecutive days off.

review_pros: The school district is organized.
review_cons: Staff parking is a nightmare. It is almost impossible to improve one's mandate level unless a senior underwriter leaves. Training is dreadful. All systems are manual with little automation. There is a lot of gossip and clique behavior. Operations managers do little visible work. There is constant restructuring and low pay compared with competitors. The CEO is overly cheesy.

summary: Merged with TRPC Retirement Plan Company; employees are now treated as assets without job security.
review_pros: Good location, and holidays are observed whenever the stock market is closed.

review_pros: Regular hours and paycheck. Nice surroundings and convenient facilities. Great coworkers campus-wide, mostly great students and faculty. Lots of freedom and autonomy.

advice_to_management: Give more to employees.

Extreme nepotism and no job security. Uneven distribution of job responsibilities. The workplace has continually gone downhill.

review_pros: Fellow employees made this employer a bit bearable.
review_cons: Underpaid, underappreciated, and lack of communication.

review_pros: Diversity, global impact, and work-life balance.

summary: Great benefits for part-time workers too.

summary: Stay away from Siemens Government! Undervalued, underpaid, disorganized!

review_cons: Long hours; it takes about a week to adjust.

summary: There's tradition, and there's downright old-fashioned.

review_pros: On-time and good salary.

review_pros: None- terrible company. Leadership rules my fear

No value addition from juniors is accepted. Offices are outdated, as is the management's thought process. High packages for beginners are seen as a conspiracy to prevent them from finding comparative jobs. The CEO is skilled at recruiting needy people he can control. Quality standards of products are falling drastically.

summary: The right hand doesn't know what the left hand is doing.

review_pros: Great team, but communication is hard. On-site sushi chef.

review_pros: Discount on clothing. Great commission when hitting goals. Growth in the company for loyal employees. Close-knit relationships with coworkers.
review_cons: Sometimes requires working overtime on some projects.

The company is profit-minded but does not treat salespeople with the respect they deserve, despite them being the ones selling the product.

summary: Good place, but lots of headaches.
review_pros: Technical work is always impactful, interesting, and challenging.
review_cons: Toxic work environment managed by an emotionally unstable group of people who are also team leads and friends with each other.

advice_to_management: Allow employees to do their work without constant micromanagement and unrealistic expectations that cause salaried workers to work without pay. It is a horrible company.
review_pros: Work from home. Year-end bonus.
review_cons: Corporate obligations, political agendas, rotating director-level staff, consistent restructuring, and de-prioritization of trust-funded technical assistance activities take up too much time and can hinder staff's ability to do real work.

review_pros: Fantastic colleagues, a nice office in a great location, meaningful responsibility given quickly, and slick marketing and brand.
review_cons: Heavily micromanaged. Salaried employees have to work well over eight hours a day to complete their jobs.

review_pros: Some people are kind, knowledgeable, and genuine.
review_cons: The business is burdened by enormous tech debt, which the C-suite refuses to address due to a poor grasp of technical concepts and unwillingness to listen to experts, leading to consistent deprioritization of tech debt. There is high turnover in engineering leadership roles, and the company has been without a CTO for 1.5 years, a significant red flag for a tech company. The C-suite lacks experience scaling a company at this stage of growth, evident in the CEO's poor decisions, lack of accountability, and apparent incompetence. Freetrade's app has stagnated while competitors have advanced. The C-suite does not care about employees; during a recent redundancy process, they rescinded job offers, lied about the number of redundancies, and extended probations so redundant employees only received one week's notice. Leadership communication is poor, lacking empathy and failing to address concerns directly.

review_pros: Paycheck and benefits were incredible.
review_cons: Passive-aggressive behavior towards some employees; those deemed "out of the loop," not churchgoers, or vocal about employment law are targeted for redundancy during crises. For example, during COVID-19, ten people were made redundant eight weeks into the first lockdown despite furlough schemes. The culture is dead; it used to be about people and engineering excellence, not just money. The company claims high Christian principles to secure contracts but then dismisses staff unnecessarily, even when government funding could have retained them. This is abhorrent. The company has lost its way and is altogether toxic.

review_pros: Very corporate; if management likes you, you can pretty much do whatever you want.
review_cons: Starting at 9 AM and getting home between 8 PM and 11 PM every night does not allow for a schedule or family life.

review_pros: Junior employees are very talented and deserve better.
review_cons: This is a very toxic company. Management preaches core values and brand promises, which are propaganda to brainwash employees. There is a high employee turnover rate. Employees constantly feel inadequate.

summary: Great company to work for, especially as a first job in the workforce.
advice_to_management: Needs a more efficient way of implementing positive change.
review_pros: Very well-structured scheduling. Opportunity to move up. Ability to learn many skills transferable to other jobs.
review_cons: Problems with the President, COO, CEO, and Owner.

review_pros: It provided a paycheck for bills.
review_cons: Below-average pay for similar positions at other companies. Inefficiencies are rarely optimized.

summary: Growth at the beginning, then toxic and stressful afterwards.
review_pros: During the school year, mornings and Fridays off allow for errands. It is rewarding when students and families see the results of efforts and trust in achieving academic needs and goals. Most teachers are dedicated and friendly. Employees are in charge of their own center with business autonomy (as long as sales targets are met). It offers significant learning about running a business and is a great resume opportunity for growth in the education field.
review_cons: Lack of leadership and responsibility.

summary: P&G was great, but locations weren't for me.
review_pros: A company with history and reputation, well recognized, selective, and takes care of its employees. Great pay, especially for the mid-west. Perfect place for a business-oriented engineering project manager.
review_cons: Work on Saturdays. Extreme pressure from management when falling behind sales targets, leading to micromanagement by the District Manager. Salaried employees are asked to work beyond their job scope. Employees are expected to meet sales targets while managing current students' academic progress, which is too much for one person. Since the new CEO, millions have been spent on new software (Microsoft Workspace, Salesforce, Docusign), with questionable necessity, and new laptops are not connected to printers, requiring old laptops for printing. High turnover rate of education coordinators due to high demands and underpayment. No room for career growth if the center director stays for years.

employee_job_title: Digital and Technology Solutions Degree Apprentice
advice_to_management: Engage more with frontline employees.
review_pros: Good colleagues and plenty of career progression.
review_cons: Projects move slowly and change is incremental. Cincinnati location was not appealing.

advice_to_management: Address incompetent management.
review_pros: Some great people and perks.
review_cons: Senior management is slightly disconnected.

review_pros: Best works council in the country.
review_cons: Incompetent management and lack of strategy.

review_pros: Great relationships with employees and students. Pay is okay for entry-level, benefits are good.
Good salary.
review_cons: Overworked with long hours. Inexperienced people giving feedback to experienced teachers.

review_pros: Good work colleagues, though they often left.
review_cons: Zurich acquired QBE Seguros, ruining the work dynamic by laying off people and automating everything, destroying a 'unicorn' company.

advice_to_management: Wish the company hadn't closed down.
review_pros: Good pay rate for the work done. Good work-life balance, great supervisors and management, comfortable working environment, and helpful teams.
review_cons: The company fleeced employees by promising bonuses for promoting a loyalty program, then ignored requests for payment. Similar issues were common. Lone working was standard, with only one minimum wage staff member on desk and no support for angry guests. Managers were harassed with 24/7 on-call duties. High staff turnover indicates poor treatment of employees.

advice_to_management: Improve contact between internal teams.
review_pros: Enjoyed resolving payment issues.
review_cons: Rotation was overwhelming initially, but shift changes every two weeks became manageable.

advice_to_management: Differentiate jobs by actual responsibilities, not just generic titles. If job titles are used for pay bands, ensure they reflect actual work, rather than assuming all employees with the same title perform similar jobs.
review_pros: Great benefits, including paid time off and insurance. Standard office hours with only occasional requests for overtime.
review_cons: Non-payment issues due to billing problems.

advice_to_management: Reinforce policies and restart enterprise compliance hiring teams. Management is horrible.
review_pros: Decent benefits, vacation, and schedule.
review_cons: Salary schedules were based on job titles, which often didn't align with actual responsibilities. Despite periodic market adjustments, salaries lagged market rates.

summary: Lack of maker and checker system for daily decisions.
advice_to_management: The whistleblower policy is nominal. HR should intervene and decide on exception cases instead of following managers' decisions. There is no focus on employee growth or exposure; only upper management has been promoted in the last three years.
review_pros: Job security and balanced life if work is managed effectively.
review_cons: Employees are not treated well. Issues include management bias, retaliation for speaking up, and no repercussions for sabotaging or exposing other employees' personal information. The company is a mess.

advice_to_management: Be careful with the agency.
review_pros: Excellence in quality and customer perspectives.
review_cons: Immediate managers are too powerful. HR is ineffective. No exceptions are approved, even in critical situations. Managers exhibit a fearless, biased approach, as HR is uninformed.

advice_to_management: Do not allow people to stay in the same roles for over 20 years, as it prohibits change.
review_pros: Bonus targets were met every year.
review_cons: Staff is poorly valued.

summary: Tall poppies get cut down, and don't get Covid.
review_pros: Many great people in the company, but one bad apple can ruin the experience.
review_cons: Lack of growth, change, positive aspects, leadership, management support, structure, and opportunities.

advice_to_management: Listen to advisors, reduce numerous small daily tasks, and allow senior staff to focus on high-level work. Many good people left for this reason.
review_pros: Good company for advancement and growth.
review_cons: Toxic work environment in the department. One manager regularly talked over or shouted down employees. A culture of backstabbing and negative talk about employees behind their backs. Little to no concern for employee development; professional certifications were ignored. Employees were expected to stay in their lane. Suggestions for improvement or cost-saving ideas were met with denunciation. No leniency for Covid-related absences regarding arbitrary deadlines. DEI considerations were seen as leading to racism and sexism, with no appreciation for diversity of experience and ideas.

review_pros: Great concept and competitive salary compared to other retailers.
review_cons: Management is out of touch with branch-level operations. Sales goals are difficult to achieve due to unapproved credit deals and an overly complicated process.

summary: Used to be a great company.

review_pros: Great people, flexible work culture, and many new projects and learning opportunities in the past.
review_cons: Covid-19 halted future plans. The company was already undergoing drastic and difficult changes before the pandemic.

advice_to_management: Be respectful and patient.
review_pros: Opportunity to work with good team players.

summary: Relationship Manager, Operations Marketing, Bank Branded Cards.

review_pros: Decent pay and benefits, strong focus on training and corporate mobility, and professional handling of COVID-19.

summary: Felt let down by this company; it makes the motor trade look awesome by comparison.

review_pros: Hires the best employees for every position, fosters a great family-like bond, and work is executed effectively.

summary: Toxic leadership and inability to drive change.

Newer cubicles are smaller, with 15 cubicles resized to 19 in one aisle.

review_pros: Creative colleagues and opportunities for business travel.

review_pros: Great culture and awesome people to work with.

advice_to_management: Customer service should involve agents, supervisors, and managers working together to resolve customer issues and retain service, showing care for employees assisting customers. Supervisors are often unavailable to assist customers who request to speak with someone higher up, even with a large number of employees. This job is not recommended, even for high school graduates, as other entry-level positions offer better support and potentially higher pay with less stress. The company is a disgrace to customer service, offering only $14.50/hr for a stressful job with angry customers and minimal support.
review_pros: Work-from-home option.

review_pros: Teamwork and support system.

review_pros: Relief of clocking out at the end of the shift.

review_cons: Growing so fast, their training and regional teams struggle to have time to support on-site staffs, so communication is slow, and leadership goes to the fires; Also, there is lots of corporate reporting that takes time away from on-site work.

summary: Run away from this sinking ship.
review_pros: Flexible with time off. Work from home.
review_cons: Everything else. Management has no clue after the company was sold and restructured. The CEO is a know it all who clearly knows nothing of media production…and the employees are not happy.

review_pros: If you're ex-military or go to church with the boss, this is a great place to work.
review_cons: Otherwise, get ready for divisive political battles every day you go in to the office.  They'll snooker you in with high-falutin' talk about making it a great place to work and being there for their employees.  They'll cater a lunch every month and hold meetings where you can speak up and state your views.  But as time goes on, you'll start to realize that no one is listening.  And those meetings are starting to add up, and they're all supposed to be on green time.  And then you'll program will get cut by the government for no reason, and it'll be adios muchachos.

review_pros: Innovative Worldwide Talented Passion Drive

summary: A working nightmare because of regional management.
review_pros: PTO, flexibility, training, logo wear, and site teams.
review_cons: Horrible pay and benefits. Employees are always overworked, underpaid, and underappreciated. There is a lack of support from upper management.

advice_to_management: WDI should be driving new direction, not merely responding to Resort requests. Motivating factors need to be innovative experiences, not merchandising tie-ins.
review_pros: Freedom in the classroom to teach using your own style.
review_cons: New employees are hired at a higher wage than loyal, long-time employees.

summary: Amazing and fascinating experience that shaped my career.
review_pros: Creative atmosphere and deep Disney history. The product is happiness, and the environment reflects that, making it a great place to work. Multiple opportunities for advancement.
review_cons: Sporadic workload. Compensation could be more competitive.

summary: Great agency to learn and grow.
review_pros: The company cares about development; employees get an assigned dedicated mentor for personal and professional help. Taking time off was easy and encouraged, with a great approval process. Office life was great, transitioning to a flexible model for in-office or remote work. Great people and work-life balance; teams would lighten the load if someone was working too late. Opportunities to learn a lot about media relations and social media.
review_cons: Managing five clients was slightly too much; four would be ideal. Pay is average. Not many other cons.

review_pros: I learned a lot working at Meta and met many amazing people.
review_cons: Grossly underpaid and overworked. The company spends more time blaming and finger-pointing than actually resolving issues. They do not care about employees.

summary: Downward spiral from a great thing.
review_pros: Wonderful teammates who often become friends for life, sharing a 'PTSD' kind of bond. Benefits are fine. Significant learning opportunities, often due to understaffing, requiring junior team members to take on responsibilities above their title/pay grade.
review_cons: One side of the business is clearly prioritized in terms of support, vision, and growth, leaving 50% of the team without that experience. No work-life balance; at one point, I worked 250 hours a month. No one genuinely helps individuals grow towards career goals. The 'favorites game' is ridiculous; a small circle of people beloved by the Founder can do no wrong, even after serious complaints about bullying and lack of work, and still receive promotions. Harassment is not handled well; despite constant complaints, HR/leadership act unaware, suggesting poor communication or indifference. Abusive individuals still get promotions. Feedback is nonexistent. The Founder is frequently inappropriate, making moves on employees at social events, drunk dialing in the middle of the night to scream or cry, indicating instability. DEI work, though run by a wonderful person and committed team, is not integrated into daily work by leadership and is instead used for flashy award submissions.

review_pros: Good place to start a sales career. Organized company with the best sales tools available in the market.
review_cons: Bro culture and lots of social politics.

review_pros: Prior to October 2021, the company hired good-hearted people who worked hard, covering Election Day 2020, January 6, and the COVID story with limited resources. Coworkers were supportive, friendly, and talented journalists.
review_cons: Since relaunching, Newsy has made problematic hires, including individuals with questionable social media posts or minimal journalism experience. The company has not learned from its mistakes despite multiple relaunches over the last five years. Most employees are overworked, benefits were slimmed down in 2021, anchors are merely faces, and managers do not effectively manage. Internal politics have become nastier. Newsy no longer stands out, attempting to emulate others with purple graphics. The editorial direction has reverted to traditional journalism's bad habits, like 'getting both sides' and breathless live news coverage. Employees are uncertain about their standing with influential managers and are often sidelined or pigeonholed despite speaking up. During the relaunch, at least five directors were required to reapply for their existing jobs. Some reporters were automatically shifted to new teams, while others were told to move to Atlanta with little assistance or guarantees, or face termination. The company repeatedly makes the same mistakes.

review_pros: Opportunity to work with motivated students.
review_cons: Hours are not very flexible.

review_pros: Company is pushing for growth, top leadership transparency, same-level coworkers are nice and helpful, and remote working is available.
review_cons: Very heavy workloads, poor program management, low pay for hours worked, zero work-life balance, and little room for advancement. Clients can be abusive towards employees with little to no correction from program leadership. Benefits are not very good and expensive. Training is poor on all levels. Program leadership is selfish, manipulative, and demotivating. There are too many trackers. The company is badly understaffed, making vacations impossible as you return to an even worse situation due to lack of coverage.

review_pros: I am proud of all the experiences gained from the company, which will surely be useful in all aspects of my career.
review_cons: My efforts were never good enough for the owners, who were overly perfectionistic. While they wanted their business to thrive, their leadership style was micromanaging and lacked trust in their employees. It was not a pleasant experience because our best was never considered enough.

review_pros: The company truly cares about providing access to tools and programs for self-development. It offered a flexible work-from-home schedule pre-COVID (this may have changed).
review_cons: Once acquired, there were mandatory daily morning calls focused on selling insurance, which did not apply to the role. Parking was sometimes challenging, there was constant construction, and compensation was an issue.

review_pros: It was a paycheck, and employees were paid on time. There was some flexibility to work from home during COVID-19.
review_cons: Lack of leadership. Management is a joke.

review_pros: Good work-life balance, nice coworkers, and opportunities for proposal work.
review_cons: The company tends to forget about employees not in the main office. They promise opportunities to try new areas, but once your skills are identified, you become stuck. There is little chance for growth if you are not in the main office.

review_pros: Some great people work here, with an elite few earning a very comfortable living at a young age.
review_cons: The biggest problem is the unachievable target of 10 calls per day; the sales floor average is 5, resulting in an average commission percentage of 5%, which is far below the industry standard. My commission was 1%. This means you can sell extensively, but if you don't follow their rules, they pocket the money, and you get paid nothing. Secondly, it's a revolving door with high turnover. The one-week notice period for all employees indicates a lack of care from the company.
No work-life balance. Promotions are granted based on flattery, not merit. Salaries are pathetic.

summary: Good overall company, but bad benefits.
review_pros: Great training and support system.
review_cons: Very poor compensation plan for a big company.

review_pros: 1Path is a good place to gain quick experience. Employees are quickly immersed and learn to administer many systems, with ample assistance from senior positions. The company also paid for certificates, testing materials, and study courses. Overall, it was a good place to work for a certain period.
review_cons: The company is cheap. Every year, insurance costs increased while benefits decreased. Promotions and salaries were poorly handled; during COVID, promotions, merits, and bonuses were frozen. Despite being told 'cash is king,' the client base increased with huge clients, and new people were hired. Regardless of performance, promotions were capped at 10%, even for top-tier workers who covered on-call shifts or handled multiple projects. Expectations were unrealistic; if you showed effort, work would be piled on you, leading to burnout while others with lower numbers did less. A manager once berated me for not taking on a project, then dismissed it as 'just a joke' when I explained my workload.

summary: Lighting decor specialist, finding the right product to suit customers' needs with a positive attitude.
review_pros: I love working with customers, sharing my knowledge, and helping them understand important aspects of choosing specific lights, ensuring proper size, style, and function.
review_cons: Unfortunately, there was a store closure, which was surprising given what I thought was fairly good profit. I was also surprised by my own success in my first sales job.

advice_to_management: Employ people who know how to run a business.
review_pros: Working with really good people.
review_cons: It is not a well-run company.

advice_to_management: Believe in your people.
review_pros: Great company with many good people and a friendly atmosphere. It's a great place to begin your career, offering good entry-level positions, pay, and benefits.
review_cons: Poor communication from mid-level management. There is a lack of trust in mid-level management. It takes too long to make sound decisions, and there are too many meetings.

advice_to_management: Improve management skills; workers are humans, not robots.
review_pros: Very flexible schedules, diversity in the workplace, and a great atmosphere.
review_cons: The pay did not equal the workload. Management did not always allow growth and some managers had favorite employees.

advice_to_management: Fix work-life balance issues for sales people, as they are the heart of the company. Care more about them. Work on fixing the pay structure for smaller, less high-end locations.
review_pros: Good stable income, employee stock options at the four-year mark, and a super fun people and work environment.
review_cons: No work-life balance, zero weekends off, and work every major holiday. You can make several times more money selling other vehicle brands. Friends selling other car brands earn much more than Tesla Owner Advisors and work fewer hours.

review_cons: Bad place to work; people come and go due to the toxicity of managers and team leaders who are self-important and blame others for their own mistakes. summary: Good if you play the game.

You can learn about the industry from a non-siloed role, though this means doing work that should be distributed among multiple people. Benefits are affordable and decent, allowing specialist visits without much insurance fuss. Direct deposit is available.
review_cons: Very limited career progression; a role must be vacated for someone else to be considered. There is a long waiting period before making progress. Long hours, long days, and excessive micromanagement. The environment was not cohesive.

review_pros: Close-knit group that paid well.
review_cons: A tremendous amount of pressure to do more than a normal position.

advice_to_management: Be consistent in your instructions. Don't let go of those who built you up!
review_pros: Work from home (COVID-related).

Management is horrible, and they do not work with employees if issues arise, instead firing or demoting them without warning. Management is extremely fake. There are unrealistic expectations for the amount of pay, and overtime is not paid.

summary: Always keep an eye out for a new job.
advice_to_management: More kindness and empathy in how you treat your employees would allow you to retain them longer and reduce high turnover. Additionally, it would be beneficial if the same 'type of person' were not always entrusted with the lowest hierarchical positions.
review_pros: I loved my job, but hated my bosses. They are vicious, backstabbing, lying women who will throw you under the bus to make themselves look better and write you up for following their instructions to save face with the customer.
review_cons: No loyalty to hard-working employees. I worked there for 2.5 years and was laid off despite hundreds of similar jobs I could have been moved to. The layoff was due to the big boss not liking me, not productivity, which was well above metrics; it was a popularity contest.

summary: Company that needs to reinvent its management and adapt to modern times.
review_pros: Fixed salary slightly higher than elsewhere.
review_cons: No profit-sharing or incentive bonuses for 3 years. Non-existent management, more interested in results than employee well-being or actual customer satisfaction.

review_pros: Everything is amazing everyday all day

advice_to_management: Have HR review the performance management system.
review_pros: Very nice people to work with.
review_cons: The codebase is too legacy and broken. The general mood is a bit too corporate.

advice_to_management: Try to hire some of their contractors. They have many good, loyal, and hard-working individuals coming in and out of their doors whom they should consider more closely.
review_pros: Good company culture, compensation, and benefits.
review_cons: The performance management system has a forced distribution of ratings. Many managers are not held accountable for coaching and developing team members.

review_pros: People are great, and benefits are excellent. It offers a learning and growing experience. Lillian Maliahas was an excellent and intelligent supervisor.
Learning opportunities, benefits, and exposure to multiple cultures and geographies.
Teleperformance is a great platform to work.
Work-life balance is good.
review_cons: Salary may be a concern.

advice_to_management: Be mindful that not everyone learns in the same manner. While striving to be the best, it's necessary to be humble sometimes.
review_pros: Very diverse. Great benefits. Very good pay scale. 401k matching dollar for dollar.
review_cons: Some people do not know how to be team players or realize they are part of a team that needs to succeed together.

advice_to_management: Take a more personal interest in people and focus on retaining staff.
review_pros: Some of the people there are really nice.
review_cons: Management, environment, expectations, stress, and lack of empathy for employees' mental health are issues.

summary: It's an okay place to work.
advice_to_management: Hold people accountable for their work and create a common set of working conditions across the organization.
review_pros: Great people to work with, and most areas are fun.
review_cons: Workloads are not evenly balanced, and there is much duplication.

advice_to_management: Embrace proven methods for project management, business management, business analysis, and general day-to-day problem-solving. Coach people in these ways so that skills are transferable, and everyone is ultimately uplifted.
review_pros: Lots of opportunities, the company cares about its people and culture, and there are many resources available for consultation.
review_cons: Frequent restructures lead to a loss of corporate knowledge and intelligence, forcing a restart from square one. In projects, there's a strong focus on meeting deadlines no matter what, but a lack of structured approach with proven techniques, sometimes favoring a 'whatever it takes' attitude over a measured approach.

summary: Constant flow of calls every day.
advice_to_management: Give minorities and women a chance. It seems like only white men are perceived as good at or excelling in IT. Yelling at employees does not improve their performance.
review_pros: Benefits are probably the only pro.
review_cons: Rotating schedules, no downtime, open 24/7/365, and low pay for the amount of work.

advice_to_management: Stop micromanaging employees and making them feel their best isn't good enough. Understand that coworkers who lack work-life balance cannot perform at their best. For Florida stores or stores that closed at 2 p.m. on Saturday, hours were extended, but you are paying employees to do nothing because few customers are in stores. If the bank is open 5 days a week from 8 to 6, why stay open on Saturday until 4? Learn from other banks; they barely have customers at this time. Learn from Europe, where people conduct business early in the weekday, and their businesses are not negatively impacted.
review_pros: The benefit package is great. There are many hardworking and skilled people who work there. You meet all kinds of people from every walk of life.
review_cons: The bank is becoming a retail store, more concerned with sales goals than its customers. They disregard forcing old accounts into new ones by changing maintenance qualifications. Banking hours are unrealistic, as processing closes at 4 p.m., leaving workers little time for work-life balance. Sales goals are unrealistic; not all products suit everyone. For example, an 82-year-old customer who prefers in-person banking and has no internet knowledge will not use online banking, and it makes no sense to pressure them. If you don't meet prior week's goals, expect an extra early meeting to explain why, followed by a full shift. Retail and banking are different; customers don't need so many accounts. Wells Fargo has removed the joy of going to work with a smile, leading to daily overwork, stress, and job insecurity.

review_pros: Job security, career growth, and team-involved work.
review_cons: Long hours, limited paid time off, and intense labor.

advice_to_management: Pay more.
review_pros: Decent atmosphere.
review_cons: Money is too low to live.

advice_to_management: Give employees more formal training on key technologies.
review_pros: Great people, meaningful projects, and the ability to learn new skills.
review_cons: Very long hours and not much formal training.

advice_to_management: Pay attention to those moving up the ladder who require management and collaboration training. Some, having 'grown up Google,' believe their way is the only way, which is highly unfortunate. With the right training, this could be avoided.
review_pros: Some of the most intelligent and creative people I have ever worked with in my career.
review_cons: Some of the most emotionally unintelligent people I have ever experienced. While I am very familiar with Agile environments, having a boss incapable of articulating goals without losing emotional disposition is unfortunately a norm here, even in the late 2000s.

advice_to_management: Increase pay
review_pros: Not difficult unless you're getting rushed. Employee discount. Nice co-workers.
review_cons: Low pay. Gets pretty boring at times.

review_pros: Great people, who are smart, friendly, and helpful. Great flexibility and work-life balance.
review_cons: Does not appreciate its staff. Poor remuneration and career progression. Poor management of projects.

advice_to_management: Too big to listen. Too rich to care. Too absorbed to remember.
review_pros: Work/family friendly schedule, great benefits, great pay, generally good people to work with.
review_cons: Overwhelming number of meetings to discuss other meetings about the process by which to proceed with other meetings. Very little real "desk-time" to get anything done. Volatile E&P business doesn't add to long-term hope for a job.

advice_to_management: Better planning
review_pros: Great pay, insurance, comfortable environment!
review_cons: Long work hours and disorganization.

advice_to_management: You must like to live under pressure; it is a quarter-basis results company.
review_pros: Integrity, training, inspiring leaders, technology, on-the-job training.
review_cons: Many acquisitions and as a consequence, an imbalance of employee numbers. Different cultures at the same time. Matrix structure, long decision processes.

advice_to_management: Follow the OSHA guidelines. Give people their 30-minute lunches. When equipment is broken, do not continue using it. Listen to your staff and their complaints. Buy something other than pizza to thank your employees.
review_pros: No pros unless you like working intense, elongated hours with minuscule yearly raises, being given full-time responsibilities at part-time pay.
review_cons: Safety comes last. Employees worked past exhaustion. Not OSHA compliant. Favoritism amongst employees to management. Union is bought out. Insanely extensive hours (mandatory, no notification 24 hours in advance).

advice_to_management: Good luck.
review_pros: You feel cool wearing a Tesla logo.
review_cons: Working at Tesla was a nightmare; the stress was insurmountable. It was a chaotic, hostile environment. Every quarter they were changing our compensation or job requirements. A lot of things didn't seem legal as it pertained to how they were changing things. The most incompetent employees were promoted, especially to director. This then had a domino effect on the rest of the employees. Most of the time, no one knew what was going on, and the worst part of it was how the customers were being handled with gross neglect. As a female employee, it was very apparent that women were not respected in the workplace and were not given the same opportunities as their male counterparts. Overall, Tesla seems like a cool company that's trying to change the world, but the reality is it is a giant, chaotic mess. One bad decision after another.

They will give you a big hike and then cannot onboard you. Two days before joining, they usually say they cannot afford you. Before that, they will ask for all documents, resignation acceptance emails, etc. The HR department is full of uneducated and fake people.

summary: Unfortunate Waste of 6 of my Best Career Years
advice_to_management: A little fairness goes a long way.
review_pros: The money was good for the area.
review_cons: No matter how hard you work, if you're not one of the "chosen ones," you probably will not get the training needed to advance. To me, it seemed corrupt, far too competitive, and strategically controlled to benefit very few carefully chosen individuals. Many others were unhappy but wouldn't dare say so for fear of retaliation, despite the "most ethical company" reputation they have somehow "earned."

review_pros: Good number of clients, hence they ensure no employee stays on the bench for long.
review_cons: Shifting from one project to another becomes tough at times.

advice_to_management: Stop going through employees periodically and try to keep us.
review_pros: The company offers great benefits and scheduling.
review_cons: Even if you are considered a good employee, you could be laid off due to the irresponsible spending of Tesla.

company_name: The Denver Center for the Performing Arts
review_pros: The art and creative environment.
review_cons: The culture can be toxic. Management plays favorites, and if you’re on the wrong side of those favorites, good luck. Favoritism has nothing to do with merit. Working harder will not help. They think you should feel lucky to work there instead of the company feeling lucky to have talented employees. We learned that everyone is expendable and they don’t actually care about their employees. I left on my own; I was not let go.

review_pros: Paid sick time, easygoing management.
review_cons: Extremely unprofessional workplace, poorly operated. Horrible training, extremely gross working conditions, insanely unsafe working conditions. Does not provide proper tools or equipment.

review_pros: Excellent pay, healthcare, and benefits to US standard.
review_cons: Culture is disjointed after being built through global acquisition. Limited communication between TMT and the organization, plus no clearly articulated internal values (only external brand promise of "excellence") means no guiding principles for management across a global organization. Identity politics are quite standard, but after 1.5 years of leadership changes and key personnel changes (some of which may not have been for the better), it will be interesting to see how this company evolves.

review_pros: Work-life balance is decent.
review_cons: Poor executive leadership and lack of consistent direction.

summary: I'd rather stick my foot in a blender and eat a foot smoothie for lunch than go back to XLN
review_pros: The people. I managed to meet some amazing people (who have now all left!) and we are still friends.
review_cons: A normal day in my job at XLN would start with management screaming at you to get at least 25 card machines working when yesterday the sales team only managed to sell 5. Where they expected us to pluck the other 20 from, I don't know. We would then work very hard to get 5 or sometimes even magically get up to 7 from last-minute deals. Then the manager would throw their phone across the office in a tantrum. Managers were also seen to stomp feet, bash computer mice against their table, and order very expensive perfume/jewelry to the office just to show everybody what they bought. On several occasions, managers were heard telling staff members that "they did not understand" (that 20 card machines had to be 'made live') because English was not their first language, and they should be "grateful this country accepted you." Along with this came promises of raises which never materialized and bogus incentives, e.g., "Sell 5 items and you get to go home early" (this email would be sent at about 5:30 pm). We would also be made to feel guilty if we left on time and did not come in early. My brief 6-month stint at XLN has given me and my therapist a lot to get through.

summary: If you want to lose your work ethics and integrity, this is the place!
review_pros: Camin Cargo Control has competitive pay.
review_cons: Hostile environment. Unsafe work practices. Violations of regulations and procedures at every level. Falsifying of results and records. A thorough audit by a client will find that the "flagship" lab will give you results on instruments they don’t possess. They will strong-arm you to comply with their unethical practices. I received very poor training, and most tests everyone would just write down a result and say "that it’s going to be." It wasn’t until I went to a competitor that I saw how little training I had and how disgustingly unethical Camin Cargo was/is.

summary: Great Job, Great Atmosphere, But Job Security Isn't Great
review_pros: Competitive salary, unlimited PTO, fantastic working environment.
review_cons: Private equity running media always leads to sudden layoffs. I was one of almost 30 that was culled out of nowhere, a couple months after they shuttered another magazine. They acquired a new magazine on the same day of the massive layoffs. Feels like a game of fiscal hot potato. Take the job, cash the check, and gain the experience, but don't expect security, especially after the company gets a massive buy-in and they need to "streamline" to look good to investors.

summary: A company that hires incredible people
review_pros: Some of the best people I've ever worked with. Everyone cares about their work and always strives to improve processes and share learnings. The team is highly collaborative, talented, and always willing to listen. There is a great sense of teamwork even after moving to remote work. There is an opportunity to grow and explore new roles if you voice your interest. Management is very supportive and does its best to ensure you continue to grow your career. I've seen many older reviews about bad management and micromanaging, but the investment into management the past couple years has turned it all around. There are incredible, hard-working, and smart managers who truly care about individuals on their team. Every company has rough patches, and Eventmobi certainly has had some, but if you give feedback, there are always people ready to discuss and work with you to solve the problem.
review_cons: Things move quickly, and you need to be able to adapt. It can be good but might not be for everyone. Difficulties growing with legacy tech. This has gotten better during my time here, but it can lead to some barriers and compromises with the product.

summary: Company that believes in growing together with its employees
advice_to_management: Processes should be dealt with case by case when possible.
review_pros: Working on the latest and greatest tech stack. Plenty of opportunities to learn new things. Best team culture to work with. Big company giving you the opportunity to move around different teams from time to time, helping you take on different challenges and grow. Best work-life balance. Opportunity to pursue different career paths if you are interested.
review_cons: Decent but not great salary package. Too many layers of processes which sometimes gives you limited freedom, but CBA, being a top-tier bank, needs to have such processes which can safeguard us and customers against damages.

advice_to_management: You are oblivious to the big picture.
review_pros: Competitive salary, good benefits, 50% discount on wireless products & service.
review_cons: Only interested in promoting those under 50. Promotions are not regularly based on merit. Outsourcing or eliminating many jobs for experienced workers. Extremely interested in millennial customers and employees only.

summary: Lots of resources, little respect for individuals' contributions
advice_to_management: Google has had a lot of growing pains into a large company, and that's completely understandable. It is a big company, and a great big company to work for if the work and culture match your preferences. However, weighing technical interviews heavily on the backs of interns looking to make a difference for their teams and the company is detrimental to morale and contributions. The SWE program is still a great opportunity for interns, but Google itself would have a lot to gain if this dogmatic aspect of the culture and process was fixed.
review_pros: 1. Lots of resources: computing, educational, culinary, locational, people, financial, etc. You get paid pretty well, even as a SWE or EP intern. 2. Lots of high-quality technical infrastructure that you will gasp at if you've spent enough time developing on standard/enterprise software. 3. High-quality technical process and an appreciation for best practices. 4. Amazing networking opportunities. Google interns come from and end up all over the place, so being an intern is a great opportunity to meet lots of different people.
review_cons: 1. Lack of real respect for individuals. This is a symptom of Google being a large company with incredible scope to pick and choose people, along with having many excellent people. You are treated well at the surface level because you were able to get through the interviews and meet the obligatory standards, but you are not treated as an individual outside of your team's breadth. In addition, while you don't see rank explicitly pulled very often, the company is very top-down in policy and culture. This is common in large companies across the world. It's certainly easier to ignore at Google than other places, and you are pampered in a number of ways. If you want that sort of gritty individual respect from the organization, work at a small company. 2. Intern-specific: for full-time conversion, interviews can overshadow contributions to individual host teams. Interns are often left with the decision of maximizing their acquisition of gainful skills and their contributions to the company, or passing up on an opportunity for full-time employment at Google. Interview skills in tech are, at higher levels, focused on things that may not be application-specific for the intern's project, even if they are working on something of significant business value. This is backwards; I can count on one hand the number of tech companies I've heard of that still do this.

employee_location: Cobham, Surrey, South East England, England
advice_to_management: Stop blaming and start taking responsibility.
review_pros: Reasonable work-life balance; no expectation of weekends or evening work. Plenty of VPs/Ds who do very little if that's what you want.
review_cons: I have never worked anywhere where so many PowerPoint slides can result in so few actual decisions. Systems are unfit for purpose. Obsession with top-down planning and 'messaging' to senior management. Focus on 'getting stuff done' so the right message can be given to senior management, with little regard to 'getting it right'. Prevailing blame culture means middle management spends more time avoiding responsibility than actually adding value. Bizarre role-based promotion process effectively blocks progression until your boss leaves, and leads to large numbers of AVPs applying internally for VP roles they have no qualifications for.

summary: Not the first line company founded by Colonel Lilly anymore
advice_to_management: Cut the VPs by 75% and hire back the 5000 foot soldiers you stupidly discarded in 2010.
review_pros: Deep pockets; not afraid of using new technologies.
review_cons: Lost the core respect for the "People Element" they used to espouse so much. They throw away skilled people like used diapers just to save a few dollars that are then funneled to yet another empty suit VP.

summary: Gain experience for a year or two and then move on
review_pros: Made some great friends. Starting salary is okay if you ask for it, depending on the department. Somewhat flexible. Mostly easygoing with mistakes. Okay to gain some experience for a year or two but then move on; the role does not lead anywhere.
review_cons: Pay increase is minimal/non-existent. Promotions usually lead to termination/being forced out. Benefits are not great; no RRSP/Pension/Vision/etc. Some people have better benefits than others. Too many cooks in the kitchen; 3 owners and about 10 managers/VPs/leads to manage 25 product creators. Process is way too complicated for the scale and type of business. Too many ongoing projects and no organization to help manage; insane workload, even for the fashion industry. Toxic environment, no transparency, gossiping starts from management and trickles down. Teams throw each other under the bus. Some sales people scream/swear/slam doors/send rude emails. One woman micromanages and harasses junior staff like crazy, and owners are aware but do not take action. Office stayed open for most of the pandemic; WFH meant a 50% salary cut. Not an equal opportunity employer. Favoritism and opinions based on appearance, ethnicity, religious groups, etc. Has treated some pregnant women very unfairly.

review_pros: Nice people, low pressure. Great pension and work-life balance.
review_cons: Too heavy with non-technical people. Some very low-caliber people who know how to pass civil service interviews rather than know how to do their job.

review_pros: Great work-life balance. Flexible work arrangements and a great way to advance your career.
review_cons: Lack of direction from senior leadership.

review_pros: They are a well-established clinic system, so your job will always be secure.
review_cons: They don’t have a lot of opportunities for growth.

summary: Corporate, safe, but it’s all about the partners
review_pros: Café and canteen social spaces, subsidized food and drink. Nice teammates and big main office with lots of natural light. Campus convenience.
review_cons: Projects working with unethical clients and countries with poor human rights records for company and partner profits. Variety of projects can feel repetitive. Partners make decisions and show little care for less senior staff. Little remote working flexibility.

review_pros: Higher pay than any other district in the state (besides perhaps Park City). Low expectations. Teacher's union fights for you.
review_cons: Will most likely teach in some of the roughest neighborhoods. Bad behavior (fighting, drugs, etc.). High gang violence. Too many outside-of-school hours required.

review_pros: Good environment and support.
review_cons: Not great money, cannot progress if part-time.

summary: Dedicated employees, performative executive leadership with little integrity.
review_pros: Employees work here because they care and want to work hard. There’s a reason Agrace has a solid reputation, and it’s because of the hundreds of patient-facing employees who give so much of themselves.
review_cons: Patient-facing roles have little to no work-life balance, and those who attempt to establish work-life boundaries are generally punished and/or regarded as not being a team player. There are many performative aspects of this company in an attempt to pander to the masses around diversity and inclusivity, but this company is grossly out of touch with what that means.

advice_to_management: Don't just say "well, pick up the phone and dial more." Actually find a process of what works by trial and error and implement it.
review_pros: Great compensation. Great portfolio product suite. I love my coworkers.
review_cons: Management has no clue how to lead.

summary: Not a place for recent grads!
review_pros: Other than the laid-back office environment, not many pros come to mind if you don't already have a book of business when hired. You're competing daily against other office members for the same business accounts.
review_cons: Cold calls up to 200 a day, micromanaged by multiple upper management employees. Upper management does nothing to help gain business except for telling you to "hit the phones." Overall, I was lied to from the start of their 1-week training program.

review_pros: The county is overall a good and enjoyable place to work; my rating reflects what it's like working in IT. One positive note is there have been some strides in user education around security.
review_cons: The IT department is plagued by innumerable issues that are mostly self-inflicted. Number one is management, a team which has undergone a complete changeout over the last few years. One of the last competent managers, a calm voice of reason, retired early out of frustration with the new leadership. There's been a tonal shift in department culture. Morale is low, and the department's panicky management style encourages finger-pointing and contention among the staff. There are unclear standards around change management and how to work with other teams within the department that never get addressed. Everyone is on edge, and there are repeat offenders who will belittle and disparage other staff in meetings. They had a business psychologist come in a couple years ago to interview the staff in small groups. Feedback from the staff was almost unanimous, but it seemed like the doctor’s report and suggestions were thrown out since it was critical of the director and deputy director. The department is stuck in gridlock. There are a lot of half-baked processes, and then new processes and new committees to figure out why the processes aren’t working. Have a question? Your direct manager will either be unavailable or won’t know how to help you because they’re unsure of the direction the director wants them to take, or they’ll point you to another manager who’s just as unconfident. County staff have become unhappy with IT and aren’t shy about sharing that with you when you work with them. I can’t blame them because it’s hard to get help or work just isn’t being done. There’s a process for what projects you’re supposed to work on, but management will quickly shoehorn a different #1 priority project/issue onto your plate, usually weekly, or anytime there’s a fire caused by their poor planning or some highly visible problem that makes the department look bad. Your head will be spinning trying to track your time in tickets, a time log, your Outlook calendar, and your time card (while making sure it’s properly coded to the right work order).

Everything: micromanagement, bad work-life balance, no shift allowance, no leave encashment, no smooth existing process. Variable payment always delayed. No standard process for employees and HR. Employee policy changes overnight as per company convenience. Only active on LinkedIn.

review_pros: Great people that deserved better.
review_cons: What kind of real estate company shuts its doors in the middle of the greatest real estate market boom of all time? One that is led by incompetent leaders. REX failed because of the incompetence and arrogance of its CEO. He listened to the advice of people who knew nothing about real estate. Those people single-handedly began the destruction of REX with their ill-informed ideas, which included the disastrous concept of "whole REX." The CEO didn't listen to the right people. He didn't listen to the agents that worked in the field. He often said REX is a tech company, not a real estate company. A tech company whose website was a joke and whose app never worked properly. He failed this company but most importantly failed the good men and women who worked for REX. On Friday, REX laid off the majority of the remaining staff with no severance pay. A slap in the face with no disregard for their livelihoods. FAIL! The CEO had the nerve to dangle a carrot of a possible IPO. Lies.
Very negative work environment.

summary: Simply Put - A Bad Place to Work
review_pros: Pay is good. Some people seem to care. Benefits are okay.
review_cons: It's hard to pinpoint how bad things are with dysfunction across every level. Ineptitude thrives at an already sinking company. In the absence of technology, processes, and any form of standards, they solely rely on heroics. Unless you are a banner-level client, you'll get little attention or support. A lack of upward mobility (unless you are skilled at pandering to C-suite), preferential treatment, and unheralded promotions at the whims of management. Unfathomable amounts of broken promises and platform flaws with the only explanation, if you can call it that, from management is a cop-out like "things are tough" or some other tired cliché. Management is either so invested in creating and selling a façade that things aren't bad, broken, or failing (we're a happy family, they say!) that they actually believe it, or more alarmingly, don't see the reality. Tech is broken or unstable, competitors are seizing market share and new logos, turnover across departments is crippling, and morale just sinks lower. Yet upper management will force a smile and say "things are fine" with better fortunes just around the corner. How long you believe the latter part is what you'll wrestle with before seeing the only logical choice, which is to exit a company decaying from the inside.

review_pros: Great group of employees (which is honestly the main reason a lot of people stay). Good benefits, great pro-deal access.
review_cons: Management seems really cool and understanding at first, but they have their favorites, and it shows. If you're not one of the chosen ones, you'll know it too. They say feedback is always welcome and it's a "gift" and "not personal"; however, they will use it against you when it suits them.

summary: Save your life, Abort! Abort! Do not join even if it is your last option
review_pros: You get a job easily here.
review_cons: 1. They micromanage excessively. You are questioned if you are idle from CRM for even a single minute. 2. They deduct half-day salary if metrics are not met on a daily basis. The manager doesn't even approve attendance if metrics are not met, and you will be marked absent. 3. No consideration for you as a resource. They think you are robots who can work 24/7. All they care about is your revenue. 4. Your job is safe until the day you are getting revenue. Once that stops, you are fired. I have seen top performers of one quarter getting fired in the next one as their revenue dried up. In short, there is no consideration for your past contributions. All they care for is how much revenue you are getting today. 5. Even the CEO and other founders only talk about revenue figures in the organization-wide town halls. 6. You may have to work all 7 days of the week at times, and if you luckily get 1 day off, you will be pressurized to get more revenue on DMs on your day off. 7. The HR in the organization is just there to hire and fire people. They don't believe in building a good culture.

review_pros: A great company to work for!
Compensation is above market (the only pro).
review_cons: CEO is obnoxious and loves to hear the sound of her own voice. Most toxic work environment I have ever experienced. Laid off 30% of the workforce in June and are now hiring again.

summary: Be careful, it's all smoke and mirrors.
review_pros: Red carpet treatment during training, company trips, and parties. Generally, the staff in the office seem very nice.
review_cons: Many undelivered promises from top-down to employees and customers. Sometimes it seems like a blatant lie. Inconsistent with commission pay and ability to track. Danny and Jack are narcissistic liars. At company parties, the CEO has ridiculous requests and almost always crosses the line. Some staff don't understand personal space or the idea of what professionalism is. Lots of sexist and racist comments thrown around; it's surprising how they get away with it.

review_pros: The people I worked with were great.
review_cons: The people I worked for were bullies and awful.

review_pros: The employees are very nice, but very afraid.
review_cons: High turnover, condescending owner who views employees in a very poor light. I personally witnessed an employee being badgered until tears were produced. The owner took pride in breaking this individual's spirit, which was very sad. Horrible management; no organization; just not a good place to work.

review_pros: The opportunity for advancement in the company.
review_cons: Upper management chooses those who get promoted based on who they like, not experience or achievements. Everything is extremely disorganized. Upper management has too much power, which goes unchecked, allowing employees to be treated unfairly.

review_pros: I worked for Fahlgren Mortine for about 1.5 years, and it was an exceptional experience. There are many talented people that I was able to work alongside and learn from. Management is open to receiving feedback, supportive, and creative. The teams approach new projects with fresh ideas, making it exciting to be involved and, in turn, being proud to be a part of the team. There are many opportunities to get to know other team members (across a large company) because they are sure to do monthly activities, and they did a great job boosting company morale even during a pandemic!
review_cons: The structure of the company is very different. I think that causes some confusion in the different roles and departments.

review_pros: Connecting with new people, discount, great health insurance.
review_cons: It took nearly my entire time with the company (+3 years) to realize that the poor, unfair, and unethical treatment of people, myself included, is part of what led to my incredibly high stress levels that then led to an autoimmune diagnosis and the obvious need for the health insurance they provided (which we pay for, it is not paid for by the company). It makes better sense to put my mental health first, which means leaving the company, and then theoretically there is less of a desperate need and want for great health insurance. They thrive on knowing that people mainly choose to stay after all the injustice just for the health insurance. They thrive on keeping people unwell. Scott Nash gives power away to the most mediocre people, and they take the power to their head and absolutely hold it over other people. Scott Nash cares about profit over people and planet. His greed for money and power is evident as the company continues to grow while staffing becomes less and less. I have met few really great people here, and they either quit or get fired unfairly, or are threatened off the clock so nothing can be done about it with HR. "If you can't beat them, join them" only works if you have numbers because there is power in numbers. Do not think for a single second that you alone will change anything about how this place is run from the top down.
