To begin, the company needs a new owner, and nearly all of management (lower and upper) needs to be replaced. That won't happen. Management is literally the worst I've ever experienced, and this comes from someone who has worked in and with the public for greater than 12 years in a variety of retail spaces. The mediocrity of people they put in management will leave you laughing because it is very much a joke. Managers who bully, harass, or are totally absent-minded. Brainless. Heartless. Lacking compassion. Self-serving. Ego addicts. Drug and sex addicts, while on the clock! Managers who work in pajamas and rags for clothes, people who downright smell foul and need to shower; what a way to set an example of self-respect, but yet they expect us to respect them. Management does not live up to, nor do they set good examples for, the company values. All that while being encouraged to speak your truth, and you dare risk your job if you do! Only expect HR to protect your job if it is convenient for them to do so. Otherwise, expect to get fired. The company knowingly employs thieves and sexual predators, harassers, and offenders, and less and less cares about the employees at the bottom level, which is evident given the lack of security cameras, because the owner Scott does not believe in using them. So basically, you work at your own risk of being thieved from or worse, sexually, emotionally, and psychologically abused. So much abuse of power. So much abuse against humanity. It's literally the same stuff you see in the media but on a smaller scale and at a local level. Not a "green" job for you if you truly care about the environment; it will only make you mad and stress you out to be so exposed to people who actually do not care, workers and customers both. I didn't even mention the amount of financial waste that happens here, mostly due to pure human error, yet you are told there is no money for wage increases (but yet they keep opening new stores with little to no team to even recruit). It doesn't matter if you have a family to support or if you are single-handedly keeping plastic bags from becoming trash by turning them into new, repurposed goods. It's not worth sacrificing your mental health. If you truly care about the environment, work for a different company. It's easier to know from the start you are working with people who openly don't care versus working with people who pretend to when it's convenient for them. MOM's is the most dishonest place I have ever and will ever be employed.

review_pros: Nice co-workers within my dev team; others at my level were happy to take time to acclimate me to tools and software, sharing of knowledge.
review_cons: Everything else. Onboarding was an onslaught; management was pushing hard for releases of many features at once versus focusing more than one dev on a feature at a time. As a Junior, I was told to ask lots of questions and lean into any process or learning, which we all did, and that would be enough. I kept meeting with managers to get feedback but basically got "do better." Eventually, my manager stepped down, so the team was disorganized at the end. I was let go in the first wave of cuts they did; the explanation was that I did not have enough technical knowledge to work there. But I had asked for help and direction for over 3 months and passed their technical exam during interviews no problem. Underpaid for the role per average rates, benefit package was below average, no long-term benefits (RRSP matching, etc.); instead, there is a stock option plan that you receive over a 4-year period at a time.

review_pros: None, meaningless work for money-hungry people.
review_cons: Avoid working for this company; the culture is poisonous.

summary: The whole hierarchy needs to be reallocated.
review_pros: High salary, startup experience, and support for self-learning.
review_cons: Time scheduling, work hierarchy management.

review_pros: Team members are great.
review_cons: The CEO is a nightmare, paranoid about trade secrets and employees not working hard enough, despite never arriving before 11 AM. The environment is beyond toxic; leaving felt like escaping an abusive relationship. The CEO overpays because it's the only way employees stay. There is no process or rationale for decisions, leading to no accountability. Favoritism among employees is real and changes with the CEO's mood. Avoid working here.

review_pros: Office premises (SDB1 and SDB2) are nice. The canteen is good, and snacks are better than meals.
review_cons: I worked at Birlasoft for two years due to a bond. As a fresher, I had no idea what project or technology I would get and was excited to code, but they had no good development projects. I was assigned to a maintenance project with shifts and non-technical work. The manager was unprofessional. HR was unhelpful, and senior colleagues did not listen. My notice period was three months, and HR initially denied serving notice and bond together, but I later learned it was possible. I eventually left without a job during COVID-19 but fortunately found a much better one. My friends are still stuck in ticket allocation and monitoring work.

advice_to_management: Do better.
review_cons: Favoritism and sexual harassment are rampant. Bonuses are never followed through. As a former employee, I was promised a $500 bonus for staying until September 1st. I was let go on September 5th and denied the bonus because I was not employed on the payout date. Multiple attempts to contact HR via voicemails were never returned.

summary: Interesting work, great colleagues, terrific developmental opportunities.
advice_to_management: Senior management attempts to shift the culture by bringing in outside talent but fails to understand that most cultural problems stem from executive leadership.
review_pros: Industry leader, opportunity to build long-term relationships with dealers and customers, global career opportunities, developmental opportunities.
review_cons: Very conservative culture; creativity and risk-taking are not valued.

advice_to_management: Management should improve, especially regarding time management for clocking in on time for shifts.
review_pros: Good experience for fast-paced work and a great job for young teens to develop skills.
review_cons: Managers and customers can get cranky when not satisfied.

review_pros: Great office activities to build culture, good coworkers, helpful CPEs.
review_cons: Poor management, too many people working with seven or more managers.

review_pros: 40% discount, 25% friends and family vouchers, nice office.
review_cons: Toxic and bullying culture, very corporate. Micromanaging, staff members try to belittle you. Cliquey vibe. High staff turnover.

summary: Decent, but not an awesome place for an engineer.
review_pros: Pay is good. Rank-and-file people are more pleasant than the industry norm. Work-life balance is fine.
review_cons: Engineers generally have a lower status at Mailchimp, possibly by design. Advancement depends more on pleasing others than job insight. Many current technical leaders, while good at bootstrapping a small company, are not suited for technical leadership at Mailchimp's current size. Humility, a cited core value, is not honored in practice.

review_pros: The office environment is good.
review_cons: Always working overtime, low salary.

review_pros: Overall, responsive and good relationship building. Individual management personnel are personable and caring. Professional. Room for growth.
review_cons: Did not always follow through with commitments. Errors in PTO.

review_pros: Great people, overall rewarding work, good pay and benefits.
review_cons: Poor employee recognition. Senior leadership favors certain managers and discards others, seeking ways to terminate them or force them to quit. Archaic, disconnected computer/software systems. Understaffed IT department, so improvements are years away. Staff are blamed for uncontrollable errors. Performance reviews are unhelpful, lacking constructive information.

summary: Culture shift felt forced, not organic.
review_pros: Generally good people, decent salaries, never a dull moment.
review_cons: The culture shift appeared contrived rather than an organic or true reflection.

summary: Toxic! Half of the people have been terminated, half quit.
review_pros: Paychecks clear. Executive team buys drinks after work. Courtyard crew.
review_cons: CEO is inadequate. People of his stature are in short supply.

review_pros: Volunteers were wonderful, and the mission of serving others is meaningful.
review_cons: Poor leadership from the top, misuse of funds, inability to genuinely care for people, high turnover.

summary: Fraud company. They will not pay even half of the promised stipend. I have proof.
review_cons: Very unprofessional. HR is useless. Do not waste your time in this company.

review_pros: Great coworkers and team leads. Many fun company events and contests.
review_cons: The biggest issue at JFQ is the training department. Most trainers have little to no mortgage or general training experience. Senior processing training is minimal, focusing only on streamlines for one week. Once on the floor, employees are told not to ask questions and to Google before approaching a lead. Conventional and full-doc loans are assigned without warning or training, with leadership expecting no mistakes. There's a disconnect in senior training content, frustrating senior processing leads. Job security is poor due to high turnover; people leave unhappy or are fired without reason. Mass firings have occurred recently, yet the company claims no money issues. Pay is horrible and below industry average. Look elsewhere.

review_pros: Remote work. Small size allows for experimenting with accounts using personal theories/methods. Leaders try to care about employees. Not required to work weekends.
review_cons: Intolerance of employee mistakes. Insufficient benefits and career opportunities.

summary: Horrifyingly terrible. Do not work here.
review_cons: The upper management family writes false positive reviews. This company is a dirty pyramid scheme. Employees are forced to attend insane cult conferences twice a year where the CEO engages in psychobabble for hours. They have zero respect for employees, and the turnover rate is insanely high. Employees are constantly yelled at and diminished by upper management. The company lacks taste and will likely fail within two years.

summary: Great place for learning and advancement.
company_name: Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City
review_pros: The Fed offers many professional development programs, including ITIL certification for IT staff, even new helpdesk employees. All employees, including new and less-experienced ones, are included in meetings and communications, which is great for learning and identifying future specializations. Benefits are excellent from day one, including health insurance, retirement (401k and pension), and vacation (minimum 18 days to start).
review_cons: The Helpdesk is very regimented to ensure coverage, which can be excessive. Breaks and lunch schedules change daily. Overall hours can also change; I was moved from 7:30 AM-4:30 AM to 11:00 AM-7:00 PM after training, which was not what I agreed to. The business casual dress code was annoying, especially for helpdesk IT staff who do not interact with clients, unlike the Minneapolis Fed team who could dress casually. It seemed unnecessary for a non-typical bank with no daily customers.

review_pros: Wellness plans for pets, ability to make your own schedule.
review_cons: Managers are required to work in support roles (receptionist, assistant, or tech). LVTs may work in all support roles except veterinarian, which detracts from management focus. Manager hours count towards the maximum allowed staff hours. To remain profitable, staff are burned out by the high number of patients seen daily.

review_pros: Relaxed atmosphere, decent benefits, remote work, fun company doing cool things in the camping space, $500 annual camping credit for participating campgrounds, occasional downtown meetups in major hubs.
review_cons: Pay might seem high, but no bonuses are offered, and compensation increases are below the inflation rate. Management is young; some managers are clueless and too sensitive to feedback or coaching. Operationally, there is much to learn and figure out. Those needing good structure and supportive leadership may be disappointed.

summary: It's difficult to be talented at Ingram.
review_pros: Ingram has some of the best employees who are dedicated and work to eliminate roadblocks within their scope of influence.
review_cons: Insights and recommendations from highly-skilled employees aiming to grow the business and increase internal efficiencies are bypassed due to politics and the empowerment of the wrong people. Competition between business units and a lack of VP interference against toxic behaviors like exclusion and elitism define the workplace culture, while experts are sidelined. Several employees perform tasks that would be multiple roles elsewhere and are not paid market value. Employees are also not allowed to move to better-suited roles if leadership prefers to keep them.

review_pros: Offers maximum possible salary. Once assigned to a client company, job security is good.
review_cons: If not assigned to a client project, you will be fired. In-house projects are mentioned during recruiting but are not real.

summary: You will learn a lot about being a leader and how to work with others.
review_pros: It's extremely fun to work here, selling cupcakes. Stalwart Concepts works with a good brand and provides a fun and friendly environment. I had a good time and they helped me transfer to a similar office in Miami when I moved out of state. This is the place to be if you are in Tampa.
review_cons: The downside of performance-based jobs like Stalwart Concepts is quickly realizing how hard you are willing to work towards a goal. I was upset with my results but knew I wasn't giving my all. If you overcome personal challenges and find a rhythm, you can advance.

review_pros: Good job if you are interested in sales.
review_cons: Long hours, boring meetings, lots of travel.
No concessions for employees who want to attend school.

review_pros: Wonderful and friendly team members, some great talent.
review_cons: Worst leadership, with extreme micromanagement. Leadership is clueless and lacks business sense. Workplace bullying from senior leadership is common; colleagues and I have cried due to mistreatment. All departments work crazy hours, often due to management trying to please clients unnecessarily, rather than client demands. This is likely due to company greed, not paying for extra hours. The situation is so bad that employees have had mental breakdowns and taken sick leave. Conditions are worse for the US team, who commonly work past midnight. The company uses a contract clause stating "salary covers all time required to complete your job" to make people work like slaves. The unlimited leave policy is a hoax to lure talent; employees don't even have time for standard four weeks of annual leave. The company has forced employees to take long weekends to balance accumulated leave. No one has enjoyed this perk. High turnover; many team members have left in the last 2-2.5 years. Research and think twice before applying or accepting an offer at TimeZoneOne, unless it's a last resort.

review_pros: Very fun atmosphere, and most people are nice.
review_cons: They let me go after barely training me for a month.

summary: Great culture, but the company can do better.
review_pros: Amazing associates, great discounts on everything including the cafe, great work environment, consistently staying on top of trends, consistent long-term employment.
review_cons: Severe lack of pay and pay raises, even with experience. Benefits could be better. The company's handling of COVID closures was messy; short-term hires were fired, and full-time employees were expected to use vacation without additional pay.

review_pros: Paycheck.
review_cons: Terrible and verbally abusive managers.

review_pros: Worked with amazing people. Hard workers had opportunities for advancement. People are smart, fun, and productive.
review_cons: Extremely high turnover, especially in the management team; some of their best people were allowed to leave. Hardest workers were sometimes taken advantage of. Inter-departmental politics made it difficult to get things done. Harsh approach to worker retention. The HR team was backstabbing. Some managers were borderline sexist.

summary: Broken people in a broken workplace.
review_cons: Poor leadership, hostile working environment, insufficient personnel for training, exploitation of temporary workers, and lack of HR presence. I was gaslit, slandered, and abused almost daily. Coworkers gossiped about everyone, including me and our supervisor, within earshot. The supervisor was sarcastic and lacked critical communication skills. I worked closely with a sociopathic teenager entrusted with my training, who taught me the exact opposite of my duties, which management refuted. This likely ensured job security for her and others manipulating the situation. Working here is an uphill battle.

summary: Long hours, tight pay, no loyalty.
review_pros: The travel aspect was nice in the immediate short term.
review_cons: Pay is low, and raises are nonexistent. Employees work 60+ hours per week on salary with no overtime. Employees form cliques and are not friendly or welcoming.
Breaks the law regarding pesticide application.

summary: If you're a processor thinking you'll process here, no. This is a call center.
review_pros: Amazing LOS system. Great resources on the company intranet.
review_cons: You cannot work your loans because this place is a call center.

review_pros: Rooftop terrace events. CEO made nice gestures on holidays, offering pies and champagne.
review_cons: Strange company culture, individuals are undervalued, leadership is toxic and out of touch with reality.

review_pros: I was thankful for being accepted for the position when I desperately needed a job.
review_cons: What is told to potential recruits differs greatly from reality. The company is secretive and lacks transparency. It takes a long time to release claims and benefits to employees. I do not recommend applying here.

summary: Nice place to work unless you are working under a specific manager.
review_pros: Freedom to work with the latest technologies.
review_cons: Immature manager who does not care about employees, only focused on showing off to upper management.

summary: Unprepared leadership, archaic company without humanity.
review_pros: Benefits, health plan, and dress code.
review_cons: Work overload, low salary, completely unprepared leadership from the board to immediate managers.

summary: Avoid unless you like chop shops.
review_pros: Nice teams, but bad management and inexperienced people.
review_cons: Confused management, lacking strategy. They promise "great culture" but it's a chop shop, and they hide things from staff and clients.

summary: Good pay, not a great environment.
review_pros: Great pay compared to similar retailers.
review_cons: Too rigid corporate structures that did not help people take initiatives.

review_pros: Weekly pay, good commissions. Opportunity to meet many new people daily. ADT has great home security systems, allowing you to make an impact in someone's life.
review_cons: Door-to-door sales. Requires a specific type of person to excel beyond making a few sales.
Talked a great show but never delivered.
Will overwork you if you allow it.

review_pros: The mentality is to do no work and receive a substantial paycheck. Senior management spends all their time in meetings and does not notice. They will applaud you for "working hard." If you want an easy life, Muscle Food is for you.
review_cons: The senior management team lacks vision and experience with food. Unless the team is replaced, the company will remain a bargain basement for substandard food.

review_pros: Good team, good location.
review_cons: Senior management does not listen to employees; they do what they want and what they think is right. They are rude and disrespectful towards staff, treating them poorly.

summary: Get your foot in the door, then get out.
review_pros: Hired me with no experience, providing exposure to cybersecurity. It was the only fun company; most SOC guys and developers arranged breaks to play video games or shoot pool.
review_cons: Atrocious pay; most coworkers, including my first boss of four years, had roommates. Starting pay is comparable to or slightly more than an In-N-Out employee. Novacoast's "tight-knit" culture is actually nepotism; most department members had prior relationships with the manager. If you're not into Reddit-type humor or gaming, you'll feel out of place. Most employees are students or recent graduates, as SOCs are built in college towns to exploit cheap student labor. High employee turnover: managers quit with their teams, or new hires leave after a month or two due to unsupportive shifts or being stuck alone on night shifts. The SOC constantly gets new customers, a dangerous formula for an undermanned, underpaid 24/7 staff. Novacoast exploits the continuous stream of students seeking cyber experience. After two years, I left, as have all my former department colleagues. Better pay is available elsewhere. Kansas or Michigan locations might be better due to lower cost of living.

review_pros: Working around animals is always a joy. Decent pay.
review_cons: Job description was inaccurate. I was put on a register without training. After quitting, I received my W-2 but not my final paychecks. They withheld my last paycheck, claiming they didn't have it, and I had to contact HR for a reprint.

review_pros: Good salary, providing a good training program for newbies to gaming.
review_cons: Stoke office is good, but avoid the Gibraltar office. The management team is a joke; they asked employees not to talk or say hello/goodbye to workmates in case of interruption. They questioned employees about toilet breaks and monitored internal chat history. There is no empathy for ill employees. It was a nightmare.

review_pros: Fast processing of job applications, likely due to high resignation rates creating vacancies.
review_cons: Poor leadership from top management. A combination of Chinese and Indian cultures with corrupt government-style politics creates a toxic workplace culture. The culture of NCS is worse than that of a third-world country.

review_pros: Opportunity to meet new people, visit new places, and live in a new city for the summer.
review_cons: Very strict rules, poor and unorganized management, unprofessional.

review_pros: Some decent individuals who truly know how to do their job.
review_cons: Very limited promotion opportunities, selective and based on favoritism. Senior engineers do not know how to mentor junior engineers.

summary: An honest truth for my time with WPG.
review_pros: The marketing team has many great creative minds but lacks recognition. WPG recognized skill gaps and made smart hires. Jennifer and her team hire professionals and trust their expertise in the digital space, challenging employees to think bigger and leverage tools to exceed expectations. The WPG Brands team, under Kimberly's leadership, shifted from reacting to tension to proactively adjusting, turning chaos into a steady tempo through brand consolidation.
review_cons: WPG did not position itself for success in the digital landscape. The company failed to leverage years of website data to understand content driving commerce. The reviewer wished for more opportunity to build digital presence.

review_pros: Decent compensation for advisors and managers.
review_cons: Controlled advisory recommendations and culture, with little autonomy in providing fiduciary advice to clients.

review_pros: Wonderful team, hybrid work, 'unlimited' paid days off.
review_cons: Grossly underpaid compared to workload. Disorganized company, leading to increased work for employees.

review_pros: Remote, flexible schedule, decent PTO policy, decent benefits, stipend for home office, stipend for working at communal office spaces. Culture was decent.
review_cons: Kept as a temp for over a year despite promises of permanent employment, resulting in minimal PTO, benefits, and very low pay. No bereavement time off. Accomplished promotion-worthy work as a temp, but it was erased upon permanent employment, putting the reviewer a year behind peers in career advancement and leading to consistently lower pay. Laid off two weeks after finally being properly promoted. The company prioritizes fancy offices and execs over valuing employees, with multiple rounds of layoffs since October 2021. While the team was great, the company as a whole is not highly regarded.

summary: Seems like a good place to work, but...
review_pros: You learn a lot about the industry, and it can be fun at times.
review_cons: Employees feel stifled professionally. Investment in appearing 'cool' could be better used to benefit customers and employees.

advice_to_management: The good ones leave early, so the remaining are there for the paycheck.
review_pros: Advancement is fantastic if you know the right people. The company offers its own health clinic and good stock/RSU options.
review_cons: Dog-eat-dog culture. Employees who don't conform to the company's ideology are pushed out. 'Think Big' means thinking exactly like management. Boys' club mentality.

review_pros: Great place to work at CBA.

advice_to_management: Trust in every cast member's ability to do their job. Don't extinguish their light.
review_pros: Working with glimpses of creative brilliance. The lot offers a comprehensive range of amenities, from commissary to Starbucks and dry-cleaning.
review_cons: Unbearable red tape and endless, pointless meetings. The new CFO lacks confidence in her team, which will adversely affect employees.

summary: Unhealthy work environment, terrible middle management, and no tech culture.
advice_to_management: Get your leadership right and promote people who really do the work rather than people who project to do the work.
review_cons: Toxic peers. Terrible middle management. Excel-based work despite being hired for ML roles. No tech culture, everything is ad-hoc. No work-life balance. Zero job security. Managers with no coding experience judge coders as beneath them.

advice_to_management: Get to know the process of the department you manage and attempt to learn that process and talk to associates.
review_pros: Ability to leave when desired (if time allows). Great colleagues. Overtime available.
review_cons: Low pay, requiring overtime to survive.

summary: Do well, and we'll give you more responsibilities and no additional pay!
advice_to_management: Modernize systems, especially the website, to prevent downtime during sales. Address tenured employees who are less knowledgeable than new hires.
review_pros: Decent and inexpensive benefits (medical, health, free standby flights). Good camaraderie. Company appreciation events.
review_cons: Very low starting wage. Union protection is limited for the first 7 months. In the reservation center, good performance leads to more responsibility without a pay increase, despite dealing with angry customers and less knowledgeable tenured co-workers.

advice_to_management: Engage with employees more often and put laptops down. Most employees feel treated like numbers, not valuable assets. Stop moving area managers around; allow them to build relationships with employees.
review_pros: Decent wage, 7 paid holidays, opportunities for overtime.
review_cons: Too much favoritism for promotions. Area Managers are often recent college graduates with poor communication skills. Jobs are solely focused on numbers. Write-ups occur without considering personal issues or workflow problems. Poor communication to L1 associates. On-site HR is not trained to handle most issues.

review_pros: Located next to a mall.
review_cons: Management is rigid, shallow, and stuck in the dark ages, despite the company presenting itself as progressive. Too many unhappy, unfulfilled, power-tripping middle-aged/old women in leadership roles. Village champion mentality. Rampant favoritism harms staff morale. Focus on appearances over substance. Too many self-centered sycophants. Pointless 2-3 hour meetings are common. The company is full of 'dead wood'. Mental health is not recognized as a valid condition by old-school senior management.

advice_to_management: Increase wages, especially for new employees, and do not pressure employees to make sales. Employees tend to hide decisive information from potential clients to make quick sales.
review_pros: Willingness to share knowledge and positive information. Opportunities for learning and benefiting from teamwork. Positive environment, great employer, and a good place to work.
review_cons: Low pay for new employees. Too much pressure to show results, sales, and referrals. Employees are motivated by bonuses with less emphasis on quality.

advice_to_management: Utilize existing talent to close gaps in sales and client management. Address the competitive losing streak and develop a winning strategy. Make 'focus on the customer' more than just a slogan.
review_pros: Talented people. Good industry to work in. Good focus on innovation.
review_cons: Incompetent Canadian leadership. Sales and client management teams lag behind market leaders like Amex and VISA. Leadership is unable to drive improvement.

review_pros: Good opportunity and work culture.
review_cons: Low salary in the beginning but high chance of success.

summary: Low Pay, No raises (2% at best), & Archaic, Inflexible, Micromanaging Executive Leadership.
review_pros: Most colleagues that you work with day to day are great.
review_cons: Great disparity between upper and lower management. Handled COVID terribly by bringing all employees back after 3 months. No work-from-home flexibility, even for high performers.

advice_to_management: There are really brilliant people at the Business Leader level who should be promoted to Senior Business Leaders and Group Heads.
review_pros: The company continually offers training courses and programs in all business areas, so there is a lot of opportunity for professional growth and development.
review_cons: Consultants' opinions are not valued by long-term senior executives, despite their contributions to strategic planning. The company could benefit from transforming its talent pool during its transformational change.

advice_to_management: Go to the ground.
review_pros: Good place to work in.
review_cons: Needs more attention from management.

summary: Decent place to work until they let you go due to Corporate greed!
advice_to_management: Focus on loyal customers and employees, not just numbers, to avoid driving business away and high turnover. It was once a pleasant place until the company became greedy. Lead by example and listen to employees who give 110% of their time, as higher-ups are compensated for most of it.
review_pros: Great compensation and benefits from day one, including 401k match up to 6%, tuition reimbursement, and plan discount. Learned new skills in sales process and device support. Colleagues were like a second family, making work bearable.
review_cons: Inconsistent schedule, making a life outside of work difficult. Extremely long hours during holidays, often without lunches or breaks due to busyness or understaffing. Some managers were unprofessional and disrespectful to employees and customers, engaging in unethical practices to increase sales, seemingly encouraged by upper management focused solely on numbers. Company culture and customer experience declined, especially after recent major layoffs. Employees are quickly dismissed to cut costs, regardless of tenure.

advice_to_management: Care about your employees.
review_pros: Pay is good. Work from home.
review_cons: Horrible benefits. Little career advancement despite 4 years of service, while mentored individuals were promoted. Strict T2 requirements prevented promotion. Poor schedule, with changes every 3 months and forced night shifts, making work-life balance impossible. Higher-ups prioritize 'business' over employee care, leading to the company's decline. Success requires constant presence and perfect weekly stats.

summary: No Work-Life Balance, Cold calling three hours a day, and low pay.
advice_to_management: Hire more branch staff. Eliminate cold calling and stop treating employees as workhorses for low pay. Reduce pressure on store managers, as it negatively impacts their employees.
review_pros: Wonderful part-time off plan for full-timers, offering 4-5 weeks annually for sick time, holidays, and vacation.
review_cons: No work-life balance due to full-time commitment and a seven-day bank operation, restricting other engagements. The company is sales-driven and treats employees poorly, forcing cold calls to businesses. Severe understaffing leads to struggling branches, long lines, and customer complaints. Employees are blamed for negative customer experiences, even when caused by understaffing. The job became extremely stressful, leading to the reviewer's departure.

advice_to_management: Pay more to get better people.
review_pros: Flexibility, care about you, multiple places to move up.
review_cons: Salary low compared to other insurance companies.

advice_to_management: Treat employees as humans, not slaves.
review_pros: Overtime pay on the weekends.
review_cons: Micromanagement, unrealistic KPIs, low salary, and mental trauma.

advice_to_management: Try to understand the field service more.
review_pros: Flexible hours, unlimited PTO, company car.
review_cons: Poor management, high turnover, lack of training.

review_pros: Good colleagues and vacation time.
review_cons: 200 calls/day, mandatory sales pitch script, no room for growth.

summary: Positive Review - little bad to say.
review_pros: Very nice, caring owners. Lots of overtime available. Attentive direct supervisor who supports success and growth. Very nice co-workers with similar mindsets. Fun atmosphere. Large cubicles with ample personal space. Quick growth opportunities for hard workers.
review_cons: Requires working in a fast-paced environment and handling stress with ease. Requires thick skin in the industry.

review_pros: Amazing people, dedicated and caring for their team and clients.
review_cons: Poor leadership and highly toxic environment.

summary: Good for freshers and not for experienced.
review_pros: Learning curve for freshers. A few good team leads. No salary deduction during the pandemic.
review_cons: Policies change frequently without prior notice. Lacks a proper appraisal policy and increments. Judgement based on hours worked, not efficiency. Requires juggling multiple projects daily. Difficult to leave the company, with potential salary hold for 30 days after serving notice.

summary: Will never wish it on anyone.
review_pros: Very good working level: passionate, keen to make a difference, very collegiate. Peers are the only saving grace and keep work meaningful.
review_cons: Ineffective, toxic management. Incompetent or unresponsive HR. Ridiculous workload and unnecessary last-minute changes. Strange performance management system based on perception rather than results. No career progression; many haven't been promoted in years.

review_pros: Good pay, comparable to other US tech startups. Perfect for fresh college graduates to stay and learn for a year or two.
review_cons: Employee tenure has not changed since 2009, indicating that claims of positive changes are ineffective. Customer advocacy is weak, with most case studies being small, regional convenience stores, some of which are no longer clients. The product's sole focus on brick-and-mortar retail is risky in the digital age, as consumer habits shift online. The company's finances appear unstable, making it vulnerable to market changes or customer loss. Inclusion and diversity are marketing ploys, not practiced culture (e.g., no BIPOC or female VPs as of April 2021). The company has a political, top-down hierarchy. Employees are made to sign vicious one-way NDAs and separation agreements, waiving rights to speak up, which explains the disproportionate number of positive reviews.

summary: It’s ok temporarily. It sucked the life out of me.
review_cons: Inconsistent policies that change to benefit the company. Lazy co-workers are not held accountable for lack of production or poor quality work. High turnover, with new employees constantly being trained who may not return. The plant manager is unqualified.

review_pros: Good pay, good benefits, and some really good people.
review_cons: The promoted 'FAST family' lifestyle was toxic, leaving no life outside of work. Employees felt guilty using PTO, fearing they would let others down. While employees were expected to forgive company mistakes, individual errors led to long-term shunning and gossip. Blatant sexual harassment by partners, clients, and co-workers was prevalent. Higher-up women dismissed complaints, stating 'that's how it is' and sharing similar experiences.

summary: Lack of investment into plant and employees.
review_pros: Great co-workers and some staff.
review_cons: Impossible to transfer departments. Lower pay than industry standard, compounded by payroll issues. No investment in employee training. Poor morale. Maintenance only for emergencies, leading to significant downtime. No company interest in investing to maintain or create future revenue streams for the mill. Everything is falling apart.

summary: Run as fast as you can!
review_pros: Some amazing people still work there, though many of the best have left. The company is in decline.
review_cons: Highly incompetent management (upper and middle) is ruining the company. Promotions are based on favoritism, with people promoted overnight without interviews. HR is powerless, with their recommendations ignored. No empathy for employees, exemplified by layoffs just before the holiday season. Compensation is below market; senior employees are expected to work excessively because juniors earn less, and managers use higher pay as justification for overwork. Work-life balance is a nightmare in some departments, while others are idle. Overwork, already present, is now unbearable after layoffs. Female employees, especially in tech, are undervalued and unappreciated, with a toxic belief that good engineers must be male. The company hires only to lay off people, sometimes within weeks. High risk of being on the next layoff list.

summary: Love doing what I do if I could actually do my job.
review_pros: Flexible hours.

advice_to_management: People are focused inwards, fighting windmills. Nothing is changing, cooperation is poor, and only reports matter. Self-marketing is extremely vital.
review_pros: Decent salary, bonus, and benefits.
review_cons: No strategy whatsoever. Uninterested management and bad communication. Excessive focus on reports.

advice_to_management: Be more transparent.
review_pros: The money was great.
review_cons: Unprofessional, unorganized, and lack of alignment. Director and recruiter lied to cover themselves. Promised no sales or sales goals, but had monthly targets.

review_pros: Learning about IVs was interesting.
review_cons: A company where small, insecure men with big egos succeed. Manager and supervisor temper tantrums are common. Type A management is encouraged over true team building. Lack of diversity in management.

summary: A good way to get your foot in the door to the IT Field.
review_pros: Experience gain is the main pro; one can start with no IT skills and get up to speed. Offers a diverse working environment.
review_cons: Management is not forward-thinking. Low starting wage ($15/hour) for a technical job requiring attention to detail and production speed, especially during a recession. Back-breaking work. Unprofessional colleagues and horrendous workplace animosity, exacerbated by quotas creating a toxic environment. Slow communication to upper management; requests for improvements are usually rejected. Mandatory standing, based on the belief it improves production. Non-existent training and fluctuating procedures. Minor errors, easily fixable by QA, result in Non-Compliance Issues. Promised bonuses for harder work were delayed by three months, with metrics for awards only then being developed.

review_pros: Great caring people, salary, flexibility.
review_cons: Benefits, management, no work-life balance.

summary: I am grateful to have worked for Parexel. A great place to learn and grow professionally.
review_pros: Amazing team members. Amazing opportunity for learning and growth. Direct management was supportive and helpful.
review_cons: Lack of advancement and compensation; went 3 years without a raise or promotion despite being a top performer. Learning occurs through 'trial by fire' with an emotional cost. Overwhelming workload. Incompetent safety leads who require instruction on their job.

review_pros: Good pay.
review_cons: Unprofessional managers, extremely rude and unorganized bosses. Constant need to reiterate availability, and paychecks were frequently incorrect. The company reduced full-time hours to less than 6 hours a week after hiring a replacement, seemingly to minimize unemployment benefits. Worst company to work for, with no central office or support for issues due to dispersed higher-ups.

review_pros: Professional organization with a class-act Branch Manager in Charlotte who expected 100% effort. The office and company had a strong team feel. The VP of Sales was always available and helpful. The owner genuinely cares about employees, aiming to be the best, not the biggest, payroll company.
Many passionate people dedicated to the brand's success. Made great friends. Fun product and photoshoots with great content, despite limitations in a censored world. Survived many redundancies.
review_cons: The company changed hands multiple times, leading to shifting brand directions and a struggle to maintain authenticity while promoting a niche, underground history with restrictions on nudity and 'adult sites'. Initially, many toxic individuals in senior positions, but the environment improved after their departure. A subsequent acquisition by Four Marketing introduced new problems: the present agency disregarded AP staff's brand/customer knowledge, leading to a lack of collaboration and independence. They ridiculed teams and interrogated individuals. Two men from Present exhibited misogynistic and unprofessional behavior, but are believed to have left.

review_pros: Everyone was willing to share knowledge and positive information. One is able to learn a lot and to benefit from teamwork. Positive environment, great employer, and a good place to work.
review_cons: Ineffective communication strategies lead to disorganization. Employees act independently when they don't fully understand tasks. Teamwork is lacking.

summary: Preys on industry newbies, similar to the rest of the industry.
review_pros: Nice coworkers, happy hours, and a relaxed day-to-day compared to other industry companies.
review_cons: Compensation plan is inadequate for responsibilities. Promotion to Senior Loan Officer is a pay cut unless you are a top producer. Remote work is only for top-producing Senior LOs; others are in-office and constantly monitored. Numerous metrics (20-30) are tracked and used against employees daily, often inaccurately adjusted.

review_pros: Very good benefits and work-life balance.
review_cons: Poor management, lack of career advancement opportunities, high turnover, and lack of collaboration between accounts.

summary: Good company to start your career.
review_pros: Good for first-time workers.
review_cons: Poor management, low pay, breaks codes, and poor upkeep of facilities and equipment.

review_pros: Travel opportunities pre-pandemic, though accommodations involved sharing a 2-bedroom, 1-bath Airbnb with a male coworker.
review_cons: Upper-level management is ineffective and often absent, hiding in offices or gossiping. The atmosphere is anxiety-inducing due to inconsistent feedback and threats to job security. High turnover is caused by constant hiring, firing, and quitting due to emotional trauma. A notable incident involved the CEO's assistant quitting on the spot due to the environment.

review_pros: Good pay, supportive coworkers, multiple divisions, and a strong product suite (carts).
review_cons: Lack of direction from direct manager, who would not return calls unless success was tied to their personal agenda.

review_pros: Consistent 9-5 schedule with weekends and holidays off.
review_cons: Education is run like a business, with the owner prioritizing money. Instructors receive poor salaries, making it hard to find quality staff. Tuition is high, and the company nickel and dimes for everything.

review_pros: Monappa is a good colleague with great work ethics who truly cares for his team.
review_cons: Micromanaging CEO with no work ethics. Value is placed on employees from a certain dominant linguistic group, while hard work and loyalty are not valued. The CEO has no respect for employees.

summary: Useful for starting a career in scientific sales.
review_pros: Strong initial technical training, offering much to learn in the first couple of years. Good camaraderie among staff. Fun charity activities.
review_cons: Excessive micromanagement and overly high productivity expectations, often affected by external factors. R&D offers only niche products, hindering mass-market sales and business growth. Career progression is challenging unless senior positions become vacant, and sometimes the best person is not promoted.

review_pros: International company, always seeking ways to improve.
review_cons: Dysfunctional HR team with a lot of politics.

summary: Good for learning management consulting processes and procedures.
advice_to_management: Pay more attention to lower-level staff.
review_pros: Great employee development and great people.
review_cons: Excessively long hours and difficulty getting promoted if not on the right team.

summary: Take the job but continue seeking other opportunities.
review_pros: Good health and dental benefits, but vision benefits are useless.
review_cons: Dysfunction from the top down. Unqualified people are promoted without support. Excessive red tape hinders progress, leading to working with many frustrated individuals.

summary: Zero work-life balance for the production team.
review_pros: Great employees and fun after-work activities.
review_cons: Despite initial appearances of great perks like unlimited PTO and free meals, there is no work-life balance, with production team members regularly working 10-14 hour days, weekends, and holidays without overtime. The workload is impossible to complete in an 8-hour day, leading to high stress and low morale. Employee surveys about concerns are ignored, with management offering superficial distractions like pizza parties instead of solutions. Management lacks understanding of how to support the team, focusing on blame and scapegoating. Job security is nonexistent, with employees often fired without explanation, reinforcing a perception of expendability. Many employees leave within a year due to these issues.

summary: Great if you lack experience and can tolerate abuse.
review_pros: Good challenge to learn products and stay updated, fair compensation, and internal training. Suitable for compliant rule-followers lacking creativity or ambition.
review_cons: Very political environment where internal relationships outweigh capabilities. No clear career path, and the Peter Principle seems to apply. Advised to avoid the Anaheim office.

summary: Mediocrity all around, especially in leadership.
review_pros: Positive social environment and quality employee entertainment.
review_cons: No culture of quality or learning. Leadership is inexperienced, lacks humility, and exhibits high ego.

review_pros: The company has developed a business structure and attempted to instill solid values in a medical niche field.
review_cons: Despite stated values of patient care, only bare minimum performance was acceptable. Prominent staffing issues led to a lack of accountability and a culture of gossip, cliques, inappropriate behavior, and defiance of company values. Poor performers felt secure due to staffing shortages. Management lacked leadership training and ignored employee complaints. 'Compliance' was interpreted as cheating, lying, and covering for each other.

review_pros: Many growth opportunities, great inter-team communication, numerous benefits, and great people. Top leadership seemed to care about individuals.
review_cons: Left after a year when my manager departed, leading to direct work with an uncaring and uninformed middle manager. This manager hindered personal growth. Despite this, the team and company culture (excluding that specific manager) fostered a strong sense of connection.

review_pros: Great team of talented people, catered food, good office environment, entry-level game industry positions, and health/dental/life insurance benefits.
review_cons: Job security is nonexistent after recent layoffs, with over half of employees actively seeking other jobs. While the team was caring, the current situation is desperate, with management trying to extract maximum effort. Management often ignored insights from those with a better understanding of the Roblox ecosystem, leading to project failures due to poor game design and unprofitable spending.

summary: Management values the organization over employees.
review_pros: Serving poor, immigrants, and other needy groups.
review_cons: Management does not value employees and places them in unsafe conditions for the organization's benefit.

summary: A good place to start a career, but not to stay long-term.
review_pros: Friendly work environment, like-minded employees, good experience and transferable skills training, and encouragement to write freely for portfolio building.
review_cons: Teams are overstaffed due to mass employment. Wages are low for the workload. Opportunities for progression are offered but not reflected in pay, and pay reviews are not provided.

review_pros: Rewarding work and research topics, nice colleagues, good work-life balance (35 hours per week), and a DB Pension.
review_cons: Lack of structure and processes hinders research. Poor direction on ownership forces researchers to manage marketing, communication, and finance, which are not their strengths. Publication takes six weeks, leading to stagnant data. Very limited budget for new ideas or team expansion.

summary: The workplace resembles a never-ending episode of The Office.
review_pros: Known brand with interesting growth and acquisition opportunities. Hope they've learned to acquire companies cleanly and respect new employees.
review_cons: Inefficient processes; approvals and tasks take too long. Employees were forced to sign new contracts over a holiday weekend without legal access. A culture of self-importance exists. The CEO left in the first week, causing emotional distress among some, and there's a 'kool-aid drinking' atmosphere.

summary: Company culture ruined by the new CEO.
review_pros: Subsidized cafeteria lunch and free espresso machines when working in the office.
review_cons: The new CEO, hired in late 2017 to cut costs, transformed Gentherm from a top company to one of the worst. Constant threats of layoffs and pay/benefit cuts are demoralizing. Layoffs, targeting experienced employees, occurred shortly after his arrival and continued through 2020-2021, even after pay was restored post-COVID deferrals. The culture shifted from collaborative to competitive, favoring those who 'suck up' while experienced staff train replacements before being let go. Short-term financial gains from price cuts are unsustainable. The company's commitment to diversity and inclusion is superficial, with no real diversity at the executive level. The 'Women's Network' is perceived as a joke. The reviewer cannot recommend working here until the CEO and his cabinet are replaced.

summary: Employees are not expected to stay long.
review_pros: If you wish to diminish your skills, mental health, and good qualities, this is the place for you.
review_cons: The company culture does not expect employees to stay long. Unlimited leave is offered but rarely approved by managers, even for minimal requests. Colleagues escalate issues when contradictory requirements are pointed out in meetings. Communication lacks empathy and etiquette in a remote-first environment. Salary increases are minimal (e.g., 5%). There is a lack of trust between US and India engineering teams. The manager who conducts most interviews has a team with a 50% turnover rate within a year.

summary: Loved the job and was sad to leave.
review_pros: Excellent incentives, great opportunities for progression, strong encouragement for networking, and large client exposure allowing career guidance across various industries. Sad to leave due to needing to return home.
review_cons: Monitoring a significant number of incentives can be challenging, potentially leading to missed opportunities. Hours can be long, but incentives compensate for them (work hard, play hard culture).

advice_to_management: Continue transforming. Stop limiting hiring to top-tier schools to improve diversity.
review_pros: Variety of subjects, quick assignment of responsibilities, many smart people, international environment, and strong culture.
review_cons: Difficult to move within the company if not from a top-tier school. Mid-managers do not systematically challenge their teams. Career evolution becomes difficult at a certain point.

review_pros: Working with Rehab Manager Karen Yong was a pleasure. She was active with the APTA Home Health section, knowledgeable about PT practice acts and reimbursement, and an excellent, patient, kind, and true leader and mentor.
review_cons: Healthcare is challenging, requiring high productivity and extensive documentation. Working in San Francisco involved visiting sketchy neighborhoods and health-hazardous homes. The reviewer retired early from the profession at the start of the pandemic.

summary: Not a great place to start a career.
review_pros: Good clients from whom many things can be learned.
review_cons: High work pressure, poor management, and comparatively low salary.

advice_to_management: Give staff more autonomy to perform.
review_pros: Good compensation, benefits, coworkers, technology, and financial strength. Some managers are also good leaders.
review_cons: Difficult to focus on work due to reporting and training demands. Some managers use old-school approaches and micromanage.

summary: Good reputation, but poor management and low pay.
advice_to_management: Provide help and stop playing politics.
review_pros: Strong reputation and long-standing presence in the banking industry.
review_cons: Clueless management, horrible operations departments, and low pay compared to peers.

advice_to_management: The issues are systemic, suggesting the Board of Directors needs to address executive-level problems. Such experiences damage the company's professional reputation, and ignoring them will not help.
review_pros: Remote work and kind IT staff.
review_cons: Terrible onboarding and poor communication. Paid training was not fully compensated. Employees were asked to use personal credit cards for Azure subscriptions. A complete disconnect existed between training materials and actual job expectations. Onboarding device materials and setup were outdated. The reviewer quit after three weeks without being trained on time tracking and was not paid for time past initial training.

review_pros: Stable company with a good bonus structure and retirement plan.
review_cons: Long hours, challenging bosses, and short vacations.

review_pros: Poor management skills, Worst start up company to work for
review_cons: Poor management skills. The director does not treat people with respect.

review_pros: NO pros whatsoever! RUNNNNN FAR AWAY!
review_cons: Horrible management, low pay, and unfair treatment.

summary: Okay before COVID-19, much worse afterward.
review_pros: Trustworthy colleagues.
review_cons: Poor management. Consistently low staffing and difficulty obtaining supplies. Raises are only given after mass employee departures due to poor treatment.

review_pros: No pros exist within this company
review_cons: No respect for employees and no value for their contributions.

review_pros: Initially, working here was viable and profitable. Awesome coworkers (excluding higher-ups). Remote work option.
review_cons: After going public, the company rapidly declined. Unnecessary programs and 'improvements' were introduced, with new staff hired while hundreds were laid off. Employees experienced overwork, pay cuts, removal of promised bonuses, and constant changes to policies and benefits.

summary: Good starter job, but the company's growing pains present issues.
review_pros: The job is relatively easy after learning primary cell isolation. Compensation is good, potentially justified by being on-call every other week. Free food and snacks are provided. The team is great and cooperative, leading to a generally low-stress job. The manager is amazing and accommodating. Benefits include paid medical and dental, and free vision, disability, and life insurance. Minimal paid parental leave is expected in 2023.
review_cons: Limited advancement opportunities within the team, primarily to tech lead, which is used to retain staff. The team is mostly manufacturing, not R&D as initially stated, leading to repetitive work. Being on-call every other week, with about four calls per month outside business hours, is not ideal despite advance scheduling. PTO is limited to about 10 days per year plus 1-2 flex holidays. Startup growing pains cause stress. The reviewer left due to the job not suiting their work style and the boss's harsh actions and frequent mood swings, which kept the team on edge. Executives work from home during inclement weather, but technicians are denied this, even with minimal work.

review_pros: Cannot think of anything positive to say
review_cons: Backward pay structure fails to attract top talent. A very cliquish and close-minded monoculture exists due to long-tenured employees and few outsiders. Lacks understanding of market research, marketing, and a sound vision. Develops unwanted products. Does not embrace new ideas. Dreadful atmosphere where nobody smiles. The reviewer was glad to leave.

advice_to_management: Implement cross-training for both management and workers.
review_pros: Decent benefits including 401K, dental, and vision.
review_cons: Poor pay and no job training; employees are left to learn job requirements independently.

review_pros: none that matter come to mind
review_cons: Approximately 20% churn rate in the TLV office, affecting developers, team leads, product staff, and even office managers.

review_pros: Pay and benefits are decent, but could be improved.
review_cons: Initially mission-driven, the company shifted to a profit focus as it grew.

review_pros: Freshers and interns can learn a lot due to a lack of rigid processes and restrictions. Access is provided to explore one's domain. Offers good work-life balance and flexible timings. Employees can work independently within set timelines and targets.
review_cons: Lack of processes. Not suitable for experienced or management-oriented individuals. Unclear vision and mission. Deficiency of projects. Fails to retain valuable employees.

review_pros: Great pay, opportunities for growth, fast-growing company, and supportive staff.
review_cons: General startup challenges common to every company.

review_pros: Great people and a wonderful team. Fair benefits and pay. Wonderful projects and an interesting client roster. Beautiful working space and huge growth potential.
review_cons: Lack of direction, training, and support. Very little consistency. Decisions are not always made for the good of the entire business.

review_pros: Good pay compared to other districts.
review_cons: The system itself is disorganized.

review_pros: Large organization with opportunities for learning, development, and training. Good culture, global advantage, international culture, and diversity.
review_cons: Busy, large organization with many deadlines and long hours.

summary: The devil you know is better than the devil you don't know.
advice_to_management: Stop judging employees.
review_pros: Consistent shifts, available overtime, and bonuses.
review_cons: Worked hard for over a year and achieved KPIs, but the bonus was withheld upon leaving.

advice_to_management: Consider more flexible hours.
review_pros: Nice people to work with.

advice_to_management: Management would improve if upper leadership stopped making ridiculous demands.
review_pros: Bonuses, but only after being forced to reach sales goals.
review_cons: They do not care about employees, only about numbers.

review_pros: Stable employer, good cafeteria, and great location.
review_cons: Bad and incompetent management. Terrible pay. HR does not advocate for employees. High turnover rates. Management has a revolving door policy.

review_pros: Good place to gain experience, but don't stay too long.
review_cons: Payment is below competitors for the same positions.

review_pros:
Could achieve a rating with right Management Acions.
Pros:
+Good pay
+Good technology 
+Good transferable technology to other companies
+Strong Business Financials & Backlogue.

review_cons:
Corp. Management established New Culture where People are totally expendable.

2. Working environment, Highly Politicized, low level of Integrity, Honesty, Trust and Respect. Contradicting company's "Core Principles and Values." (greatest challenge!)

3. Lack of job advancement (may change)
4. Struggling with acquisitons and restructuring (may change).

advice_to_management: Pay for work, not based on previous salary.
review_pros: Toxic culture and lack of integrity.
review_cons: Employees receive low feedback, leading to giving up.

summary: Accenture is a big brand with an awesome working environment.
advice_to_management: Employees are human, not bots; show some respect and dignity.
review_pros: The working environment is great. All assets are provided for flexible work. Leads are supportive and guide through the process.
review_cons: Despite facilities and supportive leads, management now treats employees like slaves, lacking respect. This behavior started after COVID-19; previously, everything was good.

advice_to_management: Monitor crew behavior.
review_pros: Rude politely good kind nice
review_cons: Crew members do not care about others.

advice_to_management: Increase transparency and accountability from management.
review_pros: Sufficient hours and flexible shifts.
review_cons: Often required to punch out later than expected.

summary: Great place to kickstart a career, but leave before it becomes detrimental.
advice_to_management: Address the inability to sell, stop BU presidents from faking figures by loading wholesalers last minute with huge discounts, continuously cut the blueprint, and stop promoting individuals solely based on short-term delivery.
review_pros: Promotion is based on potential, not just achievements, which allows for fast growth but can lead to working with ineffective 'sweet talkers'. The company offers significant learning opportunities and early responsibility. The culture is direct and decisive.
review_cons: Toxic management, especially in the last 2-3 years, with leaders backstabbing each other for good news rather than results. Lack of empathy in the People function. The company struggles with organic growth, leading to constant cost cuts, immense pressure, and burnout. Employees who stay too long are labeled 'blockers'. Diversity and inclusion are superficial, with real career opportunities mostly going to Brazilian males. Pay is below market average, and bonuses have been absent since 2019 due to poor company results and declining shares. The company is very short-term oriented.

review_pros: It is a good company to choose.
Good environment.
review_cons: No transport.

advice_to_management: Encourage work-life balance and prompt managers to recognize staff achievements, especially during difficult times.
review_pros: Time at the bank passed quickly. Started with a 2-week temporary assignment and stayed 30 years until redundancy. Was offered other roles but chose to pursue something new. Would have stayed if not for redundancy.
review_cons: Due to staff levels, it was sometimes unclear where to seek help. The environment was very changeable and at times stressful.

advice_to_management: Develop a better sales model. Employees are forced to sell unwanted products like "Hum," a car product unrelated to phone service, often by deceptively hiding costs in customer plans. Failure to sell results in negative coaching, despite customer disinterest or even product damage. This dishonest practice is enforced with threats for not meeting quotas. Implement a fairer scheduling structure; currently, only top performers get desired schedules, while others are subject to an impersonal computer system. The promise of internal promotion is largely a joke, benefiting only a select few.
review_pros: Significantly higher pay than most retail jobs, generous paid time off, and great health benefits.
review_cons: It is still a retail job, with severe micromanagement. Employees are required to work every weekend and are forced to sell terrible products like "Hum," facing trouble if they fail to do so.

advice_to_management: Stop favoritism, be better resources for the team, and help them succeed instead of undermining them. Push back on executives collectively. The CEO is responsible for setting and fixing the company culture.
review_pros: Decent pay, benefits, remote work options, and some smart, nice, and productive colleagues.
review_cons: Constant, impulsive organizational changes by executives with little forethought. Expectations to work and be available on weekends, even if not disclosed during hiring. Unreasonable expectations, constant stress, and overwork. Promotions are difficult and often based on favoritism. Very little work-life balance. Low employee retention, with most employees staying 2 years or less. Employees are expendable, and it is not a long-term career place. The corporate culture is unlikely to improve unless executives care.

advice_to_management: Be transparent. Allow students to solve complex issues and gain real experience.
review_pros: Great opportunities for students to move between teams, gaining exposure and experience.
review_cons: Mostly tedious administrative work.

advice_to_management: Replace the HR Department with a more professional one. Address issues when many employees complain or leave due to a single member of Senior Management, rather than ignoring them.
review_pros: Friendly colleagues. Outdated systems and processes offer the opportunity to learn products in-depth due to minimal automation.
review_cons: Blame culture. Unprofessional HR Department. Low pay and long work hours. Zero tolerance for errors. Only one female in management. Outdated systems and processes.

summary: Workplace drama, management indifferent to strategic solutions, and a difficult environment for junior employees.
employee_job_title: Assistant Vice President In Equities Derivatives IT
advice_to_management: Develop strategic solutions instead of pushing meaningless agendas. Provide incentives for junior employees to stay and feel motivated. Listen to SME advice. Address complaints of harassment and inappropriate behavior in the office. Do not skip exit interviews; high turnover indicates major issues with managers or teammates. HR should monitor interoffice relationships to avoid conflicts of interest.
review_pros: Good teams, strong developers, knowledgeable business, potential for interesting work, and good internal mobility opportunities.
review_cons: Terrible, frequently changing management leads to confused goals and wasted money on scrapped projects. Management promotes a bad workplace culture and disregards employees. HR and managers ignore inappropriate behavior, including affairs and sexual harassment. Junior employees are left with problematic, unfinished projects when others move to new roles.

advice_to_management: Listen to employees and hold abusive leaders accountable.
review_pros: It wasn't bad a few years ago but became toxic and intolerable.
review_cons: Leadership supports and encourages negative, chauvinistic, and unethical behavior, which the HR department is aware of and allows to continue.

review_pros: Managers are very inclusive and want employees to succeed.
review_cons: Work can be stressful depending on location. Training was inadequate for certain tasks.

summary: Good training, poor leadership, and forced company culture.
advice_to_management: Stop faking a positive company culture; let it grow naturally by taking care of employees. Pay them on time, stop unprofessionalism, gossiping, and fraternizing.
review_pros: Frequent and relevant product trainings. Some middle management and cross-team colleagues offer good tips and tricks.
review_cons: Most management is poor. Project clients are condescending and encourage gossiping about managers. There is a lot of general gossip and unprofessionalism within management teams.

advice_to_management: Prioritize hiring individuals who genuinely want to work and perform their jobs properly.
review_pros: Easy work for those with common sense.
review_cons: The majority of employees are young teenagers lacking customer service skills and respect.

advice_to_management: Focus on performance management, provide a clear path for career advancement, and collaborate with HR partners to improve job satisfaction.
review_pros: Many different job opportunities, decent pay (though lower than MRP), and work-from-home options.
review_cons: No promotions (requires applying to different jobs). The financial bottom line is the priority. The culture is artificial, like something from an HR textbook.

advice_to_management: Continue running the co-op program as is. The program provided significant learning, and JNJ management inspired interest in working there after graduation, despite initial disinterest in supply chain.
review_pros: Open-door policy, helpful colleagues, autonomy on projects. The 6-month co-op makes you feel like a full-time employee. Treated like a full-time employee, not just a co-op. Great hourly rate and overtime.
review_cons: Many reporting responsibilities in addition to regular, project-based work. Difficult to secure a second co-op due to high competitiveness.

summary: Work at HSBC only if necessary.
advice_to_management: Address the root cause of business issues instead of blaming employees for lack of success. Simplify procedures.
review_pros: Good benefits, insurance, and nice co-workers.
review_cons: Sales pressure, unprofessional management, and complicated systems.

advice_to_management: Clearly define job descriptions and objectives for employees. Implement and enforce a process for oilfield project management. Properly train managers in both technical and soft skills. Enforce English language skills for operations in the oil industry.
review_pros: Flexible and tolerant management. Adequate pay for international employees.
review_cons: Disorganized. Not internationally open; it is mostly an Italian company with limited growth opportunities for foreigners. Operates more like a public company than an IOC, with local employees resembling government workers. Employees are stressed and not always efficient. Lack of clear business processes. Human resource management is non-existent and lacks transparency.
Little to no details provided about training or expectations, leading to being talked about behind closed doors.

review_pros: More holidays. Opportunity to work with complex frameworks and various technologies.
review_cons: System is over-designed and very complex, requiring significant time to understand.

summary: Horrible environment; management does not care about employees.
review_pros: Employees are paid on time.
review_cons: Owners do not care about employees and do not take COVID seriously. Extremely high turnover, and overall, not a good work environment. Few employees stay longer than a year.

review_pros: Nice coworkers and good location.
review_cons: Unrealistic expectations from management and work-life balance is not a priority.

review_pros: Made many friends and enjoyed working with colleagues who made the day great.
review_cons: Work was hard.

advice_to_management: Pay technicians and pharmacists more to reduce high turnover.
review_pros: Good time off, great communication from higher-ups ensuring everyone is informed, and a positive culture.
review_cons: Stressful work environment, impossible metrics for bonuses, constant new tasks, deadlines, and endless goals/clinics.

summary: Easy to get hired, hard to perform.
advice_to_management: Recognition for a job well done is highly valuable. Incentives are limited if work is not supported and appreciated.
review_pros: Set schedule, easy hiring process, and an hour lunch break.
review_cons: Takes time to earn decent money, with a glass ceiling reached within 3 years. Benefits are mediocre. Professional growth is difficult to achieve.

summary: Great place to work, but with issues.
advice_to_management: Develop long-term processes rather than relying on momentary personalities.
review_pros: Great branding and history. Top-notch banking organization. Highly dedicated front office staff.
review_cons: Organization, especially in IT, is erratic; they sometimes act like Google. Personalities often prioritize advancement over job completion. Preference for short-term fixes over long-term solutions.

advice_to_management: Improve communication and make greater efforts to solve equipment issues.
review_cons: Teleperformance is not a great place to work; it is very unorganized. Managers are unprofessional. If equipment is broken, employees receive demerit points even after communicating with managers, who still assign blame. There is a complete lack of communication.

summary: Good start for an entry-level job.
advice_to_management: Train leaders and organize schedules to ensure a reasonable workload.
review_pros: Stability, good environment, valuable experience, and work-from-home options during the pandemic.
review_cons: Lack of organization, weak leadership skills, no tools provided for work, very long hours, and disorganized schedules.

review_pros: Good pay and benefits.
review_cons: Very toxic work environment.

summary: Not bad, but not a tech company.
review_pros: Relaxed work environment, flexible scheduling, and openness to new ideas.
review_cons: The loudest people dominate. Lack of direction for team or growth. Constant reorganizations negatively impact morale.

advice_to_management: Streamline home loan processing duties.
review_pros: Decent pay with flexible hours. Training was consistently well done. Some encouraging managers.
review_cons: Micromanagement. Pressure to process incomplete work. Expected to handle too many responsibilities.

advice_to_management: Maintain current practices.
review_pros: Great work-life balance and great leaders. Enjoyed time there with great teams.
review_cons: Few cons, but there is a lot of organizational shift.

review_pros: Good tech stack and colleagues.
Opportunity to work with good brands and great in-house team members.
Great, caring fellow teachers.
review_cons: Defamation of former employees.

summary: Static organization with little forward momentum.
review_pros: Work-from-home/flexible work options. Good tools and applications for work. Onboarding product is a solid solution.
review_cons: High turnover. Lack of innovation. Homogenous thinking and behavior, leading to disorganization, ineffective solutions, and cultural complacency regarding improvement or change.

review_pros: Remote work, 100% healthcare paid, and unlimited PTO (though employees may not feel comfortable using it).
review_cons: The company hires based on 'mental stamina' to handle a sales workhorse environment. Competition is toxic, not encouraging.

The employee's experience ended terribly, and they would not recommend working here. The company initially appeared to care about employees but proved otherwise. The department the employee was hired into was shut down on the third day of onboarding, forcing a move to sales with no experience. Hours were changed, negatively impacting family life. Despite excelling and receiving a promotion and pay raise within two months, the employee was required to obtain a real estate license, involving six months of studying outside work hours. After obtaining the license, the employee was moved back to the original department, then 'promoted' to an unfamiliar role without application or interview. Reservations were expressed, and after a period of slow operations, the employee was moved back to the original hired role. Two weeks later, the employee was fired for not being a 'good fit,' despite adapting to all changes and being a top performer in sales. Only one week of severance was provided. A month later, a large layoff occurred with significantly larger severance packages, suggesting the company used the employee's honesty to avoid a layoff. This was potentially the company's second or third layoff in three years.

summary: Avoid if you are in marketing.
review_pros: It's probably good that everything is so constantly chaotic. It takes years for people to be noticed as incompetent.
review_cons: The marketing department is in shambles, with a very high turnover rate. It scores poorly in surveys. The company uses temporary fixes instead of permanent solutions. There is a lack of responsibility and accountability.

review_pros: Global Dimensions treats employees like family. CEO Chris Newton, Candice, and Noura genuinely care. The motto 'People first. Mission always' reflects their commitment. High respect for the GD team, and would work with them again. Appreciates the support during employment as a Linguist in Afghanistan. Chris and Noura are kind people and great leaders.
Enjoyed being a property manager. Pay was below appropriate levels for apartment size and responsibilities. Benefits were acceptable.
review_cons: Training was inadequate, left to a power-tripping property manager who was also managing another property. Insufficient time for proper training on numerous required reports. Regional management was unhelpful, often preoccupied. Expected to manage property renovations in addition to regular duties, making 8-hour days insufficient. Lack of a dedicated project manager for renovations. Requests for help with expired leases, unposted HAP payments, and messed-up ledgers were ignored, with instructions to prioritize other tasks. Was told to lie to owners about property problems and to falsely report skipped tenants as merely behind on rent to hide vacancies. Fired without warning for being overwhelmed. High management turnover. Company prioritizes regional managers over property managers. Regional manager displayed unprofessional behavior, discussing personal matters during a work lunch.

review_pros: Benefits are the only notable positive.
