review_cons: Poor management and no clear path for advancement.

review_pros: Great people to work with.
review_cons: The pharmacist was not a team player.

review_pros: Dogs allowed in the office and free food on Fridays.
review_cons: While colleagues (non-leadership) are kind and hardworking, the company itself is not competitive in the job market. Many employees come from worse jobs, finding MT 'not bad,' but quickly realize they are underpaid, overworked, and lack growth opportunities, especially in less valued departments. Growth is often promised without a clear plan. Salaries are significantly lower (10-15k+) than market rates for similar roles. Leadership is inexperienced, unapproachable, and either unaware or unwilling to address employee issues like capacity. The company primarily rewards sales, has incompetent leadership, and fails to uphold its stated values. It is not competitive in a thriving economy. Employees are advised to treat this job as a transition, acknowledge limited growth, and set boundaries to avoid being overworked or exploited.

advice_to_management: Adopt new modalities to keep employees happy.
review_pros: Good work environment, with teams dedicated to their goals.
review_cons: Tendency towards full in-person work.

advice_to_management: Consider the challenges drivers face.
review_pros: Good pay rate, overtime, and weekly incentives.
review_cons: Unsympathetic management, on-call requirements, and haughty customers.

advice_to_management: Keep new hires and offer them higher salaries.
review_pros: Good work/life balance, great work environment, supportive team.
review_cons: Salaries are too low, and it's hard to get a promotion or advance in your career.

review_pros: Great team and management create an open and motivating working environment where everyone is equal, and ideas and opinions are heard and encouraged. It's a great place to work for anyone wanting to make an impact and be part of the tech world.
review_cons: Employees must feel comfortable making decisions and having a certain level of autonomy/responsibility.

summary: Stay away from the London office
review_pros: Decent benefits, nice offices, and real potential.
review_cons: The London loan processing team lacks agility, self-organization, and autonomy, with top-down micromanagement. Team leads and managers are manipulative and untrustworthy. Employees are expected to work excessive overtime and meet unrealistic deadlines. Management in this team is self-serving.

advice_to_management: Focus on nurturing employees from different backgrounds, rather than prioritizing those from legacy Chevron.
review_pros: The company prizes its ethical foundation, has a strong focus on safety and gender diversity, and a very talented workforce.
review_cons: It is a very risk-averse company; most leaders have avoided risky assignments. There is a strong connective thread for those who worked together in their early years, especially on California assignments.

review_pros: Very good people to work with at the functional/individual level. The company managed the COVID situation relatively well. The new CEO appears to be trying to correct course on culture and engagement. Generally interesting industries and technologies, with opportunities to get a lot of exposure to different parts of the business operation.
review_cons: A cost-cutting mindset has been deeply rooted over the last five years, with the Finance department driving the company, not tech or engineering. The technical team's experience level has greatly reduced through headcount reductions and outsourcing. The company is unable to effectively innovate organically, acquiring technology through M&A with limited success. It finds ways to run leaner every year, burdening remaining staff with more work, resulting in extensive burnout and turning a blind eye to turnover. There is limited meaningful appreciation for employee experience, and benefits continue to be reduced year after year.

review_pros: sunrun looks like a amazing company they are so nice, but when you actually work for them its a different story. I have been in sales 11 years and have a bachaelors in marketing, the pandemic forced me to look for another job so I went back to in home sales. I quit sunrun after 30 days, I leave out the defamation of character and belittlement I suffered at the hands of my sales manager I can take that, I was there for the $$$ I was a 40% closer in water softeners, my first 30 days I got 3 sits sold 1 one, the first sit had already had a no from sunrun cuz they only do roof installs, but sunrun basically keeps sending reps out till they buy, they never close a lead, the second my company issue computer failed so I couldnt deliver proposal, the third appointment yeah, I was the second rep out but I sold it, however sunrun couldnt do it cuz costco doesnt let you do spanish demos so no sale first 3 weeks and training was 40 hours a weeka days a week, your on call 7 days a week even if you block off your calendar they call your emergency contacts . Oh if you do sell it cantake3 months to get paid, and your salary is 250 a week. I quit in 30 days,
review_cons: Employees will have no life. Making money is possible only if you survive the first year. The company appears amazing and nice, but the reality of working there is different. The reviewer experienced defamation and belittlement from the sales manager. Sales are difficult due to company policies (e.g., roof installs only, no Spanish demos) and technical failures. Training is 40 hours a week, and employees are on call 7 days a week. Payment for sales can take up to 3 months, with a salary of $250 a week.

review_pros: A place where one can improve knowledge and progress in their career path.
review_cons: Strict reporting times with little grace period are a disadvantage for those who work late.

review_pros: Employees will enjoy working with students to help them achieve their goals and dreams.
review_cons: Hostile working environment that does not promote growth or unity among co-workers.

advice_to_management: Properly train and educate leadership positions instead of offering quick and untimely promotions. Management needs to understand feasible demands for the company. To yield better results, operational changes are needed on a larger scale.
review_pros: With properly trained and educated management, day-to-day activities can run smoothly. Properly staffed teams are a must.
review_cons: Managers are not properly trained to oversee staff and lack leadership qualities, often powered only by their superiors. This creates a toxic chain where store managers demand unfeasible results, leading assistant managers and team leads to demand exhausting and unfeasible results from employees, contributing to high turnover.

review_pros: Once had cutting-edge benefits, but now they are just average as other companies have caught up.
review_cons: Outdated software, and the new Viya products are not catching on with customers.

summary: If you're not a provider, you're not worth anything to them
review_pros: The school cafeteria had good food.
review_cons: Everything, including the cost of food at the office bistro and "discounts," was geared toward providers and their salaries. Insurance benefits were not great, only allowing Froedtert providers. Job duties did not meet salary expectations, resulting in too much work for too little pay.

review_pros: Good pay and chances to grow and relocate.

summary: Sold a Dream, Misleading Company With No Support
review_pros: Great coworkers for the most part.
review_cons: The company misled the reviewer about potential earnings, promising $65k but resulting in $0 in two months. The recruitment process was problematic, with a high dropout rate and questionable office location near an MLM scheme. The reviewer received no shadowing opportunities during unpaid training, unlike others. Leads provided were often invalid or cancelled, indicating a lack of support and deceptive practices. The company plays games with leads and does not provide adequate support, leading to high turnover.

review_pros: No Pros! Shadiest, shittiest company you could ever work for ! Trainer was the absolute rudest! Made you feel like there was no way in hell you could be successful
review_cons: Shady company that views employees as easily replaceable numbers and does not value them. The training program is terrible and sets employees up for failure. The trainer was rude and made the reviewer feel unsuccessful.

review_pros: The location is hard to fault.
review_cons: There is significant micromanagement by the CEO.

review_pros: Great job security, competitive wages, super cool products, and excellent co-workers. Ilmor offers many employee perks to show appreciation. It is one of the best companies the reviewer has worked for, offering challenging yet satisfying and rewarding outcomes.
review_cons: Tight timelines, work-life balance is heavily weighed towards work, and there is a large gap between working level and management level.

review_pros: Great work environment, friendly colleagues, and decent perks.
review_cons: Needs structure for career progression and more experienced individuals in top management. Management is not transparent.

advice_to_management: Please maintain the same pace.
review_pros: Efficiency, easily met targets, and a professional human approach.

summary: Don't work here unless you want to work lots of hours for little pay
advice_to_management: The broken corporate culture is deeply entrenched, and there is no hope for change.
review_pros: It can be a fun place to work, and outsiders perceive it as a cool place to be employed.
review_cons: The organization is sclerotic, unable to correct decades-old procedural problems. Promotions are based on willingness to work excessive overtime and tolerate abuse, rather than knowledge or work quality, favoring "yes men." The company is committed to paying employees as little as possible, offering no performance reviews or mechanisms for raises beyond competing for higher-paying positions, which requires working 6-7 days a week for years.

summary: Processes are more important than employees
advice_to_management: Include employee satisfaction in the bottom line, with measurable metrics.
review_pros: Working on the corporate side as a Recruiter provided a stable position due to the high turnover rate in retail banking.
review_cons: HSBC did not value employees and made it increasingly difficult for retail banking staff to earn bonuses each year.

review_pros: Great place to make progress and fosters an ownership culture.
review_cons: Poor work-life balance and inconsistent spending.

review_pros: Good work environment within the group.
review_cons: Variable salary below market rate.

advice_to_management: Management should experience the routes for a few weeks to understand the current operational issues that have ruined a once great company.
review_pros: Pay is good, but employees definitely earn it through hard work.
review_cons: Employees will work excessively for the good pay, devoting their entire lives to the company. Those with families and kids will not see them often.

advice_to_management: Keep up the good work.
review_pros: Good pay and benefits for all.

advice_to_management: Listen to employees.
review_pros: Flexible working conditions and friendly staff.
review_cons: Issues with senior management, communication, and office politics.

advice_to_management: You have been the best employer throughout my career.
review_pros: Had the best experience with the company and enjoyed working there.

advice_to_management: Management needs to do their job properly and care about the staff.
review_cons: No training or support. The Virgin Media campaign was horrendous, with no help, and Team Leaders did not care.

advice_to_management: Maintain your current approach to building your reputation.
review_pros: One of the best organizations for work-life balance, as the company is very employee-oriented.

summary: Loved the job, and some of the people
advice_to_management: Listen to support staff and ask for input on projects that affect them. Do not deprecate actively used, functioning processes to invest engineering hours into unrequested features. Convert more contractors to full-time roles; the company is losing valuable talent, including highly skilled PgMs, due to a lack of conversion opportunities.
review_pros: I loved my job and was given a lot of freedom to grow and design/implement projects as I saw fit. Pay was alright, perks were AMAZING. Man, I miss the food and kitchen staff. And the food. Gained a lot of experience and made a lot of cool friends and was able to further my career goals. Would have stayed a lot longer if I could have transferred to another team.
review_cons: The perks distract you from the people that suck and project budget allocation mismanagement. Also contractors are not treated equally - some of the full time employees go out of their way to not make friends that are contractors since "they'll be gone in 2 years anyways". Engineering was flippant and rude to support staff and treated like kings no matter what. Literally had someone be racist to my face about COVID and nothing was done about it. Also saw multiple contractors reduced to tears over the way they were treated. Having the company name on my resume is valuable in the tech world, and they know that so they can skimp on some areas.

advice_to_management: Ensure employees adhere to better handwashing hygiene.
review_pros: Flexible hours, opportunities to meet new people, meal discounts, and various areas to work in.
review_cons: Long time for advancement and rude customers.

review_pros: Good money and great management.
review_cons: Hard to hit targets, especially in a quiet area.

advice_to_management: Many improvements are needed. Start by valuing those who genuinely work hard, rather than those who simply attend year-end retreats (which are exclusively pre-selected by permanent staff). Stop favoring sycophants, as it severely demotivates those who actually want to contribute.
review_pros: In the sector where the reviewer worked, it was calm, without pressure, with a great salary, two coffee breaks, and the boss was not micromanaging.
review_cons: The company did not value those who truly worked, but rather those who joined cliques or were deemed convenient. Permanent staff treated temporary employees poorly. There is a significant lack of professionalism and competence.

review_pros: Paychecks were always on time. All-star employees might avoid vulgar language. The office location was convenient, near two highways. Had great fellow employees. The CEO is actively involved, even posting reviews on Glassdoor.
review_cons: Company policy required all customer emails to go through the CEO, who would sometimes contact clients directly without consulting the employee. The CEO would demand proofreading for grammatical errors in employee emails, despite his own mistakes. The reviewer chose to leave due to poor treatment of co-workers. The CEO states the company is a sales company, not a software company, which may be misleading for those seeking a software role.

review_pros: Amazing peers, opportunities to bond over shared experiences, and chances to learn on many different projects.
review_cons: Overconfident but inexperienced leaders. Constant changing priorities with no time for onboarding or training. Very restrictive personal freedoms, including set lunch breaks and detailed timesheets that create a lack of trust. Significant micromanagement hinders productivity. A strong blame culture requires employees to constantly cover themselves. Senior Leadership Team (SLT) does not accept feedback. Very high staff turnover due to stress levels. Poor work-life balance. Culture of favoritism and nepotism. Long contracts with large financial penalties for early departure.

review_pros: Innovative software platform and high-profile clients.
review_cons: Aggressive schedules. Senior Management lacked the skills needed to deliver and retain employees.

review_pros: Enjoyed the flexibility to make my own schedule and providing patient care, though with little to no help.
review_cons: Horrible patient-to-staff ratio. The floor had an overall negative mentality. The negatives significantly outweigh the positives.

review_pros: Amazing co-workers and decent parking.
review_cons: Terrible leadership provides very little support. Benefits are consistently reduced. Very high turnover. Lack of consistency, disorganization, employees feel undervalued, and insufficient training for new employees.

review_pros: Opportunity to learn about cybersecurity from bootstrap.
review_cons: The company can terminate training at any time and withhold the security deposit (1.15L). They offer a high salary package which is a myth. Freshers are required to enroll in the company's own training certification course and pay a 1.15L security deposit. The contract for this course does not mention the CTC package, with promises only verbal. Upon completing training, HR denies promised packages due to lack of written proof. Training takes double the promised time (e.g., 4.5 months became 8.5 months). Trainees often sit idle as employees are busy with projects. They are placed in on-project training, doing employee work for free while also needing to complete training sessions and tasks. HR expects trainees to complete tasks after 9-hour shifts and on weekends/holidays. The company is essentially a fraud, seeking security deposits and free labor.

review_pros: A lot of freedom to try new things.
review_cons: Overwork, poor communication, and a blame culture.

review_pros: Flexibility to work from wherever, whenever.
review_cons: Inexperienced leaders with inflated job titles and big egos. Retention of poor performers, often by creating new roles for them. Unsolicited feedback on candidates and back-channeling are common. Equity and DEI are buzzwords; favoritism is applied to promotional opportunities, with exceptions made for those connected to CPO/CHRO, and high turnover among diverse employees is a red flag. Leaders retaliate by dissecting low engagement results to identify individuals rather than taking accountability. Lack of respect for boundaries, with common communication during time off. Lack of true strategic growth mindset, leading to reactive and inefficient processes. The company is chaotic and dysfunctional for its size and post-IPO status.

review_pros: Provides a paycheck with benefits, though benefits worsen annually.
review_cons: This organization is not suitable for those who are good at what they do, take pride in self-improvement, and want to expand their skill set. The interview process misrepresents the company as progressive and community-focused, but it is a money grab to enrich upper management, despite being a non-profit with limited income sources. Upper management distances itself from employees, showing no respect for their talents or professional opinions. The staff is unhappy, and the culture is toxic, with a vindictive attitude towards employees who leave, making them unsuitable as references. When employees leave, positions are often not replaced, and other departments absorb the workload without salary changes. Raises or title changes are given to only a select few, with little upward mobility. The CEO micromanages everyone, hindering department heads and making decisions only when pushed, often rejecting others' ideas only to reintroduce them as his own.

review_pros: Nice building, free food, and met some nice people.
review_cons: Leadership is awful and seems to emulate Dave Ramsey.

review_pros: A lot of freedom in decision-making and interesting features/initiatives.
review_cons: Very slow development of features. Interesting features are rarely released, mostly remaining in the conceptual stage.

summary: Horrible job with non-stop entitled customers.
review_pros: Work from home.
review_cons: No venting allowed and no privacy; private conversations are monitored, and employees are asked to report others for negativity. No breaks between customers. Employees are treated like children, and management makes excuses for not raising pay. The environment is toxic.

summary: Broken Healthcare System at its lowest
review_pros: I very much wish I could muster something positive from my experience at SH, but I can not.
review_cons: Employees are treated as mere numbers, and patients are viewed solely as sources of revenue. While amazing employees care for others, this is not encouraged and often leads to reprimand for not following policy. Stress levels are high, work-life balance is nonexistent, employees are undervalued, and nepotism in upper management is blatant.

summary: So much potential, so little realization
review_pros: Great colleagues, though their numbers are reducing. Opportunities generally exist for progression, but only if one fits in.
review_cons: Frustration due to constant mismanagement and restructuring. Uncertainty about whether the CEO is aware of ongoing issues.

summary: Upper management doesn't hire enough people, leading to unsafe conditions for people and dogs.
review_pros: Dogs. I still think of all my dog friends I made while working there for two years.
review_cons: Dog fights occur due to management allowing aggressive dogs into already full parks. The company is understaffed, with handlers sometimes left with over 60 dogs per shift, forcing them to choose between lunch or leaving dogs unattended.
The owner is a sexual predator who gropes staff members. The office has CCTVs everywhere, creating a prison-like feeling of constant surveillance. Bullying is regular, and partners ignore it.

review_pros: Flexible working times and the opportunity to make a difference for kids.
review_cons: Employees are treated as disposable numbers.

review_pros: Profit-sharing and Christmas bonuses are offered, but both are based on time served, requiring at least 5 years of tenure to be significant.
review_cons: Micromanagement. The company brainwashes employees with a culture that is not genuinely lived. It cares more about numbers than employees. Horrible health insurance plan. The company only cares about employees when they are beneficial, and once they mention leaving, they are treated as if they never existed. A dedicated Culture Executive gathers employee information to inform the Senior Team, leading to excessive gossip. High turnover means training is rushed to make new hires "beneficial" quickly.

review_pros: Employees report that benefits are good.
review_cons: The workplace resembles middle school, and has worsened significantly in the past two years. Understaffing due to toxicity leaves new employees untrained and confused, or current employees changing positions to figure out their jobs independently. Employees are overworked and overwhelmed, with management adding stress through negative comments instead of providing training or support. The CEO's stated support is merely words, as she allows toxicity to continue by ignoring it. Negativity is out of control.

review_pros: Free food, fun coworkers, sometimes interesting customers
review_cons: Overworked and underappreciated. Requesting time off doesn't work because you will still be scheduled. You will be expected to work your station as well as others.

review_pros: Opportunity to meet people in the industry
review_cons: Most conversations are fake. Sales techniques are used to make you feel good. There is a lack of sincerity. The company is too old school, and management lacks a forward-looking vision.

summary: Awful work environment and terrible consideration for employees
review_pros: Good pay, easy job, okay hours, easy to commute
review_cons: Terrible managers. Systems that don't work. Unfairly strict with breaks; employees are called into HR for being 40 seconds late.

summary: Bad pay, bad working conditions, and no opportunities
review_pros: Opportunity to play and test the latest software
review_cons: Consistent hours or days are not guaranteed. The work schedule is sporadic and chaotic with little to no warning about upcoming shifts or projects. Apart from senior permanent staff, all other staff members are required to hot-desk, even during the pandemic. All staff members are expected to perform duties above their pay grade or level with no guarantee of promotions or pay raises, only a title change.

review_pros: Client-facing opportunities, can polish soft skills
review_cons: Only market research, not consulting. Mundane work.

review_pros: Work was fun at times
review_cons: Micromanagement at its finest. Employees are treated like children.

summary: Discriminatory performance evaluation practices against foreign employees
advice_to_management: Stop discriminatory practices against foreign hires. Get rid of the bell curve that was promised to be removed two years ago. Promote a meritocracy-based promotion system rather than the seniority-based legacy system. Conduct full 360 evaluations with more than 4 stakeholders evaluating people, instead of the current 2-evaluator system, which is effectively a one-evaluator system.
review_pros: Tons of company resources, great benefits, amazing infrastructure
review_cons: The company lures foreign recruits to their Korea HQ with competitive salaries but does not disclose a double standard for performance evaluations. This lack of transparency means foreigners are evaluated at a higher standard due to their higher incomes. If foreigners are expected to meet higher standards due to higher pay, they should be given higher positions aligned with their salaries to deliver on those expectations. This is discriminatory, and their bait-and-switch practice is questionable and incredibly unfair.

summary: Amazing company that I was proud to work for.
advice_to_management: Recognize that employees are not just numbers; they are partners who can bring greater success if allowed to create.
review_pros: Amazing IP, products, and benefits. Competitive pay. Opportunity to work with the best in the industry.
review_cons: Managers focused only on their own careers. Massive communication issues between business sections. Reluctance or slowness to embrace competitive, cutting-edge technology and ideas in parks.

review_pros: Truly entrepreneurial-spirited company that knows how to pivot and seize opportunities. The reviewer worked there for 10 years, starting as employee #2, and saw it grow to over 800 employees and $70M in gross revenue. Left to pursue another entrepreneurial start-up.
review_cons: No cons. In 2019, the company brought in new equity partners and leadership, becoming more structured, which led the reviewer to leave for start-up organizations where they felt they could add more value. Remains in contact with current employees.

summary: Don't do it to your career
review_pros: They will throw a lot at you.
review_cons: It's not worth it. The CEO and two other directors are described as the worst people to work for. They give a great sales pitch during interviews, but after the honeymoon period, they overwork employees. Staff turnover for such a small company is staggering, with most employees not lasting a year. The CEO is manipulative and a bully, frequently undermining and screaming at people in meetings while the other two directors allow it. The CEO also treats female employees inappropriately, making and sending inappropriate remarks on multiple occasions. Potential interviewees are strongly recommended to investigate the company's claims about its business and success, as there is a lot of smoke and mirrors.

review_pros: You get to reflect a lot while killing yourself
review_cons: Stuck in the past with their operations.

summary: Company with the worst employee handling attitude.
review_pros: No pros for this company. Joining was the worst experience. The company teaches pathetic behavior towards employees, excels at providing fake reviews, and favoritism leads to poor team management.
review_cons: Worst manager. Management is poor at team management and providing increments. They ask for bonds if increments are needed. They don't know how to talk with experienced personnel, treating employees like puppets. Worst experience with HR and policies. All experienced developers have left the company.

review_pros: Friendly and helpful colleagues, strategic location, workload slightly lower than Big 4, good exposure to various audit engagements in various industries.
review_cons: Low salary compared to Big 4 or other mid-tier audit firms. Average staff benefits. High staff turnover.

advice_to_management: Fire some managers. Morale is consistently low. Without the benefits, turnover would be huge. Get rid of the petty managers.
review_pros: Smart people, great to work with, and easy collaboration.
review_cons: Managers are punitive, petty, and draconian.

advice_to_management: Continue to build talent capital, as the company has a brand for employing the best and brightest. Avoid falling prey to politics that have engulfed earlier competitors.
review_pros: Strong product leaders and mentors to learn from. They were diligent in teaching how to write good PRDs and craft user requirements.
review_cons: As a large company, customary politics are present, becoming transparent during performance review times (twice-annual promotion/bonus assessment).

summary: Nice place but needs to be bigger
advice_to_management: do your best
review_pros: Nice people and positive space
review_cons: Not very clean and needs improvement.

review_pros: Good work-life balance
review_cons: Low pay and lack of support.

summary: It's just like most Corporate America companies
advice_to_management: Put thought into hiring and layoffs, stop using old technology, pay people what they're worth, make people feel valued, and stop with cheesy and pointless trainings.
review_pros: Good benefits, health initiatives, nice facility, great gym. Worked for some really great people.
review_cons: Employees are just numbers and feel disposable. There are mass hirings and layoffs. The company is built to impress shareholders.

advice_to_management: Sell the company. The stock price was $40 ten years ago, they paid $27 billion for Guidant, and now the market cap is $9 billion, with the stock price at $5. If unhappy with the company, wait for a new CEO, as they are on their 4th CEO in the past 4+ years.
review_pros: Favorites are rewarded with bonuses and promotions. Opportunity to make a difference in patients' lives. Good commute (not in the city). Excellent cafeteria food. Free parking. Good culture for politicians.
review_cons: Management identifies 'scapegoats' early in their careers and blames them when projects fail, taking no accountability. Scapegoats are eventually put into HR corrective action and fired. Even steady performers are eventually laid off, as the company only wants to keep the top 35% of the workforce. Overtime is expected without compensation. Employees are expected to 'volunteer' for extra work, otherwise they receive a bad review. Favoritism, not objective performance, is the management mantra.

review_pros: Nice company with a good working environment.
review_cons: Very strict in nature and work type.

review_pros: Checks are paid on time. Department management has a good understanding of how to run a business and treat customers fairly.
review_cons: The company is not proactive; everything is reactive and requires many hoops for approval. Concierge and permitting are horrendous. Obtaining tools and materials is difficult. Gas cards and Pep Boys accounts often don't work, requiring employees to pay out of pocket with promised reimbursement. Many issues could be avoided with proactive planning. Management doesn't listen to most employees unless they are close with top cronies. Many egos run the company, concerned with appearance rather than tangible value for employees or customers. The company has potential but seems not to care, applying only temporary fixes and rewarding poor planning and forcing jobs done incorrectly rather than good work and knowledge.

review_pros: Decent benefits package right when hired.
review_cons: Upper management prioritizes getting new products in-house over solving problems with existing product lines. Employees must follow customer demands to keep them happy, rather than doing what's right for a successful production line.

review_pros: Great place for career progression. Would work there again.
review_cons: Some employees interfere without permission. A manager told the reviewer that the time for mourning her husband's passing had lasted long enough, and she had to hide her grief from him and others to avoid further reprimand.

review_pros: Performance bonus is decent.
review_cons: Low pay, despite claims of industry average. Employees are advised to negotiate. The company has an 'extremely boomer' mindset, with older employees distrusting millennials. Ideas are often rejected as 'unprofessional' or 'not corporate enough,' and management tends to use their own ideas after asking for suggestions. It's a cheap company with zero employee welfare; pantry options are limited to water, and even coffee costs money. Despite earning millions, they cannot afford simple snacks or drinks, contributing to high turnover. Overtime is expected without pay, and a $5 meal allowance for overtime in the CBD office is insufficient. The company is seen as a sinking ship; management needs to change its mindset to retain younger generations.

advice_to_management: Ensure raises are competitive to retain employees. Allow employees to take vacation without feeling like a burden. Hire more people to help with high caseloads.
review_pros: Full-time work-from-home. Super inclusive. Flexible start time up until 9:00 AM. Good amount of sick and holiday pay.
review_cons: New York Life wants employees to switch to working in the office. Work-life balance is very difficult due to high workload. Only one week of holiday pay can be used at a time; two-week vacations require special permission. No raise this year due to NYL merge.

review_pros: If you are part of the 'in crowd,' you can do anything you want.
review_cons: Cut-throat environment where everyone will throw you under the bus.

review_pros: Some of the people were nice.
review_cons: LFG exaggerated expectations during hiring. Training and support were lacking, and they did not reimburse for necessary study materials.

review_pros: There are none nothing is good about this job
review_cons: They sometimes do not pay. Work-life balance is nonexistent; you work more than you are at home. Pay is not worth it, and sometimes they do not pay for days worked.

summary: Good when an employee, bad when leaving
review_pros: They take care of employees. The team was good, leading to a positive experience at YASH.
review_cons: The notice period is 3 months long. No payment is given to employees after resignation, resulting in no pay for the entire 3 months. The full and final settlement statement is provided 15-20 days after the last day, and final payment takes another 20-25 days after formal acceptance, totaling 4 months without payment.

review_pros: Awesome coffee, great co-workers, discounted drinks.
review_cons: Employees must commit 3 to 6 months without vacation, as it's 'required.' One owner is described as a psychopath, while the other is kind. The unpleasant owner stares at employees from inside the store or across the street.

review_pros: Amazing clients, opportunity to build meaningful relationships. Recent pay increase for Case Managers made pay competitive. Manageable 40-hour work week; not expected to work excessive hours, nights, or weekends.
review_cons: No training whatsoever; the reviewer didn't meet their boss until the second week and was told to wait for a client. Super low employee morale and incredibly high turnover. LIHI makes no effort for retention, accepting that people leave quickly and then complaining about it. Gossipy/'mean girl' culture among upper management. Lack of transparency regarding supervision, leading to tense supervisory relationships. The new structure where property management supervises Case Managers is morally wrong and counterproductive; CMs are now expected to 'help' with evictions and rent collection, leading to feelings of punishment for advocating for tenants. Unclear expectations; written policies are nonexistent or inaccessible, and questions often go unanswered. Due to retention issues, they hire almost anyone, ignoring unqualified employees while targeting and punishing those who go above and beyond. General lack of compassion for the served population. Staff and supervisors talk about residents disrespectfully. The focus is on money; 'problematic' residents (addiction, mental health challenges) are targeted for eviction, leaving supportive services employees powerless. Armed security is present, and residents have been tased and verbally harassed, showing too much reliance on law enforcement for an organization claiming trauma-informed care. Each property feels isolated, with little interaction between staff from different locations, making the experience dependent on the assigned property.

review_pros: Lots of talented professionals to learn from. Management cares about employees. Flexible hours. Good career progression.
review_cons: Work can get hectic as release dates approach, which is considered normal. Salaries are not low but could be more competitive.

summary: Culture was the only positive aspect
review_pros: Some of the best people to work with. Good benefits. Good work technology. On-site gym.
review_cons: Poor salary and compensation structure. Political atmosphere. Bad place for Senior Sales Representatives. No regular salary adjustments.

review_pros: Good distribution and service area, good products.
review_cons: Poor leadership, changes in quotas, restrictive allocation to clients in need. HR is horrible and does not support employee concerns.

review_pros: Intent to drive work-life balance, which they do an okay job at.
review_cons: Lack of executive leadership creates confusion. The relationship between leadership and the Board of Governors is contentious.

summary: Great job, great staff, poor senior management choices
review_pros: Genuinely worthwhile job. Great colleagues. Great benefits. Great system for booking holidays and logging overtime. Great training. Hopefully, a flexible employer in the future, allowing good home-working (excellent during COVID).
review_cons: Clunky IT systems with many workarounds. Contradictory pressures to meet call completion and turnover targets versus doing the best for clients. Contradiction between emphasizing employee well-being and disciplinary measures for illness or absence. Poor opportunities for progression, as voiced by many longstanding employees; the charity tends to recruit externally for management roles.

company_name: Ogletree Deakins Nash Smoak and Stewart P C
review_pros: Standard benefits package, nice bonuses, kind coworkers.
review_cons: Very low pay. Management is controlling. More work than comparable firms. High turnover. Benefits could be more competitive. No merit-based promotions.

review_pros: New technology and ample parking.
review_cons: Too much 'dead wood' (underperforming employees) who are shifted among departments. Management and HR are aware but risk-averse, leading others to carry their workload.

review_pros: SHC has a good 401k match.
review_cons: The overall environment is stressful. Lab technicians in some facilities are poorly trained and many are unlicensed, with almost no consequences for errors. This is irresponsible and dangerous for patients.

review_pros: Great people, good opportunity for interns to learn many skills in a short amount of time.
review_cons: Company is run by interns, who are horribly underpaid. Bad management. Valuable people don't stay because post-internship offers are inadequate. Employees and interns are treated as enemies.

summary: Nice place to work, but with long hours
review_pros: Multicultural environment, opportunities for advancement and growth within.
review_cons: Long hours. Compensation is on the bottom half of the range. Schedule changes frequently.

review_pros: Good benefits available at the time. Occasional company lunches.
review_cons: Disorganized. High turnover. Unclear business goals. Favoritism. False promises. Shady business practices. Unprofessional HR team, with some members gossiping about private conversations.

summary: A non-profit with a lot of heart
review_pros: Community, work-life balance, global reach, longevity, diversity, friendly colleagues.
review_cons: Lower than average salaries. Silos. Slow to adopt new ideas. Lack of transparency.
Constant changes. The boss lacks direction. Plans last only a few days. The company always imagines itself as a high-tech company.

summary: Outdated management and shady business practices
review_pros: Young culture, fun coworkers.
review_cons: Only cares about gross profit, prioritizing patients with better insurance over Medicare patients. Management is unaware of day-to-day operations and provides no resources for success, yet expects hiring nurses while lowballing them. Grossly underpays during the 'training' period.

summary: Most staff last less than a year.
review_pros: Good mission and wonderful donors.
review_cons: Lower average pay, crazy hours, benefits could be better. Claimed 8-hour shifts often turn into 10-12 hours, 5 days a week.

review_pros: The people make the place worth working at.
review_cons: Management and the company do not care about people, including patients or workers.

advice_to_management: Raise commission or base salary to compete with other banks.
review_pros: Opportunity to learn a little bit of everything by doing various tasks.
review_cons: Responsible for everything, from teller work to management tasks.

review_pros: Freedom to conduct work at your own pace.
review_cons: Poor management, poor salary, no increments, heartless HR, all talk and no action.

review_pros: Working with seniors and older adults is rewarding, the only good aspect of employment with Sequoia Living.
review_cons: Toxic management that belittles POC staff. White privilege and ageism are deeply embedded in daily practices, treating young people as less than.
Employees are unfairly paid compared to other staffing companies. Management expects you to love your job and stay with the company forever.

summary: Company is great but needs some big changes.
review_pros: Transparent company, good attitude people, learned many things, great teamwork, amazing culture, love the office vibes.
review_cons: The new MD for the Thailand team is awful, lacks manners and empathy, and gossips about people. MD's planning skills are poor: never listens to teams, demanding without showing how to do tasks, complains often, disturbs work with new tasks, gives fake compliments, and sticks to ineffective plans. Her attitude towards the organization shows 100% focus on work and 0% on people; she overworks staff with no mercy and has zero supporting team skills. She is smart but not professional, with zero leadership skills. She judges others' work without reason and is close-minded to listening to those who know better.

review_pros: Pre-IPO bank based in San Francisco.
review_cons: Varo, despite calling itself a fintech, operates like a penny-pinching, slow-moving regional bank. While it claims over 4 million customers, only a small portion are daily active, with most accounts having double-digit or zero balances. Targeting unbanked and low-income segments is noble, but this segment is expensive and unprofitable for banks. Varo generates revenue through ATM fees, effectively "leeching" from poor people, and is unlikely to become profitable, relying on VC money. Unlike other new banks that focus on growth before charters, Varo spent over $125 million on a bank charter, which now makes it expensive to operate due to regulations. It compares itself to SOFI, valuing its stock at $7.88, but SOFI is 8 times larger and profitable, with its stock at $6.5 (Aug 2022). Money Lion, 3 times larger, is at $1.25. The reviewer believes Varo is a penny stock. Furthermore, Varo's diversity and inclusion efforts are only for marketing; the reviewer observed a lack of people of color in leadership (CEO, CPO, CMO) during their 6 months there, describing it as a "complete whitewash" despite some Chinese and Indian employees.

summary: Lives up to its 'sweat shop' reputation.
review_pros: Some cool clients, opportunity to learn quickly with the right manager, and good digital offerings.
review_cons: Incredibly top-heavy environment, understaffed at lower levels. Opportunities seem to lessen with progression. Promised opportunities, like working abroad, are harder to achieve than stated. Training and development are not as promised. Expectations are too high, leading to no work-life balance and very high stress. Management is stingy and disregards the well-being of junior staff. Not recommended for anyone at SAE level or below.

summary: Pay is good, but management is not.
review_pros: Good pay, including hourly wage and commission. The product often sells itself.
review_cons: Commission can bring out the worst in people. Management failed to maintain fairness, allowing some employees to stay in high-volume sales areas all day without fair rotation.

review_pros: Each program office at DOE has its own culture; some are much better than others. Research thoroughly to avoid heartache.
review_cons: Some managers are extremely hostile and disrespectful towards veterans.

review_pros: Good money can be made remotely if you work hard.
review_cons: After 3 years without catching COVID, I became ill on a Thursday. On the following Friday, I sent an email that was apparently not received or responded to. I was then locked out of my work email. Three days later, I received an email to my private account instructing me to return all equipment.

summary: A place to work only if absolutely necessary; otherwise, avoid it.
review_pros: The company has a large portfolio of products to sell.
review_cons: Toxic work environment fostered by the owners, who show zero respect for employees. Antiquated systems provide no clear, accurate reporting for sales or inventory. Owners focus only on the top 2-3 retailers, neglecting others' shipping performance. Management hierarchy is not followed or respected, contributing to the toxic environment; only the three brothers' opinions matter, making position titles meaningless. HR is completely ineffective, acting as a mouthpiece for the owners and doing nothing to improve the culture.

review_pros: Fast-paced, casual workplace (if preferred), opportunity for job title advancement, and access to extremely knowledgeable individuals for learning.
review_cons: Slow career growth, with job title promotions not equating to pay increases. Poor support from upper management for team leads. The company hires indiscriminately, leading to many unqualified people and difficulties in closing loans. Temp agencies also fail to vet candidates properly.

summary: Worst place to work; better to work at KFC.
review_cons: 300€ monthly pay and one banana a month.

review_pros: Getting to know the residents.
review_cons: Lazy, low-skilled, and untrained workers are handing out medications to residents. PSWs run the entire building, while management sits in the office all day. The environment will destroy your mental health.

No work-life balance, minimal pay increases, and lack of proper training. Upper management is out of touch with processes, and there is no loyalty to employees. The company dictates how employees should answer their reviews.

summary: Fantastic work, mission-driven team, fast-paced environment.
review_pros: Wonderful people to work with, great benefits, and a very mission-focused team.
review_cons: Job security is lacking (though transparently communicated upfront), and the environment is very fast-paced, requiring employees to start at a sprint.

summary: If I could give 0/5 stars, I would.
review_pros: Easy workload, normal retail duties, and entertaining customers.
review_cons: Low pay, poor management, communication issues, minimal benefits, limited growth opportunities, and small-minded superiors (including corporate).

advice_to_management: Should adopt a 5-day working culture.
review_cons: Salary structure is too low.

summary: A true multinational company in shape and size.
advice_to_management: It is difficult to advise management when the business model is extremely profitable, as the current 'way of doing things' is perpetuated due to validated results.
review_pros: Exposure to an exciting technological environment. Colleagues have varied experience and backgrounds, with multinational teams requiring mastery of conference calls. Nice perks include a company car and insurance. Team events are held in nice locations, indicating no budget issues. Salary was good for the position, though not extraordinary.
review_cons: The company is technically ill-equipped for staff to perform tasks satisfactorily; budgets are prepared in convoluted Excel files. There's no investment or thought put into evolving the finance function to match company aspirations. Finance teams partner with a US-based support accounting team, leading to difficulties in correcting errors as they have different management and are unresponsive. No technical training is provided for staff. The actual role differed from what was presented during the interview, leading to unmet expectations. The environment is slightly toxic, with office politics consuming much time and energy. Staff have a dual reporting line to their finance leader and country manager, creating conflict.

advice_to_management: Bring back mid-sized sedans; with COVID-19 here to stay, businesses will want company cars that don't share rides (for social distancing) and are electric.
review_pros: Most supportive group of people. They help you stay aware of your contribution and its impact on the company.
review_cons: Due to volatility in the auto industry, it's a lean company, meaning no coffee, snacks, or administrative help; employees are self-reliant. This was a shock initially, but the reviewer respects the ingenuity.

advice_to_management: Need to hold managers accountable for correctly and fully utilizing staff resources.
review_pros: Challenging technical environment, with lots to learn and interesting technology.
review_cons: No oversight of mid-level managers. If resources are not properly allocated, staff are fired. There is no support for the substantial learning curve, and mentoring is a joke.

summary: Nice people, but work-life balance was negligible.
review_pros: Great community; everyone is super nice and often went out for lunch, with many company-wide events.
review_cons: Often, I found myself working very late hours due to pressure from supervisors to stay.

advice_to_management: Do not assume everyone who works there is stupid, and have more sympathy for your workers.
review_pros: Decent pay for the job.
review_cons: Management was not good at all.

review_pros: Coworkers were a delight to work with.
review_cons: Management's favoritism can bring the company down.

review_pros: Good training, great software, and nice people.
review_cons: 50-75% travel (potentially higher pre-COVID). Opportunities for progression might be tenure-based rather than ability-based.

summary: Looked like a dream company, but ended up a nightmare.
company_name: IMD International Institute for Management Development
review_pros: Great location, great employee benefits (though decreasing), amazing micro-management-free direct supervisor, opportunity to interact with world-class specialists, above-average salary, and good work-life balance.
review_cons: Complete disconnection between C-levels and market reality. Several incompetent 'head of' and managers are never challenged by the board due to cronyism. Constant internal power/political wars make it the most political company the reviewer has worked for. Most departments work in silos and dislike collaborating. Overall green and pink washing. A 'boomer mindset' with old white males acting as know-it-alls. Colleagues frequently go on sick leave due to burnout, indicating total HR mismanagement. It's hard to understand how such a decaying company remains successful; they are very lucky.
I was excited to start, as it looked great from the outside. However, General Managers are angry bullies. Staff turnover is incredibly high, like a turnstile.

summary: Company trending downwards in many areas.
advice_to_management: Get rid of terrible people managers instead of retaining them and laying off competent employees.
review_pros: Large company in San Diego with intelligent, hardworking employees. Support is given to employee groups interested in different areas.
review_cons: Leadership has been trending downwards for a long time. There are an incredible number of terrible managers across the company; some are incompetent, and at worst, some are downright toxic and awful. The company somehow values this.

review_pros: The CEO balances work and life well for employees and recently hired good people for the Senior Leadership Team. Care for parents during COVID was great. The pace of innovation is excellent, providing many talking points with customers. Elastic offers health cover benefits, but no benefits like gym or salary sacrifice.
review_cons: Multiple APAC leadership positions have been vacant for 9 months; management lacks contacts or a talent list for recruitment. There's no Customer Success function for most regional customers, yet one person gets paid for all your work. Poor collaboration between teams, with many doing minimal work. Sales teams lack support due to a strong focus on a remote distributed workforce, leading to a weak team culture. Many people focus on themselves and KPIs rather than customer or company goals. Teams like order ops/deal desk are unhelpful, dictating sales approaches without understanding actual sales. No HR in the region for one year. Strong ultra-left leaning ideology.

review_pros: Smart and capable people in the team.
review_cons: Lack of visibility over future career within the firm.

review_pros: Enjoyed management's hands-off approach, which allowed for creative exploration of solutions. The company does not adhere to a 'one size fits all' mentality.
review_cons: High and extremely involved workload.

review_pros: Enjoyed working here and would love to return. It's a genuinely good team environment where everyone works together and looks out for each other. Supervisors were nice, helpful, and genuinely interested in reducing stress. Pay was fair, and coworkers were excellent, making days more fun.
review_cons: Very limited hours, with most professors only given part-time hours, which is insufficient for sustenance; many coworkers had multiple jobs. Not ideal for advancement opportunities, as promotions are very rare (though the reviewer was one of the few promoted). The company generally prefers to hire student workers through their work-study program, which the reviewer finds 'scummy'.

advice_to_management: Ensure delivery is done on time, reward clients who outperform, and provide incentives to the sales group.
review_pros: One-on-one meetings with clients, follow-up on sales and sales improvement, creation of new client base, direct meeting of client expectations, and solving logistic issues.
review_cons: Delays in deliveries and non-payment/bounced checks.

summary: Great place to work with strong ethics, committed to developing people and leading the future of the payments industry.
advice_to_management: Keep pushing to get everyone focused on bigger opportunities. Visa is a great place to work and is on the right track for future improvement.
review_pros: Solid Senior Management empowers people, recognizes achievements, and tolerates mistakes for learning. Offers competitive compensation and benefits package, with many growth opportunities.
review_cons: In constant transformation, which requires a high level of adaptability from employees.

review_pros: Benefits, competitive wage, and some nice people.
review_cons: Literally terrible work environment. Only 5 sick days per year; subsequent illnesses require a doctor's note to avoid a write-up. Stats and bonuses are entirely sales-dependent, even for loyalty/customer care roles. They negatively track callbacks within one week and cancellations within 60 days, as well as agent-initiated cancellations. The company prioritizes phone sales. Failure to meet stats results in a 'performance plan' or termination, despite stats supposedly being for bonus encouragement. This pressure leads agents to lie about phones and plans, causing later escalations and callbacks. It's a horrible system.

advice_to_management: Learn your position and do not punish employees for trying to help others.
review_pros: Pay, hours, benefits, and flexibility of shifts.
review_cons: Managers are untrained and largely clueless about procedures.

advice_to_management: Thank you for your support
review_pros: Treats employees well, and politics were minimal.
review_cons: Too many projects happening at once.

advice_to_management: Keep up the good work
review_pros: Great pay and very good benefits.
Most people are great to work with. The owner does good things and is good to her employees.
review_cons: I requested time off for my daughter's wedding, but was told I could have used PTO or sick time, which I had. Legally, I could have left because the email only specified working on Saturday, not Friday afternoon. An employee sleeping on the job was never addressed, despite handbook rules. This indicates favoritism. The AR department's handling of accounts could cause 3 major companies to leave 84 Lumber, posing a huge problem. There should also be an option to respond after a review. The reviewer claims to have proof.

review_pros: Free food and great friends.
review_cons: This place shows no care for students, who are treated as disposable despite being essential to the company's existence. They overwork students, disregard their school schedules, and expect 110% effort daily. Upper management is completely unprofessional but expects employees to be professional, which is insane.

review_pros: Genuinely customer-focused legal team. Direct team had a great culture and goals. Tabcorp was genuine in harm-reduction and doing the right thing. CEO is well-regarded amongst the team. Above-average remuneration.
review_cons: Poor leadership within the legal department, with team leaders not receiving necessary support from the Group General Counsel (GGC). The GGC was erratic and operated as a 'lone wolf' rather than supporting the team. Tabcorp leadership was unsupportive during COVID, prioritizing company finances over team well-being. Lack of career development, with outside talent preferred. Outdated systems and processes led to burnout and poor work-life balance. Work was heavily focused on 'putting out fires'.

review_pros: PTO, work from home, and Summer Fridays.
review_cons: Management, HR, culture, and pressure from leaders.

review_pros: The pay is the only good thing.
review_cons: Too much work, no time for family.

advice_to_management: Work on salary hikes and reward employees for their contributions.
review_pros: The company supported employees and their dependents during COVID by arranging oxygen, beds, and other necessities. They also offered the option to receive the next 6 months' salary in advance for those affected by COVID, showing great support.
review_cons: The job role experience depends on the team; not all teams are good. My team lead doesn't even know basic tasks like 'git push' and dumps work on me if he can't do it. Leads enjoy their jobs like government employees. I've received no support from him regarding the codebase; instead, I guide him. Salary hikes are very low, a common complaint. No goodies or perks are provided. Promotions are annual, unlike some companies with quarterly promotions.

review_pros: Some may find it beneficial not to have a strong company culture, allowing one to hide, which is good for introverts.
review_cons: Geographically fragmented teams (developers from India, US, and EU) are not co-located, leading to constant timezone issues. The company lacks processes and is driven by micromanagement. No one cares about code quality or maintainability; the attitude is to get things done as soon as possible. Few actual seniors were encountered, mostly juniors and mid-level developers, even if they called themselves senior. Scrum/Agile methodologies are not taken seriously; the reviewer had no retrospective meeting for a year. Competent employees will be frustrated by the low-quality code produced.

review_pros: Opportunity to meet many people and make friends. Gain experience in various aspects in and out of the warehouse. Surprisingly great healthcare options.
review_cons: Everything else is negative. The company website portrays a false image of robots, clean warehouses, and friendly faces. In reality, there are no robots, forklifts are old and falling apart, politics are astounding, and pay is not competitive. Favoritism is rampant; if you're not a favorite, knowledge and experience don't lead to advancement. Employees work 50-60 hours a week until burnout. Entire project management and warehouse teams have resigned or found other jobs.

review_pros: Fantastic pay, flexible with school schedule, and generally enjoyable.
review_cons: Self-checkout work and refusal to grant time off.

review_pros: Learn a lot due to high demands.
review_cons: Bad culture.

advice_to_management: Assign individuals with a proper balance of collaboration/communication skills and long-term vision. Avoid assigning non-people persons with poor professional reputations who cannot delegate due to lack of trust, as this ensures failure of major projects. Ensure tasked employees don't suffer from extreme tunnel vision and understand that stakeholder engagement and following enterprise-wide methodologies/processes are essential for positive change management.
review_pros: Paid handsomely to do absolutely nothing for several months. Sat next to some of the nicest Capital Market Technology PM ladies. Self-initiated study led to vast knowledge of US and Canadian regulations, particularly the Dodd-Frank Act, which will be useful for future contracts. Hours were quite stable.
review_cons: Hired as a senior Project Manager for a massive, company-wide US regulatory initiative, with promises of significant work and travel. Despite daily requests for work, received little to no direction, and was reprimanded for proactive efforts. Manager and program manager lacked collaboration and communication skills; the program manager was particularly unmethodical, disorganized, and openly hostile in meetings. The project group faced constant criticism. After several months, was let go for 'not meeting work requirements'.

review_pros: Small enough to make an impact.
review_cons: Poor leadership and lack of respect.

summary: Not a good company to work for. Fake positive comments exist because management asks people to write them.
review_pros: Some good people work there, and a company van is provided.
review_cons: Stressful blame culture. Lack of diversity respect: during furlough, the reviewer was made to use 10 holidays, and when explaining they use holidays to visit family abroad, was told it was company policy. Fake promises to retain employees. Poor management constantly blames ground staff. Poor inter-departmental communication causes serious problems. Heavy workload.

summary: Horrible experience stemming from upper management and owners.
review_cons: Fear of rats, with over 20 in the warehouse. Absolutely no room for growth; managers keep employees doing mindless, repetitive tasks. Direct managers are not supportive and take credit to impress the owner. Nothing to learn, as most upper managers lack Excel skills or education. Pay is not even remotely close to industry averages. Proposals for company improvement are ignored. Managers berate and call employees names, and often browse phones instead of working. False promises of career advancements, wage increases, and work-from-home options. Widespread gossip throughout the toxic company, originating from upper management. Managers refute past promises by twisting words. Forced office culture with frequent desk rearrangements and obnoxious techno music played for 5+ hours. Micromanagement even for elementary tasks. Extremely high turnover. Managers consistently choose options that decline revenue due to lack of experience. Air conditioners were never on to save costs, making the warehouse and office like ovens even above 30 degrees.

summary: Handles entire McDelivery Teams of Jeddah, KSA.
advice_to_management: Be fair and equal to all nationalities.

review_cons: Racism, cultural indifferences, long hours of duty, and no incentives for my position.

review_pros: Good coworkers and benefit package.

review_pros: A lot of hours. Easy to call for help during training.

advice_to_management: Management acts like a bunch of kids running around playing boss.

She was reported multiple times by me and other drivers. It got to the point that many drivers would elect not to do extra work and just do a single 3-4 trip run to avoid dealing with her on her shifts.

summary: Not all that shines green is pleasant.

Distressing redundancy process (8-minute call, told to shut laptop, no further human contact). CEO believes culture is fantastic, but attrition rates are over 75% for employees and 110% for drivers. Logistics management is uninterested in people, paying self-employed drivers only for planned hours (e.g., 10 hours for a 13-hour route). Company encourages happy employees to write good reviews to combat negative feedback. Lack of trust in employees, evidenced by changes to hybrid working policy (from any 2 days to specific 3 days in office).

advice_to_management: Assess knowledge and ensure meaningful directives and strategies are implemented from the top down.

review_cons: Extremely meeting-heavy culture, inadequate staff and resources. Executive leadership gives directives that don't make sense, causing employees to suffer.

advice_to_management: A very good opportunity to learn and grow your professional career.

advice_to_management: Give young people more opportunities.

The company is heading towards command and control, which will likely worsen now that competition is getting serious after the pandemic. Hiring and recruitment is a trainwreck. It's a software product company but there is little to no value placed on quality engineering at scale, with multiple layers of patchwork and no incentives to clean up. It's a badly run feature factory. Everything is considered important and urgent, which means nothing truly is. The company is growing rapidly but not managing those changes effectively. summary: Cool place, cool people, growing fast, lots you can learn.
review_pros: I worked as an Engineer at Everlywell for over 3.5 years and thoroughly enjoyed my time there. I witnessed the company's significant growth and appreciated its consistent transparency through all-hands meetings, reports, and announcements. This transparency provided high exposure to business operations, relevant KPIs and metrics across departments, and key strategies for achieving aggressive goals, which was very educational. This understanding allows teams a high degree of autonomy in delivering work; teams know what data is relevant for success and are encouraged to determine how to achieve it, making work more rewarding and interesting. Teams are free to use appropriate methodologies and can change them if they don't work. While there have been challenges, the company actively seeks feedback ("What can we do better? What's working? What isn't working?") and acts on it. There are many avenues for employees to voice opinions, including anonymous and public Q&A, 1-on-1s, surveys, and polls. The tech stack is modern and enjoyable, with an effective CI/CD pipeline simplifying deployments and preview environments. They utilize feature flags, A/B testing, a Design Language System, interaction metrics, automated testing, observability, and real user monitoring, offering many opportunities for hands-on experience with relevant technologies. Everlywell strives to keep employees engaged, especially during the pandemic, through DEI ERGs and the Product + Engineering Culture Committee, offering valuable activities. The company acknowledges global events, fostering a sense of humanity. Even fully remote, I felt engaged and part of a team without it feeling forced. Overall, Everlywell is a great place to work with cool people, learn a lot, and make a big impact. Additional Pros include unlimited PTO (never an issue getting time off), company match for charitable donations, free tests, an educational stipend, and full-time remote work.
review_cons: There can be many meetings, though the CEO encourages declining those without an agenda. A strong emphasis on OKRs and goal setting, while beneficial for career growth, creates additional administrative work, making it hard to focus on other tasks. Minimizing context switching has been challenging due to the fast pace and being pulled in multiple directions, impacting focus. Attending meetings with cameras on is heavily encouraged, which can be a minor annoyance for some. Teams tend to change frequently, making it difficult to move past the forming stage and achieve predictability, a symptom of fast growth. The sheer volume of ongoing work can be overwhelming, requiring a constant balancing act, though there has been a positive shift towards more focused team directions recently. Most of these cons are likely common in fast-growing companies, but Everlywell is noted for its efforts to smooth out these unavoidable challenges.
