review_pros: It was a great opportunity to network.
review_cons: The online internships were not great.

advice_to_management: Australian Management should intervene to remove micromanagement by implementing regular anonymous feedback.
review_pros: Brand name is the only pro.
review_cons: Indian management is readily converting this company into a micromanaged startup, contrasting with the Australian approach. Employees were directly or indirectly forced to attend the office during COVID times.

summary: Worst organization I worked for, just like a local 'lala' company (small, unprofessionally run business).
review_pros: Initially, they offer a good salary.
review_cons: Employees have to work for at least three people. High work pressure and a challenging work-life balance. HR is merely for namesake. Managers run their own policies and behave as if they are paying from their own pocket. Employees need to work around 12 hours every day. Most managers have been there a long time and are very loyal to their own managers, often being 'yes-men'. Project deadlines are unrealistic, with upper management setting random dates that managers never challenge. To meet these deadlines, employees often work weekends; the reviewer worked 3-4 months on weekends in a year. Many employees were fired last year.

review_pros: Good pay and excellent benefits.
review_cons: Too many hours, not enough time off.

review_pros: The work culture is pretty good; seniors will always help you in every step you take.
review_cons: When you don't achieve satisfactory conversion rates (which is difficult regardless of pitch quality), team leaders will treat you poorly.

advice_to_management: Treat people with respect.
review_pros: The only good thing about the place is the good wage.
review_cons: They treat employees like robots, crazy, and with no respect.

summary: Game of Thrones politics, with a 'baby culture' catching up fast.
review_pros: Opportunity to add the company name to your CV.
review_cons: Pathetic projects; finding a project that aligns with your professional goals is harder than finding an external job. Employees do all sorts of useless things to impress, but these have no industry relevance. Poor pay. Advised to stay a few years and then move on.

advice_to_management: Stop moving jobs out of the USA.
review_cons: There have been continual layoffs since 2013. The company is shifting all technical jobs to the Philippines.

review_pros: Excellent benefits, good salary, and annual bonuses.
review_cons: Too much stress due to monthly targets.

advice_to_management: Be better.
review_pros: Pretty much nothing, aside from the nice building location in Docklands.
review_cons: Literally no one cares about you. They dangle the promise of a contract during induction like a carrot on a stick, only to make you work like a donkey. People are fired frequently, like a reality TV show, with no explanation or follow-up.

advice_to_management: Listen to those who need to be heard. Actually listen.
review_pros: Met amazing people, especially on shipdock. Work was easy.
review_cons: Management became too strict and judgmental, leading to discomfort.

advice_to_management: Keep trying to reduce the adversarial relationship with the unions.
review_pros: Good pay and benefits.
review_cons: Working with the union and conflicting interdepartmental goals.

review_pros: Heavy lifting in bad conditions.
review_cons: Bad conditions with heavy lifting.

summary: Work-life balance and job security are very poor.
advice_to_management: Improve work-life balance and provide associates with job security.
review_pros: Adequate leaves are available. Salary and benefits are very good compared to other companies.
review_cons: Job security and work-life balance are very poor.

advice_to_management: Restructure departments for a more flexible hierarchy that listens to employees.
review_pros: Recognized name, spacious premises, correct hours.
review_cons: No challenge, tedious and redundant tasks, favoritism, uninteresting colleagues, low salary.

advice_to_management: Invest in soft skills, understand the science, elevate workers, don't single out employees, understand the challenges women face in male-oriented occupations, and allow for more flexible behavioral expectations.
review_pros: Benefits, 401K matching, office campus, training, resources, employee networks, internships, safety training, software, on-the-spot awards, fire drills, field trips, technology, gym, ergonomics, HSE, safety culture.
review_cons: Poor management lacking soft skills, ineffective HR support, poor work-life balance, lack of diversity awareness in STEM roles, no onsite childcare, scrutinized paternity leave.
Low salary, poor administration, poor management, lack of listening in relationships and business.

advice_to_management: Provide longer breaks and clarify who the manager is for each shift.
review_pros: Good pay rate compared to others, great bonus.
review_cons: Desire for longer breaks.

review_pros: Good salary with perks (fitness, yoga); new, open-minded culture.
review_cons: Company reorganization every 3 years.

advice_to_management: Offer more healthy food options.
review_pros: Great flexibility.
review_cons: Insufficient pay, leading to pursuit of other opportunities.

review_pros: Good health benefits and good colleagues.
review_cons: Difficult shift times.

summary: Warning: The next round of layoffs are coming.
advice_to_management: Profit motives benefit shareholders but not hard-working employees. Consider cost-of-living raises.
review_pros: Paid every two weeks, decent PTO.
review_cons: Frequent layoffs (RIFs) every 6 months, age discrimination, and retaliation for unfavorable employee engagement survey ratings.

advice_to_management: Recognize talent to prevent attrition.
review_pros: Inclusive culture, internal advancement opportunities, learning opportunities, and work-life balance.
review_cons: Low pay and small raises, requiring rehire for better compensation. Pay needs to be negotiated at the time of hire.

summary: Garbage place, CEO is absolutely nuts.
review_cons: Bad pay, no benefits, insane hours, CEO fires people frequently, CEO tries to ruin perceived threats.

review_pros: Good for resume building.
review_cons: Company encouraged fake positive reviews. Employees are unpaid, management is abusive, and personal time is not respected. Not an enjoyable place to work or build a career.

summary: Staff were dropped without warning when COVID-19 hit.
review_pros: Relaxed office environment, friendly coworkers, lower management attempted to modernize testing approaches (automated testing, agile), a few good individuals.
review_cons: Upper management halted progress, created an environment of apathy, and laid off many staff without warning during COVID-19, despite prior moves towards remote work.

advice_to_management: Avoid temporary employment trends, cutting benefits, and reducing team building for cost savings.
review_pros: Good benefits, annual reviews with raises, good health coverage with reasonable medication costs, encouraged team building, continuing education, and employee growth.
review_cons: Cuts in employee benefits, bureaucratic processes, and time-consuming tasks. A fair amount of representative management.

summary: Opportunities to advance and learn new skills. It was fun initially, but leadership was lacking.
review_pros: Great vacation accruals and health plan when I started.
review_cons: Changes over time with new managers, unkept promises. Advise to get things in writing.

review_pros: Good company that contributes positively to the community.
review_cons: Chaotic processing system, reliance on external colleagues to do their job.

review_pros: Good health benefits, Monday-Friday schedule, beautiful community and town.
review_cons: Required to work every holiday. Terrible management, employees do as they please. Lack of communication. HR prioritizes management and company interests over employees. High management turnover leads to constantly changing rules and confusion. Stressful environment due to weekly schedule confusion, rude and racist residents. Lack of support from HR and clueless management. Feeling lost and unappreciated.

summary: Small fish in a big pond.
advice_to_management: Listen to employees.
review_pros: Good brand for CV, exposure to rigid processes.
review_cons: Lack of feeling valued as an individual.

advice_to_management: Provide better resources for heavy loads.
review_pros: No customer interaction.
review_cons: Consistently lifting 100-pound boxes, not just small orders.

review_pros: Travel opportunities, fantastic co-workers, opportunity to learn from smart business owners, work-from-home opportunities.
review_cons: CEO stated staff should accept non-profit pay or leave, despite CEO's high salary. Staff are paid below industry average, receive no raises, and stipends don't cover work-from-home expenses. Staff are overworked and verbally abused by members without consequence. Organization underappreciates and undertrains staff while spending millions on lavish events for member leaders. Little room for career growth.

summary: If you like being told how horrible you are at what you do, this is the place for you.
review_pros: Great, hard-working, and dedicated coworkers.
review_cons: Underappreciated and underpaid unless in management. Zero positive reinforcement. Ineffective training, only mistakes are highlighted. Horrible morale, leading to self-doubt. Unrealistic expectations, requiring 2-3 full-time positions for one salary.

summary: Has its good and its bad.
review_pros: Good people, decent time off, great remote work policy (even before COVID-19).
review_cons: Limited upward mobility, low pay, out-of-touch management. Took on double responsibilities without increased pay or support when another role was eliminated without notice.

advice_to_management: Practice what you preach; address problems instead of eliminating whistleblowers. Examine supervisors and managers for issues. Recognize hard-working 3-10 year employees who keep the company afloat.
review_pros: Great benefits.
review_cons: HR management in Houston corporate office is ineffective. Some employees advanced by lying and undermining others. Complaints of harassment, bullying, and ethics violations are not investigated, with the company always siding with management. Managers claim employee work and success. Promotions are promised but rarely materialize, as supervisors keep employees down to make themselves look good. Dedicated employees were laid off when oil prices slid, while overpaid, ineffective mid-level managers were retained.

advice_to_management: You are all too good :)
review_pros: Great working culture, very good reputation.
review_cons: Difficult to get hired due to tough selection criteria.

summary: Good company overall, but the tech team is disappointing.
review_pros: Great benefits, and the company outside of tech seemed well-organized. Unlimited PTO was nice, though some abused it. Be aware that development talent is paid below market rate, so negotiate pay carefully.
review_cons: Chaotic tech team with extremely high turnover, leading to constant team shuffling. Employees are not promoted despite good performance, while new hires receive higher salaries. Low productivity bar, making it suitable for less ambitious developers. Interview process misrepresented the actual role, which involved minimal coding and mostly Microsoft Dynamics work. Prospective employees should set low expectations and ask thorough questions during interviews.

summary: Terrible company; do not work here.
review_cons: Awful company with rude staff and exclusionary practices in two locations. Bullying of new staff, strict rules (e.g., water breaks), and inappropriate manager behavior (pushed cake in mouth). Caused severe work anxiety.

summary: Seems like a dream job, then you start working there.
review_pros: Generally cool colleagues and awesome office space.
review_cons: Intensely toxic, negative culture with backstabbing and inappropriate comments. Lack of formal HR process. Difficult for non-male employees. Massive egos. No career growth opportunities. Terrible attrition rates.

review_pros: Some really smart and hard-working people work here. Efforts are being made towards DEI, though not entirely successfully.
review_cons: Toxic and messy environment. Many unqualified leaders create meaningless work, causing talent to leave. Concerns about micromanagement and overwork were dismissed, implying personal fault rather than company issues. Speaking up was met with "growth mindset" rather than solutions. Poor back-office systems (reliance on Excel/Google Sheets), inconsistent data quality across teams, making cross-functional work challenging. No strategic plan or long-term vision; constant firefighting. Underpaid employees and insufficient team resources.

Working 60+ hours a week for low salary, no time off (even on vacation), required 100% availability. High turnover (most quit within 6 months, except upper management). Employees hate management. CEO states "everyone is replaceable." Management sets unrealistic expectations, leading to incomplete tasks.

summary: A stimulating, technically challenging, and passion-driven community.
review_pros: Horizontal community where knowledge and experience are shared, with daily interesting conversations. Technical interview balances FAANG expectations with industry trends, emphasizing real-world application of algorithms and optimizations. Strong emphasis on LEAN and Agile methodologies.
review_cons: Mandatory use of Hubstaff, which should be optional.

review_pros: Verbling used to be a favorite teaching platform with fair fees and good teaching tools.
review_cons: Verbling no longer feels safe due to a purge of teachers who didn't comply with new, invasive third-party background checks. Data processing and sharing details were not provided. Accounts were deactivated for non-compliance or technical issues with the check website, causing loss of income. Teacher forum posts asking questions were deleted. Students were not refunded, only given credit, and classes were canceled without explanation, leading to significant financial losses for those who purchased packages. This practice is ethically questionable and unnecessary.

summary: Don't plan on making this a long-term career.
review_pros: Good benefits, interesting product.
review_cons: Cutthroat environment. Fast and unexpected layoffs. Management does not support employees.

summary: Great potential, awful management, average benefits.
review_pros: Fascinating products, industry, and tools, offering learning opportunities. Enjoyable and hard-working team in Ljubljana, Slovenia. Company has great potential with new leadership or involved investors.
review_cons: Engineering team frustrated with a condescending CEO who expects 24/7 availability. Sales teams have high turnover due to a toxic environment. Marketing team acts as a PR team, lacking added value.

review_pros: Abroad travel, career progression available.
review_cons: Low pay, high staff turnover, no help from HR.

summary: Good environment to start an auditing career.
review_pros: Education reimbursement, flexible time, professional association membership dues covered.
review_cons: Unnecessary pressure from upper management, lower pay.

summary: Wanted it to work out, but it wasn't possible (TAMG employee experience).
review_pros: Great name for resume, interesting fringe benefits (e.g., travel stipend), hired nice people.
review_cons: Extremely risk-averse (especially Cruise Critic). "Speed wins" mantra leads to shipping sloppy products and accumulating tech debt. Revenue-only focus, neglecting long-term sustainability. Poor business choices (e.g., Tripadvisor Plus) derail the organization. Untrained leaders. Poor compensation. Abysmal financial support for resources and tooling. Diversity goals are a smokescreen. Constant focus on wrong priorities. Lack of culture or belonging, even within ERGs; a "clock in, clock out" company.

review_pros: Nice, new store with good consumer offerings. Fair salary.
review_cons: Horrendous staffing, working alone daily from 3 PM - 11 PM without breaks or lunch. No on-site bathroom, requiring locking the store to use facilities elsewhere, often impossible due to customer volume. Manager refused to provide support or hire additional staff for night shifts. Unsafe environment due to sketchy and homeless individuals, leading to multiple confrontations.

review_pros: Nice colleagues outside of work.
review_cons: Staff are not appreciated. HR and IT failures hinder company progress.

summary: Great place to start out of college, but the company does little to retain good employees.
review_pros: Great learning environment and free CEUs.
review_cons: Low pay with no advancement opportunities.

summary: Amazing small company filled with great people.
review_pros: Great opportunities and amazing people.

advice_to_management: Great managements
review_pros: Good place to work and great perks.
review_cons: Few hours during certain periods and limited promotion opportunities.

advice_to_management: Be nicer and stop showing hate.
review_cons: Frequent picking on new employees.

review_pros: Great company to work for, delivering on its promises. Despite compensation changes, consistency in calls and helping families/seniors was rewarded. The company is evolving and has increased support for family advisors. During the pandemic, it supported employees and their families. Suitable for skilled individuals; those lacking skills may post negative reviews.
review_cons: Frequent changes in compensation plans were difficult for some, though these changes also brought more support.

review_pros: Flexible for those with multiple jobs or family obligations. Great benefits for full-time employees. Enjoyable occasional employee events. Promotions offered for long-term, compliant employees. Most server/food & beverage employees are great peers.
review_cons: Unreliable hours, often cut short or canceled last minute, leading to inconsistent pay. Gratuity tips are distributed quarterly as a "bonus," with unclear accounting, leaving employees struggling to cover costs and often needing multiple jobs. No background checks, leading to hiring individuals with serious issues (addicts, abusers, threats), creating an unsafe environment. Management gaslights and dismisses employee concerns about safety until a favored individual vocalizes the problem. Hypocritical enforcement of "zero tolerance" rules. High turnover due to irresponsible and insensitive behavior. Quality employees are driven away, and remaining staff are undervalued unless confronted. Inconsistent application of rules, especially between genders (male managers). Concerns about uncomfortable situations are met with political deflection. Diversity awareness and advocacy are performative.

summary: Great school to learn how to deal with work environment toxicity.
review_pros: Competitive salary for entry-level developers. Well-located Porto office. Great colleagues. HR efforts to ensure employee well-being. Company car for team leads and principal engineers.
review_cons: Toxic culture characterized by blame, backstabbing, and unfair year-end performance reviews. No clear career path. Terrible codebases in most projects. Terrible management from bottom to top.

summary: Great culture, great colleagues, great product.
review_pros: Strong mission-driven culture. Colleagues are almost universally nice, smart, and hard-working.
review_cons: As a consultant, being at the mercy of the client's pace/vision can be frustrating.

review_pros: Nice and genuinely caring people.
review_cons: Lack of focus. Executives are unaware of other groups' activities, inexcusable for a small organization. No game plans among functional units. Employees working with students (program managers, instructors, IAs) are often deemed unimportant. The Pursuit Bond model is similar to typical income-share agreements at other schools, which offer better-established curricula and larger support systems.

summary: Dysfunctional family, lack of direction, high churn.
review_pros: Good pay, nice colleagues, and a fine office.
review_cons: Working there is like being part of a dysfunctional family with a narcissistic stepfather who travels often, leaving his son to rule the house. Everyone is scared of the father, performing tasks to keep him happy even if it means making incorrect decisions.

summary: Burn your resume before applying here.
review_pros: If you like clueless, money-hungry management that screws you over, this is the place for you.
review_cons: They will drop you in whatever country you are in, and good luck getting home. It doesn't matter if there is a pandemic or not; you might get a plane ticket, maybe.

review_pros: Interesting projects are ongoing, and they are at least trying to innovate.
review_cons: Management is bad. Program management and product owners lack the skills for project leadership. There is a lot of internal rivalry, even among client officials. Most decisions are political, not technical. Everything is rushed, impacting delivery quality. There is a high internal turnover, most staff are technically weak, and good employees don't stay long.

summary: Stay away! The worst marketing agency to work for.
review_pros: Social events a couple of times a year.
review_cons: Toxic working environment. PM management team are bullies. Top-heavy with directors and management who lack expertise and professionalism. Operations directors swear a lot. Dictatorship working culture. Low salary. Narcissists at the top. Lack of transparency. Inconsistent rulebook. Be prepared to work 7 days a week. The place is literally sick.

summary: 360i was great, DAN not so much.
review_pros: Unlimited PTO, Summer Fridays, one week of holiday break, generous holiday calendar, several office events, insurance, TicketsAtWork, 401K with match, pre-tax MetroCard, adoption and fertility assistance, fun clients, free alcohol, and a rooftop. Coworkers are very cool, and there are several notable clients. People were very open to discussing career plans and allowing me to sit in on meetings to explore my interests. Office events were fun and not too forced. Leadership was very respectful in my experience. There was room for upward growth, but not as much flexibility with switching departments.
review_cons: Halfway through my time there, our team was switched to Dentsu instead of 360i, which stripped us of unlimited PTO and Summer Fridays. It felt like we always had different rules because of this. As part of the Office Hospitality team, I often felt like an outsider. We were mostly treated with respect but never truly felt like part of the team. Due to COVID, I was furloughed for 8 months and then eventually let go. I tried applying for entry-level positions and apprenticeships to stay with the company and grow my career. They pretended to want to help but never did, despite my passion for the company, its clients, and culture. I wish it hadn't ended on a sour note, but I still view the company positively.

summary: Good place to lose your qualification and get fat.
review_pros: Nice work-life balance, gentle people, good working conditions, and food. You can watch YouTube for half the working time and still be okay with your schedule.
review_cons: Terrible code quality, written unprofessionally. Massive recruiting led to many occasional people starting to code, resulting in hidden unemployment. Bureaucracy, lazy management, excessive meetings, and a fear of taking responsibility. Inventing artificial promotions and making 'Hulalalala' at every weekly meeting is ridiculous.

review_pros: They really take their values seriously. Transparency is key at Glassdoor and is felt in every decision they've made. I never felt left in the dark or like resources were being shielded from me.
review_cons: Diversity could have been much better.

review_pros: Great company, great people, work-hard-play-hard environment. You get out what you put in.
review_cons: High turnover, can be a grind.

review_pros: You learn new things about nationwide contracts and get to speak with many different people daily.
review_cons: Management is indecisive, changing the business model very frequently before even adjusting to the old one.

summary: Good for learning if they like you, terrible for being happy and for expecting some balance
advice_to_management:
Covid-19 has been handled terribly by forcing people to return to office.
I'm thankful for finding a new job where employers are more understanding.
review_pros: Smart folks to work with
review_cons:
Passive aggressive environment, tone from the top is not conducive to healthy working environment for folks who wants some balance in their personal lives.

Communication structure is horrible. Reminds me of how companies used to operate in the 80s / 90s with authoritarian culture with little regard for personal sensitivities

summary: Great place to work, just not in Texas!
review_pros: Great, friendly family company in Massachusetts. Not so much in Texas.
review_cons: Lack of knowledge and management skills in Texas. Massachusetts runs and operates flawlessly. 5 stars if you're in Massachusetts, 1 star if you're in Texas.

review_pros: You don't have to teach kids.
review_cons: Difficult to improve rate per hour because of a very strict KPI system.

review_pros: You know how much you are making.
review_cons: You need to know Japanese, or you can't communicate with other teachers.

review_pros: I got paid a decent salary. My coworkers were great people. I was lucky to be on the most profitable team, so I wasn't as micromanaged as other teams.
review_cons: The commission structure is horrible. There is no work-life balance; we only developed balance because of COVID. Management lies a lot, telling teams anything to cover their mistakes. Don't think they are on your team; you are just a number. Management is very shady in how they navigate staff, working people to the bone with very few perks. Not many holidays off; you earn half a day off every month, so if you're sick, you go unpaid or have a negative PTO balance. They beg you not to leave negative reviews on the company; many of these are fake. JumpCrew is a boys' club. They care so little about diversity, with very few women or people of color in management and throughout the company. A coworker mentioned the discrepancy to management multiple times, and there were always promises of change.

summary: Use it as an entrance into the tech industry.
review_pros: The CEO had great energy, vision, and practiced what he preached (he's gone now). The product is great; the more you read about FinTech, the more you realize its international impact. Good office location.
review_cons: The CFO (now replaced) and COO silo employees. Incestuous culture with rumors and high-school-like drama. Management became suspicious when people were having fun. In interviews, they claimed to be open to training and support, but there was none. Employees became afraid to ask questions as those who helped were reprimanded. The company is cheap.
Everything sucked, and they're cheap.

summary: Low pay, no perks like other call centers, hard to get terminated.
review_pros: Plenty of PTO, hard to get terminated, entry-level.
review_cons: Low pay, promotions based on cliques, and rules don't apply to everyone.

summary: A lot of work for little pay.
review_pros: Work from home after 6-10 months.
review_cons: Low pay. Massive amounts of work. Management wants you to take on responsibilities of other coworkers when they don't do their jobs right. After-call work (ACW) is 3 minutes, which is not enough time to finish work correctly.

summary: Some managers don't think they should wear PPE on site.
review_pros: Was really good at first.
review_cons: Allowed a bullying culture to flourish.

summary: Overworked employees, bad attitudes, lacking forward thinking, and outdated technology.
review_pros: Unique office environment and location.
review_cons: No work-life balance; always rushing to complete projects on unrealistic timelines, very reactive, not proactive. Outdated technology, product development systems, and communication tools, leading to too many emails and duplicative work. The CEO is involved in everything; only leadership's opinion matters. Attitudes are very poor, especially from leadership; very negative and not solution-oriented. Leaders will argue with each other in group meetings. Unpleasant culture, not forward-thinking, strategic, or looking for ways to improve. Sourcing and overseas vendors do not support customer needs and on-trend development, leading to presenting old or underdeveloped ideas to customers, resulting in reactionary steps. Domestic QA is a joke; they complain, are not helpful in finding solutions, or proactive planning. The corporate office is unique but outdated; they should invest in the right organizational tools, storage, workrooms, offices, gathering spaces, and technology to support a team that must go into the office.

review_pros: Learning about engineering and installing parts.
review_cons: Trying to complete additional tasks.

review_pros: Good work-life balance. Opportunities to work with different technologies. No heavy workload. Various activities are organized regularly, in which you can participate.

summary: Great experience if you're willing to work hard.
review_pros: Great experience for students, great staff, fair salary, and company-wide cookouts every week.
review_cons: Long hours, hard manual labor, and frequent superfluous meetings.

summary: Executive Management Doesn't Know the Business.
advice_to_management: Stop giving away the product.
review_pros: Remote work, unlimited vacation/PTO days.
review_cons: Mandatory salary reductions due to financial instability (twice in two years). Management changes, business goals unknown to most of the team, lack of strategy and direction. No new sites added.

review_pros: Pay was good, benefits were decent. Personnel were very helpful and honest.
review_cons: Minimal support from the regional manager. Focused on growing the company faster than the infrastructure could sustain.

review_pros: Career development, great manager and team.
review_cons: Old internal technology platforms. Very bureaucratic.

review_pros: Unbelievably amazing colleagues who became the only reason I came to work. We found collective unity by experiencing similar dysfunction within the company. Hiring managers are great manipulators, thus having a great sense for 'styles' of new hires; we all got along. Paid training and licensing. The kitchen is stocked, and lunch is provided twice a week. Competitive pay (if you negotiate for it).
review_cons: The leaders of this company are professional salespeople. They treat employees the same way they are scripted to treat borrowers, managing from a script. It's all 'smoke and mirrors'; everything is phrased and scripted to seem great, but it's all lies, a 'fine print management.' There are inner-circle politics and complete dysfunction in their leadership tactics, stemming from how the CEO handles his employees. They respond to questions with questions. The CEO has a complete disregard for the integrity of his employees and will publicly demonstrate it in front of the entire company. As a startup, there is no organizational dynamic or clear path to success; it's constantly being made up as they go. There is no structure; promotions are based on 'feel' and 'personality,' not work performance. This job highly affects your lifestyle and outlook. As a former colleague advised, 'Protect yourself at all times... be calculated in your responses... these people are liars and will milk every emotion from you until you break.'

summary: Great company in the public accounting industry.
review_pros: Great culture that focuses on employee satisfaction. The people are truly great, and compensation is slightly above average for upstate jobs.
review_cons: Public accounting industry hours can be tough. High-performing employees could be compensated better to help retain talent.

review_pros: Easy job, good commission as an associate. Some stores have good people to work with. Easy to get hired at most places.
review_cons: The District Manager was inconsistent, and store managers changed every few months. DM's mistakes are blamed on you. Turnover rate is very high; expect to be called in on your days off. They will treat you as lesser if you can't work 6-7 days a week. Computer systems are very old, and most logs are still on paper. They will not train you properly at most stores. If you're a keyholder, you need experience because they won't teach you how to open or close; they'll give you training test answers and promise to teach you later, but never do. If you have an issue with management, they won't give you a chance to respond. If a customer complains, they will hold it against you and never speak to you directly, or only inform you when convenient. They only schedule two people per shift, even during training, and store managers get reprimanded for overtime. One person manages many stores and doesn't pay attention or listen to anyone other than the store manager, unless convenient. When a former store manager left due to declining mental health and stress, the DM tried to manipulate them into returning. Pay for every position is abysmal. Associates make minimum wage but often earn more than keyholders (who handle shipment/cleaning because associates aren't properly trained) due to 7% commission. The store manager position usually starts at less than $16 an hour. The store is always a mess, and the DM complains but doesn't provide time or staff to clean it. Management does not care about employees. Shoes are always on the floor, leading to theft and damage. People leave trash and empty boxes everywhere. The dress code is outdated; you have to buy your own shirt if they don't have any, or pay for them through an order form that is often not fulfilled. The dress code is only enforced by the DM and not properly listed on paperwork. The store manual is outdated, and the DM told us not to follow it.

advice_to_management: Listen to employees more often and truly engage with them. Don't act like you are in high school; lose the hierarchy game, it doesn't help.
review_pros: Diverse, fun, engaging, career-focused, and good benefits.
review_cons: Managers change too often; some are great, and some are horrible.

review_pros: The ability to work independently.
review_cons: Work is not structured but rather thrown to you when someone else doesn't want to do it. I also believe that some select supervisors are racist.

review_pros: Some good perks and benefits programs.
review_cons: Extremely high turnover; inconsistent and outdated processes.

review_pros: The only positive thing is listening to all the gossip.
review_cons: If you get COVID-19, your two weeks out of work will be unpaid. There's currently a COVID-19 positive unit, and you won't get paid if you become COVID-19 positive. Not everyone is getting hazard pay. Nurses, CNAs, RTs, dietary staff, etc., are constantly in contact with staff going into COVID-19 positive units, so why isn't everyone getting hazard pay? It was soul-crushing to see 'Heroes Work Here' signs when administration isn't paying us what we're worth. That's why I left. Plenty of other facilities honor hazard pay. Don't even apply here.

review_pros: In my six months there, the best day I had was serving hot dogs to students as a morale booster. Everything else was awful.
review_cons: I've been working almost constantly since I was 16, about 20 years. I've had a few jobs I wouldn't return to, but most were good. The one job I wouldn't return to for any amount of money is Career Services at Remington. I've never woken up sick to my stomach for any other job. The president would inspect my attire every morning, always professional and appropriate, but I and my team were always scrutinized. Phones are bugged, and meetings are listened in on. Reviews with the regional president are nightmarish. He tells you how to prepare, then does the opposite. He sat in a dark room and degraded my team, my progress, and everything I did, while I was told to look ahead and not turn around to speak to him. I was told what colors to wear on review days and not to wear lotion, perfume, or too much jewelry because it 'offended him.' While being spoken to like an idiot, I was told to just sit and listen and never talk back. The work scope is impossible: if 100 kids graduate, you're expected to place 100 in jobs. If you place 99, they ask why not 100. Employers wouldn't hire Remington students because their education isn't good enough. When cold-calling to place students, employers would sometimes say, 'we don't hire Remington students,' or just hang up. If the regional career services director feels pressured enough (which is always), they'll tell you to fudge the numbers. Remington is an awful diploma mill, and every location should be forced to shut its doors. Avoid it; you won't last long anyway, as they don't keep anyone. When they let you go, they'll make you sign a document stating nothing unethical is happening at Remington and that you never witnessed anything unethical. Trust me, you will, every day.

review_pros: Salary, cab.
review_cons: They treat their own US employees as clients. No culture. Chennai Management ensures no direct contact with higher management in the US. HR claims there is a Glassdoor page for Transact. HR provided wrong information about shift timings for 15 people at a time. Unwanted cheap behaviors from management to hide their mistakes. Poor pantry. I left the company in 15+ days.

summary: Abandon hope, ye who enter here.
review_pros: At this point, few to none. A port in a storm if you can't find anything else. Free parking, soft drinks, coffee, and fruit.
review_cons: Long hours, no rewards for rowing a galley with no rudder or compass. Lack of product vision, direction, and fewer competent resources to keep the boat afloat, much less moving forward. Customer churn is significant. Stability is more of a fantasy than a dream.

company_name: International Committee of the Red Cross
review_pros: Works to support civilians and humanitarian law in the world's most fascinating and intractable conflicts. Very committed staff. Interesting work. Offers unbeatable life experiences.
review_cons: Approach to staff is partly military-style with strict and inflexible ruling, which may make it less suitable for non-medical outsiders with non-ICRC experience. HR rules and treatment of staff can sometimes seem shambolic, arcane, and frankly cruel.

summary: A good company to grow and learn.
review_pros: Good hike, friendly culture, and a lot of opportunity to learn and grow.
review_cons: Sometimes there is work pressure, which is true for each project.

summary: Excellent work environment and wonderful management personnel.
review_pros: The great working environment and wonderful coworkers. The caring, friendly, approachable, and superior leadership qualities of the management personnel.
review_cons: Unfortunately, the salary for CRCs is very low, especially considering the workload, advanced knowledge, and skills necessary to perform the job functions.

review_pros: Free samples, discounted services, and retail products.
review_cons: Catty environment, zero support from management, zero care for employees, no benefits, not worth your sanity.

summary: Great group of people to work with.
review_pros: The people you work with are great, and they try to do things to increase employee morale, such as cookouts and random lunch parties.
review_cons: The amount of benefits decreased. Management does not communicate effectively.
Management's only solution is to 'make more calls.'

summary: The absolute most toxic work environment I've ever experienced.
review_pros: None whatsoever. I met one nice writer there.
review_cons: Incredibly disorganized and bizarrely run from the top down. Read all the reviews if you are considering an offer from this 'company.' Humiliation, misogyny, and homophobia were daily reminders of the hellish atmosphere of this 'pay-for-play' scam sold with cooked numbers. I literally dealt with PTSD for almost a year after my short 6-month stint in this evil place.
The company knows its weaknesses and refuses to address them.

review_pros: Good benefits, but not enough to make up for poor compensation.
review_cons: Do not expect to advance at IHS Markit. You better be happy with your starting position. Low pay.

review_pros: Bonuses, increases in pay, and some room for advancement.
review_cons: Management doesn't know what they're doing, quick to write someone up, no support, long shifts, always being called in, believes clients over staff, no proper training, gossip, slander, catty work environment, quick to throw someone under the bus, staff sleeping with clients, and the list goes on.

review_pros: Good internal interpersonal team dynamics, excellent one-on-one patient care, and a very clean and technologically up-to-date service space.
review_cons: Poor hiring/recruiting process, poor top-down management control, and local branches often feel disconnected from the bigger entity.

summary: This company will ruin your life, health, and peace, not just your career.
review_cons: The top managers are swindlers, led by the most unethical, callous human you will ever come across. They will make your life a living hell and do as they please. From fooling customers to employees to partners, it is all built on a hollow, deceitful foundation. Any current or ex-employee will give you the same feedback. There is zero respect and value for employees. The CEO will yell all day, and other leaders will wag their tails, only to talk crap behind his back. Your full and final settlement and documents will never be fulfilled. Someday this company will collapse because it has crippled many hopeful careers and innocent souls.

Leadership is either too timid and shy to get things done, or they yell profanities in company-wide meetings, trying to guilt-trip employees to do better because they lack ownership themselves. They will dictate your dress code, how long you work, and give you no ability to work from home. They are a company that focuses on numbers and does not care about its employees.

review_pros: Strong team environment and excellent technology to work with.
review_cons: Lack of backup in the admin department. Vacations often result in much work needing to be completed upon return.

review_pros: Flexible work hours and good benefits.
review_cons: Hiring for unnecessary positions. Not following through on internal promotions, instead hiring from outside without clear communication. Deceptive practices regarding job security. Laying off employees without getting to know them or giving them an opportunity to prove their worth.

review_pros: Working with a life-saving technology that detects and assesses concussions (TBI).
review_cons: Frequent strategic shifts, long hours, and company is running out of money.

advice_to_management: Please actually set up an in-office.
review_pros: The higher-ups were great, as were all the benefits.
review_cons: Limited resources, no actual office space. Had to drive personal vehicle.

review_pros: Amazing job, loved everyone there.
review_cons: Sometimes felt like I wished they hired more minorities.

review_pros: Some good people worked there.
review_cons: Awful management, awful pay, awful culture. No pay increases to base pay for salespeople in 20 years. ESOP is a joke now and less than it was 10 years ago. Stay away.

summary: Useless CEO firing people within a few months after joining.
review_cons: No job security. Terminated within a few months for trivial reasons; CEO gave vague verbal requirements and then shouted for wasted time. No termination pay. CEO is rude and arrogant. Salary was never on time, requiring monthly reminders. CEO assigns random R&D work, not allowing focus on their own product. Lack of processes, structure, and crappy, arrogant behavior towards employees. CEO stated he would hire cheap freelancers, indicating a freelance mindset. They should not recruit full-time if they treat employees this way.

review_pros: You will learn a lot because the pressure of work is very high.
review_cons: High workload, never pays employees on time (sometimes current month's salary is paid after two months, and only half), CEO is rude and unprofessional.

review_pros: Work-from-home is available.
review_cons: No job security; you don't know when you will lose your job.

review_pros: Good pay, health insurance, and retirement plan.
review_cons: Management was stuck in a 1990s time warp. Employees must sacrifice family time due to the constant push to service clients.

review_pros: Smart people with a strong fundamental approach to private equity investing; good pay.
review_cons: Very long hours and little focus on fixing it from senior management.

review_pros: Good pay when actually paid.
review_cons: Long hours for low salary, long drive time with no reimbursement for gas or vehicle maintenance, poor installation and permit approval times, and employees are forced to do things that are morally/ethically questionable.

advice_to_management: Get new management.
review_cons: No space, broken machines, unsafe environment. Management doesn't care about employees. Long hours. Ministry called in on multiple occasions. Understaffed, workload is too much, not enough machinery. Concerns are never dealt with.

summary: Toxic and dysfunctional administration destroying school with great potential.
review_pros: Great opportunity to work one-on-one with students from diverse backgrounds, learning abilities/styles, and personalities, with distinct educational/socio-emotional needs. The one-on-one model allows for quick assessment of effective/ineffective strategies, unlike large classroom settings. As experience grows, knowledge and comfort in subject areas expand, allowing for the integration of resources/tools/learning platforms. Good opportunity to teach various classes, including AP courses. Many highly talented, hardworking, collaborative, supportive, and collegial teachers. Opportunities to advise student groups/clubs during lunch.
review_cons: High turnover among teachers and front desk staff, with little investment in retention; upper administration describes it as a 'stepping stone' job. This creates a lack of continuity for students and loss of experienced staff. Inadequate teacher training for students with learning differences (20 minutes weekly, often topical, focused on reporting requirements). Inadequate prep time: 1 hour for every 10 hours of instruction, paid at a reduced rate ($20/hour, well below teacher pay range). Class cancellations count against accrued prep time. No dedicated Human Resources; disciplinary issues handled by upper administration, creating a conflict of interest. An outside HR consultant is used at upper administration's discretion. History of unprofessional, bullying, and retaliatory behavior by upper administrative team members, including those in charge of curriculum and HR/benefits, with impunity. Complaints have been ignored, enabled, excused, and covered up by upper management. Top leadership positions often awarded through nepotism or to those who do not question management, fostering pettiness, immaturity, vindictiveness, insecurity, gossip, and insincerity. This creates an unproductive, toxic, incompetent, and uncaring environment. Lack of teacher control/feedback/collaboration in curriculum development. No CFO or Controller. School is consistently reactive instead of proactive logistically, administratively, and curriculum-wise.

summary: Horrible one-year experience. Toxic work environment allowing workplace harassment from team members.
review_pros: Beautiful office in Germantown, Wisconsin.
review_cons: Extremely toxic and difficult environment. Understaffed departments with high turnover. HR was notified numerous times about workplace bullying, but nothing was done. Stressful work environment. Worst year of my career.

review_pros: Progressive industry, learned a lot.
review_cons: Overworked, rampant burnout, very unresponsive/disorganized management.

summary: Good starting point, but look for something better afterward.
review_pros: Friendly team environment. Supervisor and department were great, patient with work, and willing to teach during team meetings.
review_cons: Overworked and very low pay, making it hard to increase salary once started. Projects are organized until higher-ups reassign people to other projects.

review_pros: Great work culture, good benefits.
review_cons: Senior management has no clue what they are doing with the product.

summary: Good benefits, but that's about it.
review_pros: Good benefits, some good people to work with.
review_cons: Dress code, overly secretive, outdated job policies, very political in terms of job promotion, very restrictive trading of personal portfolio even if not part of research staff, rigid hours.

review_pros: Annual leave.
review_cons: Assigned jobs are totally out of scope because team members are not capable of completing committed tasks.

review_pros: Opportunity to work with large-scale companies.
review_cons: Too much emphasis on outsourcing, which doesn't work; IT is a mess. Managers micromanage to the point of unnecessary rework. Under-resourced teams lead to exhaustion, with little concern for mental health (one day off for mental health awareness doesn't compensate for a year of strain). Promotions are non-existent. Senior leadership doesn't understand appropriate hierarchy levels. The rotation scheme advertised on job applications doesn't exist.

review_pros: HR team is good at responding and maintaining effective communication. Recruitment/onboarding team is above par in onboarding candidates with necessary instructions. Immigration team was knowledgeable and helped with H1B approval for a transfer petition despite an RFE. Employees receive good benefits, including health insurance (dental, vision options), a 401K plan, and 13 days of PTO accrued per pay cycle.
review_cons: Lacks a well-defined Marketing Team to quickly find new jobs for candidates when projects end.

review_pros: Directors and CEO threaten to sue sales agents or managers who express opposing views. Agents are terminated on trumped-up charges, which is presented as entertainment. Employees learn how to amortize a mortgage, their main sales pitch, and how a 1950s HP 12C calculator works.
review_cons: Boot-licking and backstabbing are common. Bosses teach employees to threaten agents with lawsuits for leaving. Management schemes to wreck finances and careers, terminating agents on lame charges.

review_pros: Virtual environment/work-from-home, benefits, culture, vacation policy, bonuses based on company profitability.
review_cons: Frequent re-organizations, bad leadership, lack of growth path/opportunities.

review_pros: Fully remote, good benefits, no micromanagement.
review_cons: Organizational chart made it difficult to work with others outside of the department. Revolving door with upper management.

Company views people in recovery as dollar signs and does nothing to give back to the SUD community. Multiple layoffs prove leadership doesn't care about employees. Long-term employees are laid off, bullied out, or leave due to frustration from not being listened to.

review_pros: The people you work with.
review_cons: Low pay, overworked, no support.

review_pros: Working from home and quarterly bonuses.
review_cons: Management only communicated poor performance during reviews, offering no chance to improve. Manager always blamed me for lack of improvement and never took accountability. No real maternity leave, only short-term or long-term disability.

review_pros: Employee discount, nice people, cool clothes.
review_cons: Overworked, doing the work of multiple people and not compensated.

summary: A nascent service with potential and solid foundations for continued success.
review_pros: Kind, empathetic, and focused colleagues who execute tasks with maximum effort and care. Solid and repeatable commercial model that incentivizes good behavior and prioritizes service efficacy. Good focus on employee well-being. Remarkable successes, a testament to the model, technology, and team. A lot of trust from management enables great work.
review_cons: Can feel like 'move fast and break things' at times. Feedback is generally acted on, but sometimes not visibly or quickly, leading to a loss of trust. High volume and pace of work force the team to be reactive. Hiring has been a struggle.

Fools students by promising large packages (e.g., 9 LPA) but pays around 20k per month, with performance-based incentives that are often not paid even if targets are achieved. A major scam is that salary payment starts the next month after joining, but employees are terminated before the first payday without any compensation, even after working for a month, if sales targets are not met. No time is given to adjust to the company environment. Colleges should not consider this company for student placements as it ruins students' lives.

review_pros: Great product line and good service.
review_cons: Very little growth opportunity. Not enough employees for the workload.

summary: Know your direct team (school) before deciding to work here.
review_pros: Typical sports industry perks: games, tickets, gear. Time off around the holidays.
review_cons: Low pay for entry-level positions ($30k/year) despite executives spending excessively on personal dinners, alcohol, and travel (including private jets for meetings that could be virtual). Commissions are confusing and depend on the inherited book size; small book holders bring in significant revenue but see only a fraction. Technical benefits are weak, with no 401k match. Corporate claims to be a 'family' but lays off many people, only to repost their jobs weeks later.

review_pros: The office in New York is nice.
review_cons: Horrible, reactionary management with zero vision. New initiatives every month without team communication, leading to duplicated efforts. Four re-organizations in six months. Management denies layoffs, then conducts them. C-level leadership stated, 'This is how this company works, if you don’t like it… leave,' and 'There’s no job security in life, so we aren’t going to try and rebuild the trust we lost by lying and laying folks off.' Constant breakage due to lack of quality focus (CEO focuses on minor UI details). Horribly outdated tech with no improvement. Directors and above don't care about engineers or managers. Blatant favoritism is the only way to advance. CTO aims for people to quit to make roles 'hard enough.' Poor company outlook despite flashy new deals. Zero learning and development, horrible culture. Everyone is overworked. Culture involves acting like frat boys and getting drunk after work. Avoid this company if you value yourself, your values, or work-life balance.

review_pros: Market leader and international company.
review_cons: Big hierarchy, limited promotion possibilities. They prefer hiring new people over promoting loyal employees.

review_pros: Flexible working, casual dress code, comfy sofas and breakout areas.
review_cons: Management has no idea what they're doing and plays favorites. Pay is very low and frozen to fund company acquisitions. Management is predominantly male. SEO performance is declining, making it a hard time for the business.

review_pros: If you are talented and want to grow, you must fight for yourself boldly; otherwise, you will be ignored or cornered.
review_cons: Poor work-life balance, limited learning opportunities.

review_pros: Product discounts on all items, paid holiday every year.
review_cons: Poor management and abusive environment. Primary goal was to avoid giving anything away when a product is returned. Excessive corporate red tape.

summary: Not a good MNC by the end of 2022.
review_pros: Good stepping stone for juniors and foreigners seeking a starting career in Singapore, as the company prefers hiring low-wage workers (though this may change with rising minimum wages). Employees typically work 1-2 years before switching companies. Diversity exists. Annual company stock discount for all full-time employees. Online learning coverage for Udemy and Coursera. Hybrid working culture for non-critical businesses; government and manufacturing are on-site. Generally good work-life balance, except near deadlines. IT technology (VPN, automatic software updates) is good.
review_cons: Salary is below market average despite demanding job roles. Diversity (mostly Indonesian, Filipino, Indian, Vietnamese) is likely due to low wages. Promotion is based on seniority and pleasing management/French people, not merit, leading to brilliant people leaving and average/bad ones filling roles. High turnover rate. High-ego managers and toxic colleagues; sabotage and backstabbing are prevalent. Chaotic codebase for development, production, and unit tests. Project leaders and managers do not strive for good development practices; code refactoring is seen as a burden, coding shortcuts as brilliant, and agile development is superficial. Many French colleagues are cunning, self-entitled, and arrogant towards Asians; some incompetent expats have the highest salaries. Strong politics in higher management, especially involving French people. Few brilliant people to work with; experts' expertise is questionable, and strong cryptography knowledge is lacking in a cybersecurity company. No one cares about good software development practice. Limited career progression for higher positions. Average health insurance coverage (no dental or annual health screening). Bad HR technology (Workday for performance/structure, Oracle for other functions), making benefits claims, payroll downloads, leave applications, and monthly check-ins difficult. Anonymous surveys are not truly anonymous.

review_pros: Opportunity for growth, versatile work location.
review_cons: Expensive benefits. Pay is low-balled if not negotiated. Top-down micromanagement. Hyper-inflated egos of certain leaders. Inconsiderate, partial, and unfair boss. Laughable representatives and practices. No sense of team. Inconsistent management attention and treatment for associates. Non-involved executives. Supervisors ignore employees who give notice, then harass them after they leave. Upper management is routinely dishonest with clients and prevents exposure of this.

review_pros: Food at subsidized rates, office shuttle, good work-life balance. Company supports employees during uncertain times (as long as they have not resigned).
review_cons: Some departments operate like Indian government offices, prioritizing talking over impactful work. Some departments have 'uncles' who teach morals instead of working. Legacy systems and code. Employees lose trust from their employer during the notice period; no Diwali gift is given, reflecting a low-minded HR policy. No work-from-home; people are forced to come to the office.

summary: Once we were bought out, many were slowly weeded out.
review_pros: Good salary; if lucky, a good immediate supervisor.
review_cons: Not the same since being bought out by UnitedHealthcare. Went from being a valued person to just another employee number on a spreadsheet.

review_pros: Some co-workers are nice, good products.
review_cons: Bad management, terrible response to COVID, no raises, toxic work environment.

summary: Great people to work with, but horrible management.
review_pros: Great people, amazing office space.
review_cons: Underpaid, lack of promotions, extreme favoritism, toxic management that portrays itself as the victim.

review_pros: Great people to work with, great bonuses.
review_cons: Numbers-driven, leading to a lack of care for employees. Overtime was implemented as a solution for many tickets, resulting in overworked employees.

review_pros: Weekends off, provided lunch.
review_cons: Management is out of touch; long-term employees (20+ years) are protective of their roles, and progressive mindsets don't prevail. Employees with 4+ years of service were laid off to hire temps.

review_pros: Refreshments in the kitchen are nice.
review_cons: Toxic culture. Senior management is unsupportive; new employees are more friendly and supportive.

advice_to_management: Be nice to the associates.
review_pros:
generous
bonuses
learnings
career growth
review_cons: Too much overtime, working until 12 PM.
